Title: Data Analyst (f/m/d)
Description:
Data Analyst - Join Our Innovative Team at think-cell Software




Our Story

At think-cell, we create powerful software solutions that help professionals be more efficient and productive. Founded in 2002 and based in Berlin, our tools integrate seamlessly with Microsoft Office to help users create presentations, charts, and diagrams with ease. Our dynamic, international team values creativity, collaboration, and technical excellence.




What You Will Do

We're a Data Team that operates like a scaleup within an established company: real ownership, short decision paths, and room to shape things. No corporate bureaucracy – but the resources of a mature business. Our team is made up of experienced senior individual contributors who work independently, challenge each other constructively, and collaborate closely on solving complex problems. It is an environment with a high level of trust, autonomy, and strong opportunities to learn from experienced peers across both technical and business topics.

You don't just analyze – you drive. You proactively identify gaps, inefficiencies, and opportunities for improvement, build the foundations to answer questions, and make sure stakeholders don't just receive dashboards, but know what to do with them.




Key Responsibilities:

Building and maintaining scalable data models and pipelines
Translating business questions into structured analytical approaches and communicating them in whatever format stakeholders actually need
Collaborating with teams across the company to improve data quality, transparency and accessibility
Contributing to the development of our data culture, standards, and ways of working




You will be great for this position if you have

3+ years of experience in Analytics, BI, or a similar role
Strong SQL skills and hands-on experience with dbt
Experience with PowerBI
You guide stakeholders – not just serve them, both communication and domain wise
A proactive and ownership-driven mindset
Interest in improving processes and increasing efficiency, including through ML/LLM-based approaches




Our Values

As part of our team, you will embody and help shape the following principles that define us:

Forward Thinking: We embrace change and challenge the status quo.

Ownership: We take pride in our work and learn from our mistakes.

Customer First: We prioritize delivering lasting value.

Unified Team: We foster collaboration, respect, and integrity.

Strive for Excellence: We set ambitious goals and pursue quality.




Why is think-cell the right place for you?

We empower over 1.3 million users across 35,000 companies, including top consulting firms and major global enterprises. With offices in Berlin, Munich, Denver, Boston, London, Tokyo, and Dubai, we’re a diverse team of over 220 people, committed to innovation and excellence.

think-cell is more than a job – it's a chance to be part of an innovative, inclusive team that values collaboration and growth. We encourage applicants from all backgrounds to apply. Together, we can shape the future of productivity software. We can’t wait to see what you’ll bring to the team!

Title: Intern - Data Analyst
Description:
We are looking for an Intern - Data Analyst to support our commercial research and counterparty analysis for a major logistics partner. This role focuses on translating raw market information into the clear insights our partners need to advise on complex investment decisions. You will help us untangle data that shapes how large entities enter new markets and manage joint ventures. This is a full-time, fully remote position that allows you to contribute to high-stakes projects from any location.

What you will do
Collect and organize data from various sources to build a clear picture of market behavior within the territory.
Maintain and update Excel models used to test different commercial scenarios for our logistics partner.
Research specific counterparties and competitors to identify how they are behaving in the current environment.
Prepare clear charts and tables that help partners explain complex data points to senior decision-makers.
Audit internal reports for numerical accuracy to ensure every piece of evidence we present is verified.
Monitor trade flows and port activity data to identify shifts in the logistics sector.
Assist the team in preparing briefing notes for sovereign entities and chief executives ahead of negotiations.



What you bring
A background in economics, finance, or a related field where you have practiced analytical thinking.
Proficiency in Excel, including a comfort level with organizing datasets and using basic formulas.
A logical mindset and a genuine curiosity about how commercial deals are structured and settled.
Strong writing skills with a focus on being concise and factual rather than using descriptive filler.
A high level of discipline regarding detail and the ability to spot errors in complex documents.
The ability to work independently and manage your own tasks in a fully remote setting.
An interest in the logistics industry and the practical reality of how state and private capital interact.



What we offer
A paid internship with a competitive rate for the entry level market.
The flexibility of working in a remote environment where outcomes are valued over fixed hours.
Direct exposure to senior partners who are involved in the work from start to finish.
A chance to see how commercial judgment is applied to high-stakes investment decisions.



Working at Gulf Associates
Gulf Associates is a specialized firm that values ground reality over theoretical models. We do not produce reports meant to sit on a shelf; we provide the evidence and judgment necessary for a client to take a specific position. Our culture is direct and honest, reflecting the environments where we operate. We stay close to the logic of the market, often sitting in the room during the negotiations and regulator conversations where outcomes are actually decided. Because we maintain a small book of active engagements, every member of our team is focused on work that has a clear purpose. We prefer clear communication and evidence-based drafting, avoiding the usual corporate fluff in favor of what is true and actionable. In this role, you will see how commercial decisions are made when the stakes are at their highest.

Title: Data Support Analyst
Description:
Alpine Business Consulting is a specialized team dedicated to fixing enterprise performance management and reporting systems that have lost their way. Often, a company invests in complex planning tools only to find that the implementation cut corners, leaving finance teams to deal with numbers that do not match and month end closes that take far too long. We step in to configure these systems properly or build new ones from the start. Our consultants have spent years working with Oracle EPM, SAP BPC, and modern analytics stacks. They bring real world experience to every project rather than using a client engagement as a training ground for junior staff.
We are currently supporting a major logistics firm that needs help organizing its reporting structures and ensuring data integrity across several global divisions. As a Data Support Analyst, you will play a practical part in this project by helping our senior consultants maintain the accuracy of the data flowing into their new systems. This is a permanent, full time position that is fully remote, allowing you to work from your own professional setup while learning how large scale enterprise systems are actually built and maintained.

The role
At Alpine, we do not believe in mysterious black box solutions. We believe in transparency and predictability. The Data Support Analyst exists to help us provide a single source of truth that finance teams can trust every morning. You will not be expected to manage the whole project, but you will be responsible for the essential tasks that ensure our configurations work as intended. This involves a lot of data validation, checking one system against another, and helping to write the documentation that allows our clients to remain independent once we finish a project.
Because we often work with fixed price quotes, efficiency and accuracy are important. You will spend your time digging into spreadsheets and database exports to find why a number in a dashboard does not match the source. You will help clean data before it is loaded into new tools and assist in creating the configuration guides that explain how we set everything up. This is a foundational role where you will see exactly how messy corporate data gets organized into useful business intelligence.

What you will do
Review large datasets from the logistics firm to identify duplicates, missing values, or formatting errors.
Compare reports from legacy systems against our new Oracle or SAP configurations to verify accuracy.
Help maintain the data dictionary and single source of truth documentation for the client.
Assist senior consultants in drafting clear, plain spoken configuration guides and training manuals.
Update project tracking logs to ensure all data discrepancies are addressed before the final handover.
Format financial reports and dashboards to ensure they are clean and easy for finance teams to read.
Participate in remote team meetings to discuss data findings and project progress.
Support the collection of requirements from client teams by taking detailed notes and organizing documentation.



What we need from you
Basic familiarity with Microsoft Excel, including the ability to use lookups and pivot tables to compare data.
Strong attention to detail and a willingness to double check your work for small errors.
An interest in how businesses use data for financial planning and reporting.
Logical thinking skills to help figure out why data might look incorrect.
Clear writing skills to help produce documentation that a client can actually understand.
The ability to work independently in a fully remote setting and manage your daily tasks effectively.
A cooperative attitude and a willingness to learn from senior consultants with years of experience.
Reliable internet access and a professional approach to remote communication.



What the role offers
Working at Alpine Business Consulting means you will be learning the right way to handle enterprise data. We do not use corporate jargon or fluff: we focus on what works. You will get to see the inner workings of a major logistics firm and help them solve real problems. This is a paid position, and the compensation is competitive for an entry level candidate in the current market. Since the role is fully remote, you will have the flexibility to work from home while still being a core part of a high performing consulting team. We prioritize your growth by giving you access to senior experts who value good documentation and clean data over quick fixes.
We provide fixed price quotes wherever possible and prioritize documentation that a client team can actually use. Our goal is to hand over a system that works reliably and then step back, ensuring the company has the training and configuration guides needed to remain independent. Whether it is a stalled cloud migration or a dashboard that nobody opens, we focus on providing a results that matter for the client.

Title: Data Analyst (m/w/d) Data Analytics
Description:
Unser Angebot


Freuen Sie sich auf eine leistungsorientierte Vergütung, flexible Arbeitszeiten und die Möglichkeit, im Homeoffice mit aktuellen Technologien virtuell zusammenzuarbeiten.
Wir wünschen uns, dass Sie mit uns wachsen. Dabei unterstützen und begleiten wir Ihre fachliche und persönliche Entwicklung zielgerichtet und gestalten mit Ihnen zusammen Ihren persönlichen Karriereweg.
Es erwarten Sie ein kollegiales Betriebsklima, zahlreiche Annehmlichkeiten für den beruflichen Alltag sowie verschiedene Rahmenbedingungen, mit denen wir die Vereinbarkeit von Familie und Beruf unterstützen.


Ihre Aufgaben


Sie arbeiten flexibel in interdisziplinären Projektteams aus Kunden- und Syncwork-Mitarbeitenden, direkt bei unseren Kunden vor Ort, von unserem Office bzw. Homeoffice aus.
Sie managen und analysieren große Datenmengen und entwickeln intelligente Algorithmen.
Sie unterstützen bei der Analyse, dem Design und Prototyping sowie bei der Realisierung und dem Rollout innovativer Reporting- und Planungslösungen zur Unternehmenssteuerung mithilfe von Business Intelligence.
Sie konzipieren und realisieren innovative Lösungen im Bereich Industrie 4.0, Advanced Analytics, Machine Learning, Big Data oder Artificial Intelligence.
Sie analysieren Strategien, Organisations- und Kostenstrukturen sowie Prozesse.


Ihr Profil


Sie haben Ihr Studium der Wirtschaftswissenschaften (BWL, VWL), (Wirtschafts-)Informatik, des (Wirtschafts-)Ingenieurwesens, der Mathematik, ein vergleichbares Studium oder Ihre Ausbildung mit bankfachlichem Hintergrund erfolgreich abgeschlossen.
Sie punkten idealerweise mit praktischen Erfahrungen im Bereich Big Data, Advanced und Data Analytics und haben eine Leidenschaft für neue Technologien und digitale Trends.
Sie besitzen vorteilhafterweise Kenntnisse in Programmiersprachen (z. B. R, Python), Datenbanksystemen (z. B. SQL) oder Cloud-Technologien (MS Azure, IBM Bluemix).
Sie denken analytisch und gehen Aufgaben systematisch und strukturiert an.
Sie verfügen über gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse.
Sie wollen durch Ihren Einsatz zum Projekterfolg beitragen und verkörpern unsere durch Teamgeist, Flexibilität und Erfolgswillen geprägte Unternehmenskultur.
Kommunikationsstärke und Eigeninitiative runden Ihr Profil ab.

Title: Junior Data Quality Analyst
Description:
Gulf Associates is looking for a Junior Data Quality Analyst to support our commercial advisory work. We focus on providing direct, ground-level insights that help leaders make difficult investment and partnership decisions. Currently, we are providing decision-support to a leading German logistics firm that is expanding its presence in new territories. This expansion requires a high level of data accuracy regarding local counterparties, infrastructure, and competitors. In this role, you will be responsible for ensuring the information we use to build client positions is reliable and reflects the actual commercial reality on the ground. This is a fully remote position, allowing you to work from your own location while staying integrated with our senior partners.

What you will do
Review and verify datasets related to logistics networks, port operations, and transport infrastructure.
Audit information on potential counterparties to ensure we are working with accurate profiles and histories.
Standardize data entries across our internal research documents to maintain consistency for partner review.
Identify and flag gaps in commercial data that could impact the firm's judgment on a specific deal.
Work with our research team to reconcile conflicting reports from different market sources.
Support the preparation of data-backed positions for client meetings and negotiations.
Monitor updates regarding sovereign entities and regulatory changes that affect the logistics sector.
Maintain the integrity of our internal databases by performing regular quality checks and corrections.



What you bring
A logical mindset with an instinct for spotting patterns and inconsistencies in information.
Strong commercial curiosity and an interest in how a logistics firm navigates complex environments.
High attention to detail, specifically when dealing with numbers, names, and technical specifications.
Clear writing skills and the ability to explain data discrepancies in plain English.
Basic proficiency in spreadsheets or similar tools used for organizing and filtering data.
A preference for ground reality and evidence over theoretical reports or generic models.
The discipline to manage your own schedule and meet deadlines in a fully remote environment.
Recent academic experience or a short internship in a field that requires rigorous research or analysis.



What we offer
Direct involvement in high-stakes advisory work where your data checks inform senior-level decisions.
A fully remote work arrangement that prioritizes outcomes and professional autonomy.
Competitive pay that is attractive for an entry-level role in the commercial advisory market.
The opportunity to learn from senior partners who remain active in every phase of the engagement.
Exposure to the practical logic of how large scale logistics expansions are managed and settled.



Working at Gulf Associates
Gulf Associates operates differently than most global consultancies. We avoid the fluff and theoretical frameworks that often clutter corporate reports. Our focus is on commercial judgment: the reality of how deals are made and how counterparties behave in the real world. We maintain a small book of active engagements to ensure that our partners can stay involved in the details of the work. This means that even as a junior analyst, you will see how your contributions influence actual negotiations and regulator conversations. Our culture is direct and honest, valuing clarity and evidence above all else. We are interested in what is happening in the territory right now, not just what a model predicts should happen. We expect every member of the team to take ownership of their work and contribute to a position that our clients can actually act on.
The firm is built on the idea that sound decisions require a clear view of the ground reality. For a logistics expansion, this means knowing exactly which assets are functional and which partners are reliable. As a Junior Data Quality Analyst, you help provide that clarity. Our remote structure is designed to support high-impact work without the distractions of a traditional office, focusing entirely on the quality of our advice and the results it produces for our clients.

Title: BI / Data Analyst (m/w/d)
Description:
Was dich erwartet

Als BI / Data Analyst verantwortest du unsere Modellierungs-, Governance- und Dashboard-Ebene, welche Auszahlungen, Partnerökonomie und operative Prozesse auf Basis interner Daten steuert. Du arbeitest täglich mit Databricks, dbt und Metabase.

Deine Aufgaben als BI/ Data Analyst


Aufbau und Pflege von dbt-Modellen in Databricks
Etablierung einer Single Source of Truth für Finance- und Operations-Kennzahlen
Aufbau einer KPI-Governance inklusive Definitionen, Verantwortlichkeiten und Data Lineage
Entwicklung von Metabase-Dashboards für Management, Finance sowie Strategy & Operations
Unterstützung und Steuerung der Migration von Tableau zu Metabase
Sicherstellung der Datenqualität durch Alerts, Schema-Checks und Data-Quality-Tests
Regelmäßige Abstimmungen mit Finance, StratOps und Management

Mehrjährige Erfahrung in BI, Analytics Engineering oder Data Analytics

Sicherer Umgang mit modernen BI-Tools

Arbeitsumgebung & Flexibilität


Office-First Company: Du arbeitest hybrid (3 Tage Office / 2 Tag Homeoffice) und selbstbestimmt – so, wie es für dich am besten passt.
Modernes, klimatisiertes Büro mit viel Tageslicht
Zentrale Lage zwischen Gleisdreieck & Potsdamer Platz mit sehr guter Anbindung
Große Dachterrasse mit atemberaubendem Blick über Berlin


Benefits


Freie Getränke, frisches Obst und Müsli
100 % Übernahme eines Deutschlandtickets für deine Mobilität
Dein Hund ist ein Teamplayer? Perfekt! Wir freuen uns über tierische Unterstützung im Büro.

Spiel & Spaß nach Feierabend: Nintendo Switch, PS5, Dart & Tischkicker warten auf dich

Agiles und modernes Mindset – offene Kommunikation – jede Stimme zählt

Wir bieten spannende Aufgaben und praxistaugliches juristisches Wissen – du kannst unsere Reise aktiv mitgestalten.

Über uns

Legalhero ist die moderne Plattform für Rechtsschutzversicherungen und Anwälte. Wir verfolgen das ehrgeizige Ziel, die Schadensfallabwicklung von Rechtsschutzversicherungen zu revolutionieren. Dazu integrieren wir uns direkt in die Systeme der Versicherer. Unsere Partneranwälte wickeln die Fälle hocheffizient und kundenorientiert auf unserer Tech-Plattform mit Hilfe von KI ab. Legalhero wurde 2017 gegründet und hat bis heute hunderttausenden von Versicherungsnehmern geholfen. Unsere ca. 90 Kollegen sitzen in Berlin, nahe dem Gleisdreieck.

Title: AI Analyst
Description:
AI Analyst




Angel Invest Ventures | Berlin, Germany | Full-time




About Angel Invest

Angel Invest is Europe’s most active early-stage investor, making 75 investments per year into European technology startups. We invest primarily in Pre-seed and Seed rounds, with a typical check size of €125k. In addition, we have a later-stage fund helping our portfolio companies grow beyond the early stage.

Our focus is on ambitious teams who are building something unique. Once invested, we actively coach founders for at least one year to help them reach product-market fit and prepare them to raise Series A rounds. Around 60% of our portfolio companies go on to raise a Series A.

We’ve backed 250+ startups that have raised more than €2 billion in follow-on funding and reached a market cap of over €4 billion. Notable portfolio companies include Rasa, Taktile, Peec AI, LizzyAI, Huzzle, dltHub, Originator, Fonio, and many more.

In 2025, we co-founded the Technology Briefing Center (TBC), a Berlin-based think tank that bridges cutting-edge innovation and strategic decision-making. The AI Analyst will be an integral part of the TBC, working at the intersection of research, corporate LP engagement, and venture capital.







Role Overview

We are looking for an AI Analyst to join our team. This is an exceptional opportunity to break into venture capital at the intersection of AI investing and research. While you’ll have exposure to the full venture capital process, including deal flow, sourcing, and investment decisions, your primary focus will be on building deep AI expertise and translating that into actionable intelligence for our investment team, portfolio companies, and corporate LPs.

As part of the Technology Briefing Center (TBC), you will lead high-impact research projects, produce technology briefings, and help organize executive events. A significant part of the role involves working directly with our corporate LPs. This includes producing regular reports and webinars, delivering tailored research and presentations to senior executives, and co-organizing invite-only events such as executive clubs and AI adoption workshops. You will become a trusted point of contact on AI topics across the organization.







Your Responsibilities

Conduct in-depth research on AI market trends, competitive landscapes, and applied AI use cases across industries.
Develop investment theses and prepare insightful presentations for our investment team and external stakeholders.
As a core member of the TBC, produce quarterly reports, webinars, and strategic analyses on AI developments.
Build and maintain relationships with corporate LP stakeholders at all levels, knowledge-sharing sessions to tailored briefings and workshops for senior executives.
Participate in deal flow meetings, providing AI-specific insights and data-driven recommendations on investment opportunities.
Work with the VC team to shape and evolve our AI investment thesis based on the latest research, technological developments, and market signals.
Support the team on AI deals by providing sector-specific context and research where needed.







Desired Qualifications

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree
Demonstrated expertise in artificial intelligence, whether through academic research, work experience, published writing, or notable projects.
Fluent in German (C2 level) and English.
Excellent communication and presentation skills, able to translate complex technical topics into clear, compelling narratives for both technical and non-technical audiences.
Independent, self-directed worker who thrives with significant autonomy in a fast-paced environment.
Genuine curiosity about how AI is reshaping industries, business models, and technology infrastructure, and a hands-on user of the latest AI tools, models, and products in your own daily workflow.







What We Offer

An unparalleled entry point into venture capital with hands-on AI sector experience from day one.
The opportunity to build deep, visible expertise in AI through research and direct engagement with corporate decision-makers.
Direct exposure to deal flow, sourcing, and investment decisions alongside experienced partners.
Access to a vast network of investors, founders, and corporate executives.
Mentorship from experienced partners and leaders in the venture capital space.
Competitive salary and benefits package (Urban Sports / BVG).
Regular off-sites and internal events







Diversity and Inclusion

Angel Invest Ventures is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We are committed to diversity, inclusion, and equal opportunities for all applicants and employees. Our hiring decisions are based on experience, passion, skills, and performance.

Title: Data Analyst - Marketing (w/m/d)
Description:
Unser Ziel ist eine Solaranlage auf jedem Dach, ein Speicher in jedem Haus und ein E-Auto in jeder Garage. Enpal macht das möglich mit einer integrierten Gesamtlösung für dezentrale Energie – von Solaranlage und Batteriespeicher über Wallbox, Smart Meter bis zur Wärmepumpe. Herzstück ist unsere KI-gestützte Plattform Enpal.One+, die tausende Systeme intelligent vernetzt und Strombezug sowie -einspeisung effizient am Energiemarkt optimiert.

Bist Du bereit für Lösungen, die mehr als nur ein Versprechen sind und täglich echte Lebensqualität in tausende Haushalte bringen? Was du bei Enpal gestaltest, liefert morgen sauberen Strom und verändert nachhaltig, wie wir Energie nutzen.

Deine Aufgaben


Du analysierst Marketing-, Kampagnen- und Kundendaten und leitest daraus konkrete Handlungsempfehlungen ab.
Du entwickelst und pflegst Dashboards, Reports und KPI-Systeme zur Erfolgsmessung.
Du identifizierst Trends, Potenziale und Optimierungsmöglichkeiten entlang der Customer Journey.
Du unterstützt bei der Analyse von Performance-Marketing-Kanälen wie SEA, SEO, Social Media, E-Mail-Marketing und Affiliate Marketing.
Du führst Ad-hoc-Analysen durch und bereitest Ergebnisse verständlich für verschiedene Stakeholder auf.
Du arbeitest eng mit Marketing-, Sales- und Produktteams zusammen.
Du unterstützt bei der Planung, Durchführung und Auswertung von A/B-Tests.
Du stellst die Datenqualität sicher und entwickelst bestehende Analyseprozesse kontinuierlich weiter.


Dein Profil


Du hast ein sehr erfolgreich abgeschlossenes Bachelor/Masterstudium an einer Top-Tier Business School
Du verfügst über erste Arbeitserfahrung oder Praktika in einem schnell wachsenden Unternehmen wie z.B. Startups oder in einer Unternehmensberatung/Consulting
Du bist kreativ und es fällt dir leicht andere Menschen zu überzeugen
Du zeichnest dich durch eine analytische, proaktive und stark lösungsorientierte Arbeitsweise aus
Du besitzt eine natürliche Stärke in der Kommunikation mit internen und externen Stakeholdern
Du hast großes Interesse am Aufbau von Unternehmen und einen ausgeprägten Willen Neues zu lernen
Du hast eine zielstrebige Arbeitsweise, ein hohes Maß an Proaktivität und Verantwortungsbewusstsein
Du besitzt Erfahrung im Priorisieren von Aufgaben in einem fast-paced Environment und kannst einen 100%-Ansatz beim Arbeiten an wichtigen Aufgaben umsetzen
Du stellst hohe Qualitätsansprüche an Dich selbst und andere und legst eine Can-Do-Mentalität an den Tag die andere motiviert
Du beherrschst Deutsch und Englisch fließend in Wort und Schrift


Du bist interessiert, auch wenn du nicht alle Anforderungen erfüllst? Bewirb dich trotzdem! Wir freuen uns darauf, dein Potenzial zu entdecken - unabhängig davon, ob deine Erfahrung jede einzelne Voraussetzung erfüllt. ☀️

Darauf kannst Du Dich freuen


Arbeite in Deutschlands erstem grünen Unicorn - Gestalte aktiv die solare Energiewende mit.
Die Sonne scheint auf der ganzen Welt - Bei Enpal arbeitest du mit einem hochmotivierten, diversen Team aus über 65 Nationalitäten.
Das ideale Setup für deinen Fokus - Wir sind überzeugt, dass exzellente Ergebnisse dort entstehen, wo die Bedingungen stimmen. Deshalb setzen wir auf ein hybrides Arbeitskonzept, das dir die Freiheit gibt, immer genau so zu arbeiten, wie es am besten zu deinen Aufgaben passt. Du brauchst neue Impulse auf internationalem Niveau? Mit Workflex bieten wir, abhängig von deiner Verantwortung, die Möglichkeit, deine Tätigkeit bis zu 30 Tage im Jahr ins Ausland zu verlegen.
Grown-up Business & Startup Spirit - In unserem modernen Office in Berlin-Friedrichshain findest du von höhenverstellbaren Tischen, Tischtennisplatte bis gefüllten Getränkekühlschränken und Barista-Coffee alles was das Herz begehrt.
Dein Kick-start bei Enpal - Onboarding-Tag mit Welcome Bag, Buddy-Programm und einem Team, das dich wirklich abholt.
Bleib auf dem Laufenden & gestalte mit - Transparente All-Hands, kurze Entscheidungswege und eine offene Feedback-Kultur, denn ohne Fehler gibt es keinen Fortschritt.
Energiewende geht nur gemeinsam - Bei Enpal erwartet dich ein legendärer Teamspirit und unvergessliche Team- und Companyevents.
Dein Extra-Boost - 29 + 2 Urlaubstage, vergünstigte Mitgliedschaft bei Wellhub und Corporate Benefits. Alles, was dir hilft, Arbeit und Leben gut zu verbinden.


Wir bei Enpal sind stolz auf die Vielfältigkeit unseres Teams. Weder bei der Einstellung noch während des Beschäftigungsverhältnisses werden Entscheidungen auf Basis von Hautfarbe, Religion oder religiösen Glaubens, ethnischer oder nationaler Herkunft, Nationalität, Geschlechteridentität, sexuelle Orientierung, Behinderung oder Alter getroffen. Enpal steht für einen sicheren Arbeitsplatz und geht gegen Diskriminierung und Belästigung jeglicher Art vor.

Title: Excel Expert - Remote
Description:
Job Description

Job Title: Excel Expert

Job Type: Contract

Location: Remote

Job Summary: In this role, you'll apply your expertise to help train next-generation AI systems. Your work will shape how models learn, reason, and perform through high-quality, real-world input. No prior experience in AI is required — your domain knowledge is what matters.

Key Responsibilities


Review, analyze, and enhance complex Excel files relevant to business operations, strategy, and data analysis.
Provide detailed feedback and examples of best practices in Excel for operational and consulting use cases (excluding finance).
Design, document, and share templates or solutions that reflect expert knowledge for AI learning purposes.
Collaborate with the customer’s team to identify key workflows and operational challenges represented in Excel documents.
Evaluate and simulate real-world business problems using advanced Excel features and formulas.
Communicate clearly and effectively in written and verbal formats, ensuring precise and comprehensive documentation.
Contribute to the creation of data analysis case studies and operational scenarios leveraging Excel expertise.


Required Skills And Qualifications


Expert-level proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including advanced functions, pivot tables, data validation, and automation tools.
Proven experience developing Excel solutions for business operations, strategy, or consulting environments.
Strong analytical skills and the ability to solve complex, non-financial business problems using Excel.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with a keen eye for detail.
Ability to document processes, create instructional materials, and provide clear, actionable feedback.
Experience with data analysis, scenario modeling, and workflow optimization in Excel.
Self-motivated, proactive, and comfortable working independently in a remote setting.


Preferred Qualifications


Background in business operations, strategy, or management consulting.
Familiarity with AI training or contributing to machine learning datasets in a domain capacity.
Experience developing Excel-based tools or dashboards for operational excellence initiatives.

Title: Data Analyst (On-site in Dubai)
Description:
Join the pioneer tribe. AI-First since December 2023.




Position: Data Analyst (A/B Testing & Experimentation)

Total Annual Package: Up to AED 348,000 (Tax-Free) + comprehensive benefits

• Base: AED 20,000 – 25,000/month (AED 240,000 – 300,000/year)

• Performance bonus: up to 10% of base, paid monthly

Location: Dubai, UAE (On-site) with relocation assistance and visa support




About the Role:

If you spend your days running A/B tests, analyzing conversion funnels, and digging into why users drop off before checkout — and you write your own SQL and Python to get those answers — this role is built for you. General analysts and program managers without hands-on technical and experimentation skills won't be a fit.




You'll own Puffy's experimentation program from hypothesis to business recommendation. You pull data from Shopify, GA4, Google Ads, and BigQuery yourself. You design the test, run the statistical analysis, and tell the business what to change and why. Every recommendation you deliver has to be clear enough that marketing, UX, and development can act on it the same week.




The Career Accelerator:

Puffy runs a high volume of experiments across a $1B+ e-commerce platform. You'll be involved in more A/B tests, conversion analyses, and data pipeline builds in your first year than most analysts see in three. You'll work across departments — marketing, UX, development, operations — which means broader context and faster pattern recognition. The pace is demanding, and it rewards people who are genuinely curious and want to sharpen their craft quickly.




What Is In It For You:

This isn't just another analytics role. This is your chance to become a true industry architect.




1. Own the Full Experimentation Cycle — Not Just the Dashboard. Most data analysts inherit someone else's reporting setup and maintain it. Here, you own hypothesis generation, test design, statistical analysis, and the business recommendation that follows. You build your own pipelines across Shopify, GA4, Google Ads, and BigQuery — no waiting in a queue for data, no presenting metrics without a point of view. You still report to the manager, but the experimentation output is yours to drive. Within 12 months, you'll have more hands-on testing reps than analysts accumulate in three years elsewhere.

2. Join the Elite Organization and Partner with the 1%:

🏆 Top 5 Mattress Brand in the USA with Over $1 Billion USD in Sales
🌍 Great Place to Work Certified
💡 Work with Forbes Council Member CEO Gain firsthand exposure to strategic decisions and high-velocity execution that drives a global brand.

3. Experience a World-Class Lifestyle. Dubai offers a tax-free salary, world-class infrastructure, year-round sunshine, and a global lifestyle that most professionals only experience later in their careers. Your entire compensation package lands in your account — no deductions, no surprises.

4. Become the Person Who Knows Why Things Convert. In most companies, analysts report numbers. Here, you're the person who tells the business what to change and why. When a test wins or loses, your analysis is the reason leadership moves. You'll build the instinct to separate signal from noise across segments, devices, and traffic sources — and the communication skills to make non-technical stakeholders act on what you find.




What You'll Need:

3+ years in data analysis, experimentation, or CRO within e-commerce, D2C, or any high-traffic transactional website (fintech, marketplaces, SaaS, banking)
Strong SQL — complex joins, window functions, CTEs across large datasets without relying on pre-built dashboards
Working Python (or R) for data manipulation, statistical analysis, and automation
Proven A/B testing experience — hypothesis design through statistical analysis to business recommendation; solid grasp of sample sizing, significance thresholds, and common pitfalls
Hands-on GA4 experience — event-based tracking, custom explorations, and the ability to tie GA4 data to other sources
Experience integrating ad platform data (Google Ads, Meta) with site analytics to see the full acquisition-to-conversion funnel
Ability to independently connect to and pull from multiple data sources (APIs, warehouses, flat files) without engineering support
Sharp analytical communication — complex findings distilled into concise recommendations for non-technical stakeholders
Proactive and curious — you spot testing opportunities and chase root causes before anyone asks




Good-to-Have:

Experience with BigQuery or AWS data warehousing, including scheduled queries and data modeling
Familiarity with session replay and heatmap tools (FullStory, Hotjar) for qualitative behavioral analysis
Background in Looker, Tableau, or similar dashboarding tools




The Puffy DNA:

We're restless, perpetually hungry, and fast-paced, driven by an unwavering belief that we can outperform any competitor — regardless of their size.

Be the Owner: We don't hire employees; we welcome owners. You're accountable for results, not activity.
Execute with Urgency: We thrive in high-stakes environments by making thoughtful decisions quickly and acting decisively.
Demand Excellence: We solve the hard problems that others can't, pushing the boundaries of our craft.
Be AI-First: Daily multi-turn AI conversations challenge your assumptions.
Go All-In: When the mission demands it, we rally as one team to cross the finish line.




✨ Your Total Compensation & Benefits:




💰 Compensation:

Monthly performance bonuses (up to 10%)
Tax-free income
Annual return flight allowance to your home country
Parking allowance (up to AED 7,800 annually)




🏥 Health & Protection:

Premium worldwide health coverage (upto AED 1Million)
Life insurance & Personal Accident Insurance (24x monthly salary)




🌴 Time Off:

22 working days annual leave + UAE public holidays




🌍 Relocation Support (International Hires):

UAE work visa sponsorship
International flights & airport transfers
14-day 4-star hotel accommodation




💼 Work Environment:

AI First organization with access to AI-native tool stack
Premium office environment in Dubai Internet City
Learning & development opportunities
International team collaboration (14+ nationalities)




🏆 Rewards & Recognition:

Eligibility for selection to the Leaders Club — an elite recognition program featuring premium wellness memberships, exclusive networking with senior leadership, and annual leadership retreats
Eligibility for nomination as 'We Are' Champion of the Month — monthly recognition with gift vouchers and company-wide celebration for exceptional contributions




✅ Other Benefits:

End of Service Gratuity per UAE law




The alternative is another year where your potential is capped and your best hypotheses die in a backlog no one reads.

Title: Staff Data Analyst, Marketing Analytics
Description:
Change the way the world travels

Join the GetYourGuide journey to connect people with unforgettable travel experiences around the world. Millions look to us for unique activities they can trust, and it’s all powered by our commitment to make every single journey extraordinary - including yours.

Ready to unlock your potential with a community of fellow explorers? Find your next role at our Berlin HQ or one of our local offices around the globe, from New York to Bangkok. Head to getyourguide.careers to take the first step.

Team mission

The Analytics department’s mission is to enable the Marketing organisation to take action based on data. We work on a variety of problems and datasets as members of cross-functional mission teams. We take ownership of the entire analytics funnel, from exploration through recommendations to impact measurement, and take pride in the insights we generate.

This specific position is within our Marketing Analytics team whose objective is to help our marketing scale our investments towards the most incremental and loyal customers. This is a very strategic position within the team as the Analyst will look at solving cross-channel problems in order to accelerate our growth. This means for example addressing the best strategy to grow our display and mid-funnel spend and understanding the behaviour of our repeat audiences.

Your mission


Develop quantitative analysis and statistical models to evaluate and steer marketing decisions
Propose and defend strategic recommendations to our top marketing leads
Setup and analyse marketing experiments to drive strategic decision making
Explore large datasets, give meaning to data and numbers, identify opportunities and issues, and present findings to stakeholders
Maintain and improve our marketing measurement tools (Attribution, experimentation framework, Customer lifetime value…)
Serve as a liaison between the marketing teams and the data engineering team
Mentor others in the skills needed for this role


Your toolkit


8+ years of relevant experience interpreting data in an analytics or data science role
A track record in solving marketing and growth challenges and influencing decisions/driving business results
Strong knowledge of SQL and Python for analysis (eg, pandas, scikit-learn, pyspark)
Ability to understand user behavior and meaning behind the numbers and a track record in sharing insights to business stakeholders
Prior exposure to digital and performance marketing or SEO, especially data-driven platforms such as Google Ads and Meta.
Experience with data visualization and reporting tools (e.g. Looker, Jupyter Notebooks, Databricks)
Desire to work in an international environment, with minimal direction and with highly engaged individuals


Extras that give you an edge


Experience in understanding and modelling marketing journeys
Experience with big data tools (e.g. Spark, Hive) is a plus


How We’ll Make Your Career Journey Extraordinary


Annual personal growth budget and mentorship programs for continuous learning and development
Work from anywhere in the world for 30 days per year
A hybrid working approach with three days of in office collaboration (Mon, Tue, Thur) and two days of optional at home focus time.
Opportunities to collaborate and socialize with team members through quarterly team events and yearly company-wide events
Monthly transportation and fitness budget
Discounts for you, your friends, and family on GetYourGuide activities
Language reimbursement program
Health and wellness benefits


And more…

How To Apply

Submit your CV/resume in English using the form below. For tips and insights into our hiring process and culture, check out ‘how we hire’ and ‘life at GetYourGuide’. If you have any further questions, please don’t hesitate to get in touch at jobs@getyourguide.com.

We’re an equal opportunities employer

Our commitment is that every qualified person will be evaluated according to their skills regardless of age, gender identity, ethnicity, sexual orientation, disability status, or religion. Please refrain from including your picture and age with your application.

Title: Marketing Data Analyst (all genders)
Description:
Your role

We are looking for a curious and insightful Marketing Data Analyst (all genders) to join our expanding Commercial Data Science team. You will play a pivotal role in shaping how we understand our customers and optimize our marketing efforts across marketing channels and countries.

In this role, you won’t just report numbers, you will be a strategic partner. You will connect the dots between acquisition and retention, using data to uncover narratives that drive efficiency and growth. If you are passionate about the intersection of data, marketing, and business impact, we would love to meet you.


Collaborate closely with Performance Marketing, CRM, and Influencer teams to align data insights with our acquisition and retention goals
Analyze complex datasets from ad platforms, such as Google, Meta, TikTok, web analytics (GA4), and internal order systems to refine targeting and budget allocation across channels and countries
Leverage dbt and BigQuery to ensure our marketing data models are robust, reliable, and ready for decision-making
Build intuitive dashboards with Tableau that provide stakeholders with clear and actionable insights
Design and analyze A/B tests and geo-lift studies and focus relentlessly on incremental lift, helping stakeholders understand what revenue we wouldn't have generated without a specific marketing touchpoint
Utilize, co-develop and validate our in-house Media Mix Models (MMM) to assess the holistic impact of our marketing spend and implement advanced analytical methods to optimize our marketing


Your profile


3-5 years in data analytics with a focus on commercial or marketing topics
Strong SQL skills are essential - you are self-sufficient in querying and manipulating large datasets within cloud environments like BigQuery to answer complex business questions
You design intuitive dashboards and reports that guide the user and present data in a way that allows stakeholders to effortlessly draw the right conclusions and make informed decisions
You have a solid understanding of the D2C marketing mix, incl. Performance, CRM, and Influencer and the KPIs that drive them, such as CAC, CLV, MER, retention rates
You are proactive, thrive in fast-paced environments and are able to navigate shifting priorities and manage multiple projects with a solution-oriented mindset
You excel at translating complex data into clear business insights and feel comfortable engaging with stakeholders to refine ideas and strategies


What We Offer


An appealing discount on all of our products – from essential oils to vitamins & nutrients
We offer 28 days of paid time off per year plus up to 5 days off between Christmas and New Year
The Urban Sports Club membership and either Swapfiets bike rental or a subsidy for the Deutschlandticket
We believe great things happen when we meet in person, which is why we embrace a hybrid model with one home office day per week – up to two depending on the area you are working in – and up to two weeks per year working from abroad
Receive various discounts of up to more than 50 percent on attractive brands such as Flaconi, Bosch, Apple or Expedia
You will be given real ownership and plenty of room for new initiatives, as well as the opportunity to contribute your expertise and implement your great ideas
Inviting office spaces with access to plenty of organic teas and superfoods from around the world
We like to celebrate each other! Regular tea & office parties, Christmas and summer feasts and various influencer events such as Women's Day


Apply with your CV and cover letter.

Sunday Natural emerged from a long-standing passion and research in the fields of health, healing, and personal growth. The lack of natural, high-quality products on the market was the original motivation for founding Sunday Natural in 2013. We aim to produce the products we ourselves would love to buy – as natural as possible, absolutely pure, free from any additives, and of the highest quality. In a strategic partnership with CVC, Sunday Natural is now one of Germany's most renowned quality manufacturers, with its own research and development department in Berlin. For the continuous improvement of our products, we collaborate with selected research institutes, scientists, therapists, farmers, and professional associations.

We take our ecological responsibility very seriously: that’s why the majority of our product range is vegan. We also take an innovative approach to packaging: most of our products are packaged in glass, making them almost entirely recyclable.

Our diverse team in Berlin consists of over 350 employees from more than 45 nationalities and continues to grow – but at a sustainable pace. Our goal is to foster a collaborative, supportive, and inclusive work environment with a diverse group of motivated, talented, and visionary team members.

We highly value equality of opportunity and inclusivity, and we would like to particularly encourage women and candidates from under-represented backgrounds to apply, even if you don’t match with 100% of the requirements.

Title: Data Analyst — Maternity Cover, 12 Months
Description:
About The Role

We are looking for a Data Analyst to join our team at Rugvista on a 12-month maternity cover. In this role, you will help the team turn data into clear insights that support better commercial, marketing, and customer experience decisions.

You will work with data from multiple sources, create and maintain reports, analyze performance across the customer journey, and support stakeholders with actionable recommendations. This role is well suited for someone who is hands-on, structured, curious, and comfortable working with both business stakeholders and data tools.

How You Make An Impact


Analyze data across areas such as website performance, marketing, sales, customer behavior, and conversion funnels.
Build, maintain, and improve recurring reports and dashboards.
Use tools such as Excel, Google Sheets, SQL, GA4, Looker Studio, Power BI, and AI-assisted workflows to extract, clean, analyze, visualize, and present data.
Support the team in understanding customer journeys, funnel performance, and key business drivers.
Translate data findings into clear insights and practical recommendations.
Collaborate with stakeholders to define reporting needs and key performance indicators.
Ensure data quality, consistency, and accuracy in reporting.
Help identify opportunities for improving performance, efficiency, and decision-making.
Use AI tools and automation opportunities to reduce manual reporting effort, improve efficiency, and make reporting processes more scalable.


What You Bring


Minimum 5 years of professional experience in a data analyst, marketing analyst, business analyst, digital analyst, or similar role.
Strong analytical skills and the ability to work with large datasets.
Good knowledge of Excel and/or Google Sheets.
Experience writing SQL queries to extract and analyze data.
Experience working with GA4 or similar web analytics tools.
Experience with reporting and BI tools such as Looker Studio or Power BI is a must.
Experience or strong interest in using AI tools to automate repetitive reporting tasks, improve workflows, and increase the speed and quality of insights.
Ability to explain data and insights clearly to non-technical stakeholders.
A structured, detail-oriented, and problem-solving mindset.
Comfortable working independently while also collaborating closely with others.


What We Offer


A diverse and international organization committed to creating and maintaining an inspiring workplace where everyone loves to go to work.
A dynamic, international organisation with high ambitions and short decision paths
A collaborative and value-driven culture with genuine respect for people and ideas
A solid benefits package, including wellness allowance


Start date

As soon as possible / per agreement.

We are reviewing candidates continuously, so please submit your application as soon as possible.

About Rugvista

Rugvista is one of Europe’s leading online rug retailers, offering a seamless digital shopping experience across +20 markets. For more than 20 years, we have built our own brand by combining design, quality and value, from accessible products to high-end handmade rugs.

We are a team of around 100 people, headquartered in Malmö with an additional office in Berlin. Listed on Nasdaq First North Premier Growth Market (RUG), our vision is to become the go-to brand for rugs in Europe.

HTML:
About The Role

We are looking for a Data Analyst to join our team at Rugvista on a 12-month maternity cover. In this role, you will help the team turn data into clear insights that support better commercial, marketing, and customer experience decisions.

You will work with data from multiple sources, create and maintain reports, analyze performance across the customer journey, and support stakeholders with actionable recommendations. This role is well suited for someone who is hands-on, structured, curious, and comfortable working with both business stakeholders and data tools.

How You Make An Impact

  • Analyze data across areas such as website performance, marketing, sales, customer behavior, and conversion funnels.
  • Build, maintain, and improve recurring reports and dashboards.
  • Use tools such as Excel, Google Sheets, SQL, GA4, Looker Studio, Power BI, and AI-assisted workflows to extract, clean, analyze, visualize, and present data.
  • Support the team in understanding customer journeys, funnel performance, and key business drivers.
  • Translate data findings into clear insights and practical recommendations.
  • Collaborate with stakeholders to define reporting needs and key performance indicators.
  • Ensure data quality, consistency, and accuracy in reporting.
  • Help identify opportunities for improving performance, efficiency, and decision-making.
  • Use AI tools and automation opportunities to reduce manual reporting effort, improve efficiency, and make reporting processes more scalable.

What You Bring

  • Minimum 5 years of professional experience in a data analyst, marketing analyst, business analyst, digital analyst, or similar role.
  • Strong analytical skills and the ability to work with large datasets.
  • Good knowledge of Excel and/or Google Sheets.
  • Experience writing SQL queries to extract and analyze data.
  • Experience working with GA4 or similar web analytics tools.
  • Experience with reporting and BI tools such as Looker Studio or Power BI is a must.
  • Experience or strong interest in using AI tools to automate repetitive reporting tasks, improve workflows, and increase the speed and quality of insights.
  • Ability to explain data and insights clearly to non-technical stakeholders.
  • A structured, detail-oriented, and problem-solving mindset.
  • Comfortable working independently while also collaborating closely with others.

What We Offer

  • A diverse and international organization committed to creating and maintaining an inspiring workplace where everyone loves to go to work.
  • A dynamic, international organisation with high ambitions and short decision paths
  • A collaborative and value-driven culture with genuine respect for people and ideas
  • A solid benefits package, including wellness allowance

Start date

As soon as possible / per agreement.

We are reviewing candidates continuously, so please submit your application as soon as possible.

About Rugvista

Rugvista is one of Europe’s leading online rug retailers, offering a seamless digital shopping experience across +20 markets. For more than 20 years, we have built our own brand by combining design, quality and value, from accessible products to high-end handmade rugs.

We are a team of around 100 people, headquartered in Malmö with an additional office in Berlin. Listed on Nasdaq First North Premier Growth Market (RUG), our vision is to become the go-to brand for rugs in Europe.

Title: Data Analysis Werkstudent:in (w/m/d)
Description:
Unser Ziel ist eine Solaranlage auf jedem Dach, ein Speicher in jedem Haus und ein E-Auto in jeder Garage. Enpal macht das möglich mit einer integrierten Gesamtlösung für dezentrale Energie – von Solaranlage und Batteriespeicher über Wallbox, Smart Meter bis zur Wärmepumpe. Herzstück ist unsere KI-gestützte Plattform Enpal.One+, die tausende Systeme intelligent vernetzt und Strombezug sowie -einspeisung effizient am Energiemarkt optimiert.

Bist Du bereit für Lösungen, die mehr als nur ein Versprechen sind und täglich echte Lebensqualität in tausende Haushalte bringen? Was du bei Enpal gestaltest, liefert morgen sauberen Strom und verändert nachhaltig, wie wir Energie nutzen.

Deine Aufgaben


Beantwortung einfacher Use Cases auf Basis bereits vorhandener Data Views (mit Fokus auf Sales-Daten)
Erstellung und Pflege von Dashboards zur Visualisierung von Vertriebskennzahlen
Wartung bestehender KPIs sowie Integration neuer Kennzahlen im Sales Kontext
Entwicklung und Anpassung einfacher Kalkulationen (z.B. Forecast, Performance-Metriken)
Unterstützung bei der Strukturierung und Pflege von Data-Warehouse-Lösungen


Dein Profil


Laufendes Studium im Bereich Wirtschaftsinformatik, Data Science, BWL mit Analytics-Schwerpunkt oder vergleichbar
Erste praktische Erfahrung oder starkes Interesse an Datenanalysen
Sicherer Umgang mit SQL
Erfahrung im Dashboarding (z.B. mit Power BI, Tableau oder ähnlichen Tools) von Vorteil
Grundkenntnisse im Data Warehousing
Erste Erfahrung mit Snowflake von Vorteil
Grundkenntnisse in Sales-Prozessen wünschenswert
Erfahrung mit Salesforce von Vorteil
Analytisches Denkvermögen und strukturierte Arbeitsweise
Fähigkeit, einfache Anforderungen effizient und pragmatisch umzusetzen


Du bist interessiert, auch wenn du nicht alle Anforderungen erfüllst? Bewirb dich trotzdem! Wir freuen uns darauf, dein Potenzial zu entdecken - unabhängig davon, ob deine Erfahrung jede einzelne Voraussetzung erfüllt. ☀️

Darauf kannst du dich freuen


Arbeite in Deutschlands erstem grünen Unicorn - Gestalte aktiv die solare Energiewende mit.
Die Sonne scheint auf der ganzen Welt - Bei Enpal arbeitest du mit einem hochmotivierten, diversen Team aus über 65 Nationalitäten.
Das ideale Setup für deinen Fokus - Wir sind überzeugt, dass exzellente Ergebnisse dort entstehen, wo die Bedingungen stimmen. Deshalb setzen wir auf ein hybrides Arbeitskonzept, das dir die Freiheit gibt, immer genau so zu arbeiten, wie es am besten zu deinen Aufgaben passt. Du brauchst neue Impulse auf internationalem Niveau? Mit Workflex bieten wir, abhängig von deiner Verantwortung, die Möglichkeit, deine Tätigkeit bis zu 30 Tage im Jahr ins Ausland zu verlegen.
Grown-up Business & Startup Spirit - In unserem modernen Office in Berlin-Friedrichshain findest du von höhenverstellbaren Tischen, Tischtennisplatte bis gefüllten Getränkekühlschränken und Barista-Coffee alles was das Herz begehrt.
Dein Kick-start bei Enpal - Onboarding-Tag mit Welcome Bag, Buddy-Programm und einem Team, das dich wirklich abholt.
Bleib auf dem Laufenden & gestalte mit - Transparente All-Hands, kurze Entscheidungswege und eine offene Feedback-Kultur, denn ohne Fehler gibt es keinen Fortschritt.
Energiewende geht nur gemeinsam - Bei Enpal erwartet dich ein legendärer Teamspirit und unvergessliche Team- und Companyevents.
Dein Extra-Boost - 29 + 2 Urlaubstage, vergünstigte Mitgliedschaft bei Wellhub und Corporate Benefits. Alles, was dir hilft, Arbeit und Leben gut zu verbinden.


Wir bei Enpal sind stolz auf die Vielfältigkeit unseres Teams. Weder bei der Einstellung noch während des Beschäftigungsverhältnisses werden Entscheidungen auf Basis von Hautfarbe, Religion oder religiösen Glaubens, ethnischer oder nationaler Herkunft, Nationalität, Geschlechteridentität, sexuelle Orientierung, Behinderung oder Alter getroffen. Enpal steht für einen sicheren Arbeitsplatz und geht gegen Diskriminierung und Belästigung jeglicher Art vor.

Title: Analyst:in im Energiedatenmanagement (w/m/d)
Description:
About Us

Engelhart was founded in 2013 by BTG Pactual Group as a commodities trading company. Our business model is “asset light” and highly diversified – giving us the ability to adapt effectively and nimbly to changing market conditions. We have assembled successful multidisciplinary teams, leveraging advanced fundamental analysis with deep quantitative and weather research capabilities. Our activities are underpinned by strong risk management practices and by powerful technology and operational excellence. We have exceptional teams with diverse global backgrounds and decades of experience, and are driven by a highly collaborative culture, across products and competencies.

In 2024, Engelhart acquired Trailstone, a global energy trading and technology company. The acquisition provides us with new expertise, analytics and proprietary technology which is being used to provide risk management and optimisation services to help maximise the value of our clients’ renewable power. The acquisition also expanded Engelhart’s capabilities into physical natural gas across North America, a critical fuel to support the energy transition.

Our talented and experienced individuals work together according to its four company values: Performance, Agility, Collaboration, Entrepreneurship.

Über die Rolle

Wir suchen eine(n) Analyst:in im Energiedatenmanagement (w/m/d) für unser Berliner Büro am Ernst-Reuter-Platz. Diese Person wird unseren Energiedatenmanager direkt unterstützen, um den reibungslosen Ablauf der täglichen und monatlichen Aufgaben in den Bereichen Bilanzierung und Abrechnung bei Engelhart zu gewährleisten.

In dieser Funktion haben Sie die Möglichkeit, den deutschen Energiemarkt von der Pike auf zu lernen und zu verstehen. Ab dem ersten Tag übernehmen Sie eigenverantwortlich operative Aufgaben und sind unmittelbar in die laufenden Prozesse eingebunden. Zudem bieten wir Ihnen die Möglichkeit zur kontinuierlichen fachlichen und persönlichen Weiterentwicklung, auch in angrenzenden Aufgabenfeldern.

Dies wird eine Vollzeitstelle mit folgenden Aufgaben:


Verwaltung und Plausibilisierung von Einspeisedaten sowie deren Integration in unser Energiedatenmanagement-System
Pflege von Stammdaten und Abbildung von energiewirtschaftlichen Prozessen (z. B. Bilanzierung, Prognosen, Abrechnung)
Überwachung des Datenaustauschs mit Netzbetreibern, Messstellenbetreibern und Kund:innen
Unterstützung bei der Abwicklung von Marktprozessen (GPKE, MaBiS, etc.)
Mitwirkung bei der Abrechnung der Direktvermarktung, einschließlich Marktprämien und Bilanzkreisabrechnungen
Mitarbeit an der kontinuierlichen Optimierung der Datenprozesse und Schnittstellen
Enge Zusammenarbeit mit IT, Portfoliomanagement und dem Vertrieb


Über Sie

Sie werden unser EDM-Team im Berlin verstärken, um unseren Energiehandel durch Datenmanagement zu unterstützen und zu stärken. Ihr Interesse an erneuerbaren Energien sowie Ihre sorgfältige und detailorientierte Arbeitsweise bilden dabei die Grundlage für Ihren Erfolg in dieser Position.

Außerdem suchen wir jemanden mit folgenden Eigenschaften:


Abgeschlossene Ausbildung oder Studium im Bereich Energiewirtschaft, Wirtschaftsinformatik, Betriebswirtschaftslehre oder vergleichbare Qualifikationen
Analytische Denkweise, strukturierte Arbeitsweise und Teamfähigkeit
Sicherer Umgang mit MS Excel, idealerweise Kenntnisse in SQL oder BI-Tools
Kenntnisse in energiewirtschaftlichen Marktprozessen (z. B. GPKE, MaBiS, Redispatch 2.0) sind von Vorteil
Erste Erfahrung im Energiedatenmanagement oder in der Direktvermarktung sind wünschenswert
Erfahrung mit der EDM-Software FirstSale ist wünschenswert, aber keine Voraussetzung
Sprachkenntnisse: Deutsch mindestens C1, Englisch mindestens B2


Was wir bieten


Wettbewerbsfähige Vergütung und Teilnahme am Engelhart-Bonusplan nach eigenem Ermessen.
28 Tage Jahresurlaub plus Berliner Feiertage.
€3.000 pro Jahr Beitrag zur privaten Altersvorsorge für Sie.
€20 monatlicher Zuschuss zum BVG-Ticket.
€25 monatlicher Zuschuss zur ClassPass-Mitgliedschaft.
Ein geselliges Arbeitsumfeld in einem brandneuen Bürogebäude mit einer offenen, internationalen Kultur.


We believe in inclusivity and are therefore dedicated to ensuring all employees – across gender identity, race, ethnicity, sexual orientation, religion, life experience, background and more – feel welcome and included in the company. We promote diversity because we believe it is essential to our ability to think holistically.

Title: Robotics Data Collector (Germany)
Description:
CNTXT AI is a leading artificial intelligence company focused on building next-generation AI systems, products, and infrastructure. We specialize in developing multimodal AI, robotics, and intelligent agents, powered by high-quality real-world data. Data is at the core of our innovation. We invest heavily in data collection, annotation, and AI training pipelines to enable advanced systems such as embodied AI and Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models.

Role Summary

CNTXT AI is looking for a Robotics Data Collector to support the collection of high-quality robotics and egocentric data used to train AI models.
This role involves working hands-on with robotic systems, sensors, and real-world environments to capture multimodal datasets, including video, motion, and interaction data. You will play a key role in enabling the development of intelligent robotic systems and AI agents.
The ideal candidate is detail-oriented, technically curious, and comfortable working in lab and controlled real-world environments.

Key Responsibilities

1. Data Collection Execution

Collect robotics and egocentric (first-person) data using:

Cameras (handheld, head-mounted, or robot-mounted)

Sensors (IMU, depth cameras, etc.)

Execute data collection tasks based on predefined scenarios and protocols.

Ensure consistent and accurate recording of actions, interactions, and environments.





2. Robotics Operation Support

Assist in operating robotic systems (humanoid or mobile robots) during data collection.

Support setup and calibration of:

Cameras and sensors

Recording systems

Ensure proper functioning of hardware during experiments.





3. Data Quality & Validation

Review collected data for:

Completeness

Clarity

Accuracy

Identify and flag issues such as:

Missing data

Recording errors

Sensor misalignment

Follow quality guidelines to ensure high usability for AI training.





4. Experiment Setup & Execution

Prepare environments for data collection sessions.

Execute tasks such as:

Object manipulation

Navigation scenarios

Human-robot interaction recordings

Follow detailed instructions to simulate real-world use cases.





5. Documentation & Reporting

Maintain records of:

Data collection sessions

Equipment usage

Issues encountered

Provide feedback to improve data collection processes and protocols.





6. Safety & Compliance

Follow all lab safety protocols and operational guidelines.

Handle robotics equipment and sensors with care.

Ensure compliance with data privacy and security requirements.





Required Qualifications


0–3+ years of experience

Data collection, field operations, or lab environments

Technical or hardware-related roles

Comfortable working with:

Cameras, sensors, and recording devices

Basic computer systems and tools

Strong attention to detail and ability to follow instructions precisely.





Preferred Skills

Experience with robotics systems or hardware environments.

Familiarity with:

Egocentric or first-person data collection

Computer vision or AI-related datasets

Basic understanding of:

Robotics concepts

Sensors and data recording tools

Experience working in technical labs or research environments.





Key Traits

Detail-oriented and quality-focused

Hands-on and execution-driven

Reliable and consistent in following protocols

Comfortable working in dynamic environments

Curious about robotics and AI





Success Metrics

Volume and quality of data collected

Accuracy and completeness of datasets

Adherence to data collection protocols

Reduction in data errors and rework

Efficiency in executing data collection tasks





Why Join CNTXT AI

Work on cutting-edge robotics and AI systems

Contribute to building next-generation intelligent agents

Gain hands-on experience with robotics labs and real-world data

Be part of a fast-growing, innovation-driven company

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Job Title: Data Engineer (f/m/x)

Location: Berlin, Germany

Company: Innovative AI Startup; B2B SaaS




About Us:

Join our dynamic and rapidly growing B2B SaaS startup, SAIZ, based in the heart of Berlin! We're on a mission to revolutionize the way apparel businesses operate. Powered by advanced analytics and artificial intelligence, we provide cutting-edge software solutions that empower apparel brands to reduce returns, enhance customer satisfaction, and embrace sustainability. Our team thrives on innovation, collaboration, and a startup culture that encourages independence and creativity.

Role Overview:

We are seeking a talented and motivated Data Engineer (f/m/x) to join our team. In this role, you will be instrumental in designing, developing, and maintaining our data infrastructure, ensuring the smooth integration and processing of data. You will work closely with various teams, including data analysts and clients, to support data-driven decision-making and enhance our product offerings. As a team, we plan our tasks on Jira and include them in our sprints. You will have the freedom to develop your own ideas




Key Responsibilities:

Data Pipeline Development: Design, implement, and maintain efficient data pipelines to facilitate seamless data integration and processing.
Data analytics: Build dashboards, monitor data processes & outputs, Data models
Data Infrastructure: Build and optimize data infrastructure to ensure reliability, scalability, and performance of our data systems.
Client Collaboration: Work with clients to understand their data requirements and provide technical support for data-related issues.
Data Management: Ensure data quality, consistency, and security across various data sources and systems.
Problem Solving: Address and resolve complex data-related challenges, using your technical skills to devise effective solutions.
Continuous Improvement: Stay updated with the latest industry trends and best practices to enhance our data processes and technologies.




Qualifications:

Technical Proficiency: Strong skills in Python and SQL are essential. Familiarity with data visualization tools (e.g., Metabase, Tableau, Power BI), as well as building and maintaining data pipelines for reading, structuring, cleaning, and testing data, is highly desirable. Airflow & DBT are a plus.
3+ years of work experience required
Analytical Mindset: A solid understanding of data structures, algorithms, and data modeling.
Educational Background: Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
Experience: Previous experience in a data engineering role, particularly in a startup or fast-paced environment, is preferred.
Independence: Ability to work independently and manage multiple tasks effectively.
Fast Learner: Eagerness to learn and adapt quickly in a dynamic startup environment.
Communication Skills: Excellent verbal and written communication skills to collaborate with technical and non-technical stakeholders.




Why Join Us?

Startup Culture: Be part of an energetic and innovative startup environment.
Impact: Directly influence our product development and business strategies with your insights.
Growth Opportunities: Expand your skills and grow with our company as we continue to scale.
Collaborative Team: Work alongside a passionate and driven team that values creativity and independence.
The job is full-time and remote

﻿

How to apply:

If you are enthusiastic about working in a startup environment and passionate about data engineering, we would love to hear from you! Please apply via the Easy Apply function or email careers@saiz.io , including your resume and answers to a few questions.

At SAIZ, we are committed to creating an inclusive and supportive environment where all individuals can thrive. We warmly welcome candidates of all different backgrounds and identities to apply, valuing the unique perspectives each individual brings. Our ongoing dedication is to further diversify our team, enriching our collective experiences and driving innovation.




Join us in our mission to transform the apparel industry and digitize production in fashion with innovative data-driven solutions.

Title: Document Reviewer - Remote
Description:
Job Description

Job Title: Document Reviewer

Job Type: Contract

Location: Remote

Job Summary: In this role, you'll apply your expertise to help train next-generation AI systems. Your work will shape how models learn, reason, and perform through high-quality, real-world input.

Key Responsibilities


Carefully review large volumes of structured and unstructured data, ensuring accuracy and completeness.
Identify and flag issues related to data quality, consistency, and potential risks involving Personal Identifiable Information (PII).
Annotate, classify, and, where necessary, redact sensitive information to maintain privacy and compliance standards.
Follow strict guidelines, taxonomies, and workflow processes for data categorization and validation.
Perform quality assurance (QA) checks to verify data integrity and validate outputs.
Maintain detailed records of reviewed documents and actions taken to ensure traceability and accountability.
Collaborate with the customer's team, providing clear and concise written and verbal communication about findings and recommendations.


Required Skills And Qualifications


Proven experience in document review, auditing, annotation, redaction, or data classification at scale.
Exceptional attention to detail with a high level of accuracy in repetitive tasks.
Familiarity with handling and protecting Personal Identifiable Information (PII).
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to document findings and communicate effectively with team members.
Demonstrated ability to follow complex guidelines and adhere to established taxonomies.
Experience performing QA or validation work on large data sets.
Ability to balance efficiency with precision in a remote work environment.


Preferred Qualifications


Background in legal, compliance, privacy, auditing, compliance or information security domains.
Prior experience supporting data-driven AI or machine learning projects.
Comfort with collaboration tools and digital workflow management platforms.
