Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst – EIA/FES (DAP & Palantir Foundry)

Role Purpose

Support Energy Insights & Analysis (EIA) and Future Energy Scenarios (FES) by delivering high‑quality analysis, dashboards and data products on NESO’s Data & Analytics Platform (DAP) using Palantir Foundry. The role focuses on transforming complex energy system data into trusted insights that inform modelling, scenario development and strategic decision‑making.

Key Responsibilities


Perform exploratory and structured analysis on large, complex datasets (time‑series, scenario, market, network and policy data)
Support EIA/FES modelling teams by preparing, validating and analysing input and output datasets
Develop reproducible analysis workflows using SQL and Python within Foundry
Produce clear, well‑structured insight packs, visualisations and narratives for senior and non‑technical stakeholders
Work closely with Data Scientists, Data Engineers and Modellers to align analysis with business and policy questions
Ensure data outputs meet quality, governance, lineage and assurance requirements
Contribute to continuous improvement of analytical standards, templates and reusable assets across EIA/FES


Essential Skills & Experience


Strong experience in SQL and data analysis using Python
Experience analysing time‑series and multi‑source datasets
Strong data visualisation and storytelling skills
Ability to explain analytical findings clearly to non‑technical audiences


Desirable


Experience in energy systems, utilities, infrastructure or public‑sector analytics
Familiarity with scenario analysis, modelling outputs or forecasting concepts
Experience working in assurance‑led or regulated environments
Understanding of DAP and Foundry concepts (data products, lineage, governed analytics)


Ways of Working


Agile, collaborative delivery
Strong partnership with modelling, policy and digital teams
Focus on accuracy, transparency and reusability

Title: People Insight Analyst
Description:
Role: People Insight AnalystWor...

Role: People Insight Analyst

Work Level: £35,000 - £40,000 + up to 15% bonus + bens

Contract Type: Permanent

Location: LU5 5XE, Dunstable, 3 days in the office, 2 days working from home

Role Overview

Are you passionate about turning data into compelling stories that shape real business decisions? Join our People Insight team as a People Insight Analyst and play a pivotal role in delivering meaningful reporting and analytics that directly influence how we understand and develop our people.

This is an exciting opportunity to be at the heart of a modern, data-led transformation, helping to evolve reporting into a highly automated, scalable and insight-driven model. You will work with cutting-edge tools, building dashboards and reporting solutions that empower stakeholders to make informed decisions quickly and confidently.

You will collaborate with teams across the business, translating complex data into clear, actionable insights, helping leaders spot trends, identify risks and unlock opportunities. Your work will have tangible impact, whether responding to ad hoc insight requests, supporting strategic analytics roadmaps, or enabling self-service reporting.

This role offers excellent development potential. You will strengthen your skills in data storytelling, analytics, and automation, while gaining exposure to strategic business partnering and statutory reporting requirements. You will also contribute to building capability across the People function, helping others unlock the power of data.

If you thrive on solving problems, enjoy working with stakeholders, and take pride in delivering accurate, trusted insights, this is a highly rewarding role where your work truly matters.

Minimum Criteria

To succeed in this role, you will demonstrate:


Experience producing automated dashboards or reports (e.g. using Excel formulas or Power BI) within a business environment
Proven ability to manipulate and analyse large datasets, including data cleansing, validation and reconciliation of outputs against source systems (e.g. HR systems such as Dayforce)
Hands-on experience managing and responding to ad hoc stakeholder insight requests, including prioritising workload through ticketing, backlog or SLA-driven processes
Evidence of translating complex data into clear, actionable insights, for example presenting trends, risks or KPI performance to non-technical audiences
Working knowledge of people metrics (e.g. attrition, hiring, absence, diversity data) or similar operational KPIs, and experience supporting regular or statutory reporting cycles


We believe that everyone is unique and there should be no barriers to entry and no limits to ambition. We are committed to being an inclusive organisation that values diversity and welcomes your application whatever your background or situation.

Under-represented groups such as women, ethnic minorities, people with disabilities & members of the LGBTQIA+ community (those who identify as lesbian, gay, bi, trans and non-binary or those who use a different LGBTQIA+ term), are strongly encouraged to build a career with us. Speak to us about workplace adjustments, part-time and flexible working. Where possible we will support this.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
AND Introduction

We're on a mission to close the world's tech skills gap.

We help organisations navigate the future of technology, combining human expertise, emerging tech and AI to deliver better outcomes, faster.

Since 2014, we've worked side-by-side with clients to solve complex challenges, build high-performing teams, and create lasting capability. As technology continues to evolve, we believe the most successful organisations will be those that combine the best of both: human ingenuity AND intelligent technology.

That belief is embedded in everything we do. We call it the genius of the AND: deep expertise AND practical delivery, innovation AND responsibility, ambitious work AND sustainable careers.

Through our Guide, Build and Equip approach, we help organisations embrace change, deliver meaningful impact, and develop the skills they need to thrive in an increasingly agentic world.

About You

As a Data Analyst in a club of over 80 people, you will spend the majority of your time helping deliver client projects in data, build our data analysis capabilities, growing our product analysts and influencing how we think about data, both within AND and for our clients. You will be responsible for conducting effective analysis to clarify, evaluate complex business problems and requirements for change in a manner which supports the strategic goals of large projects. You will also be part of the Business Analysis and Product communities helping to nurture and grow team members practices and processes. This will include

Helping Grow Our Data Analysis Capability


Building close relationships with analysts in your Club, understanding their needs and the challenges they are facing on client engagements. Mentoring junior and mid-level analysts in your club in the field of Data Analysis
Guiding and supporting the acquisition or improvement of data skills for the junior to senior developers in your Club, during their time in internal projects between client assignments
Helping define best practices and standards both in your Club and working as a part of the wider AND community, ensuring that they are followed during client assignment
Providing insight and conducting research in progressive data analysis techniques


Delivering Progressive Data Solutions For Clients


Documentation of requirements using various methods, such as: Roadmap; Process Mapping; Story Maps; Use Cases; Impact Mapping; Functional and Non-Functional Requirements Specifications; Features (User Stories), Data Mapping and Data Profiling Work across multiple teams that have high impact and importance for the business
Collaborating with the Principal Data Engineer to help establish both your Club's and AND's wider Data proposition and develop thought leadership
Championing and upholding high analysis quality standards and best practice
Representing the field of Data Analysis at company functions, including hiring, and within our client acquisition to support growth and academy teams
Engaging actively with our Consultancy Data Strategy and Insight practice, to help define and materialise clients' data strategies


To Do That, It's Essential You Bring The Following


Experience of pragmatic, hands-on analysis of enterprise-scale data projects across the full SDLC from design to migration, integration and live service in an Agile environment
Hands-on experience of modern software delivery techniques and tooling in the analysis space, including usage of Jira and Confluence (or equivalent), story mapping, estimation and dependency mapping
Experience in the field of Data Analysis, Science or Analytics including a keen understanding of industry best-practice around standards, quality and continuous improvement
Experience of analysis and design of solutions using one or more from: SQL and noSQL technologies, data streaming, logging and monitoring tools, BI and Data Warehousing solutions and ETL and migration technologies
A thorough understanding of one or more from: Master Data Management, Data Lineage analysis, Metadata Management, Data Quality assessment and management, Data Transformation and migration
Ability to apply advanced analytics and statistics to business problems
Proven experience of working with large volumes of data, applying tools such as SAS, R, Python, SQL, PowerBI, Tableau, Looker
Excellent communication skills and ability to tell stories / influence with data


It's Helpful If You Also Have


Consultancy experience across a number of sectors
Experience supporting and enabling analysis communities, helping them have purpose and influence on company policy and approach
Knowledge of and experience implementing modern technologies to deliver Data visualisation, personalisation or decision support tooling
Hands on technical experience implementing or managing SQL or noSQL solutions
Experience of leading small teams, including acting as Scrum master


Why join AND Digital?

We're building a culture where talented people can do meaningful work, continue growing, AND enjoy the journey along the way.

Our model is designed to build belonging and connection: we're organised into regional ‘Clubs' of no more than 80 people, so you (and our clients) get the benefits of that small company feel while also being connected to a larger whole.

Our Practice Areas act as a ‘second home' for our ANDis - a place where you can innovate and learn with like-minded experts who care deeply about their craft, support each other generously, and believe the best ideas come from collaboration, not hierarchy.

Our culture is rooted in our values of Wonder, Share and Delight, and shows up through five traits that we celebrate and nurture in all our ANDis: we value curiosity, ambition and a growth-mindset. We are deeply client-centric. Most important of all we are human - we create an inclusive environment where people feel trusted, supported and able to be themselves.

By Joining AND, We'll Provide


25 days bookable holiday + flexible Bank Holidays
Pension: 6% of salary paid by AND Digital with a further 2% paid by you (can be increased by choice).
Aviva healthcare cover (including pre-existing condition cover) for you.
Flexibenefit: £1000 assigned to you via our benefits portal to select or upgrade the benefits that suit you the most. Any unused allowance from the £1000 can be taken as cash.
Life Assurance.
Income Protection.
Eye test + first pair of glasses.
Parental Benefits: Generous Enhanced Maternity and Enhanced Partner (Paternity) Leave.


Equal Opportunities Statement

Diversity and inclusion are hugely important to us, and we're committed to providing equal opportunities for all. We're actively recruiting for a diverse and inclusive workforce so want to ensure we do everything we can to support your application.

We want you to feel safe and empowered to let us know if you need any adjustments to be made to your application or interview process, so please speak to our recruitment team.

Title: Business Systems Analyst
Description:
Business Systems Analyst - ERP/ CRM/ Power BI - Bedford based

4 days/ week on-site with 1 day working from home.




We are looking for a proactive and detail-driven Business Systems Analyst to join our client's growing team based in Bedfordshire. This pivotal role brings together system administration, technical support, data insights, and workflow automation to optimise efficiency and innovate processes across the organisation.




If you're passionate about leveraging technology to improve business operations, this is the perfect opportunity to bring your expertise to a dynamic and supportive environment.




Key Responsibilities:

Manage and support core business systems including CRM and ERP platforms.
Oversee integrations and connections between key systems.
Develop insightful reports and dashboards using Excel, Power BI, and Tableau.
Deliver data insights to diverse internal stakeholders.
Create and deploy workflow automations via Power Automate.
Collaborate closely on projects such as system migrations, upgrades, and feature rollouts.
Partner with IT managers, internal teams, and external vendors to implement improvements.




Key Skills required:

Strong proficiency in Excel (Power Query, Pivot Tables, advanced formulas).
Experience in supporting CRM and ERP systems.
Effective problem-solving and communication skills.
Capacity to juggle multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Fluency with Microsoft 365 tools, including Teams, Outlook, Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Forms, and Copilot.
Excellent communication skills with experience working with a range of stakeholders.




Salary:

Salary on offer is between £45,000 - £50,000 depending on experience.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Closing Date

2026-08-21

Job Title: Senior Data Analyst

Location: Durrington / Hybrid working

Contract Type: Permanent

Hours: 37 hours per week

Salary: from £45k

About The Role

Southern Water is undertaking one of the most ambitious transformations in the UK water sector.

Over the next five years, we will invest £7.8bn to improve resilience, environmental performance, operational performance and customer outcomes. At the centre of that transformation sits Data, Analytics and AI. We're building a future where decisions are increasingly informed by evidence, where data becomes a strategic asset, and where analytics helps drive better outcomes for customers, communities, regulators and the environment.

Our ambition is to become a truly data-driven organisation, embedding analytics into everyday decision-making and ensuring insight is translated into meaningful action. That means moving beyond reporting and dashboards towards deeper understanding, better recommendations and smarter decisions across every part of the business.

As a Senior Data Analyst, you will play a critical role in making that ambition a reality.

This is an opportunity to work on challenging, high-impact problems that matter. You'll partner with stakeholders across the organisation, help shape decisions through evidence and insight, and influence how Southern Water uses data to improve operational and business performance.

For someone who enjoys combining analytical rigour, business understanding and stakeholder influence, this role offers the opportunity to make a genuine impact while helping build the future of analytics within one of the UK's largest water companies.

About The Role


As a Senior Data Analyst, you'll lead medium-to-high complexity analytical work from problem definition through to decision-ready recommendations.
You'll work closely with operational, commercial and strategic stakeholders to understand business challenges, frame analytical approaches and deliver clear, actionable insight. You'll help move conversations beyond reporting and into understanding causes, opportunities and decisions.
Alongside delivering your own analysis, you'll contribute to raising analytical standards across the organisation, helping build a culture where evidence, critical thinking and curiosity drive better outcomes.
This role sits at the intersection of data, business and decision-making, making it ideal for someone who enjoys solving real-world problems and influencing meaningful change.


What You'll Be Doing

Solve High-Value Business Problems


Lead end-to-end analytical initiatives, from understanding the business problem through to delivering recommendations and measurable outcomes.
Translate complex or ambiguous questions into clear analytical approaches, hypotheses and success measures.
Identify opportunities where data and analytics can improve decisions, performance or customer outcomes.
Help stakeholders move from intuition-based decision making towards evidence-based action.


Deliver Insight That Drives Action


Design and deliver high-quality analytical products, including dashboards, metrics, models and decision-support tools.
Transform complex analysis into clear, compelling stories that influence business decisions.
Communicate findings confidently to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Ensure insights are actionable, relevant and aligned to business priorities.


Build Trusted Analytical Products


Develop robust metrics, reusable analytical assets and automated solutions that support recurring decision-making.
Ensure analytical outputs are reliable, transparent and fit for purpose.
Work closely with data engineering and BI teams to improve data availability, quality and accessibility.
Contribute to the development of scalable analytical approaches that can be reused across multiple business areas.


Raise Analytical Standards


Review and assure analytical work produced by colleagues.
Support the adoption of analytical standards, frameworks and best practices.
Promote good analytical thinking, quality assurance and evidence-led decision making.
Contribute to the continuous improvement of methods, tooling and ways of working.


Support And Develop Others


Coach and mentor analysts through feedback, collaboration and knowledge sharing.
Support the growth of analytical capability across the wider team.
Contribute to communities of practice, learning initiatives and capability development activities.
Help create an environment where analysts are supported, challenged and able to develop their careers.


What Success Looks Like


In this role, you will help create an environment where:
Business decisions are supported by trusted evidence and insight.
Analytical products are reliable, repeatable and consistently valuable.
Stakeholders understand both what is happening and why.
Data is used more effectively to improve operational and business outcomes.
Analytical capability continues to mature across the organisation.
Colleagues grow in confidence, capability and impact.
Most importantly, your work will help ensure analytics creates measurable value and delivers meaningful outcomes for customers, the environment and the business.


Essential

What You'll Bring


Proven experience delivering high-value analytical work within a complex organisation.
Strong capability in problem framing, analytical design and method selection.
Experience translating business challenges into clear analytical approaches and recommendations.
Strong data storytelling and communication skills, with the ability to explain complex findings clearly to non-technical audiences.
Experience designing and developing metrics, dashboards, models or analytical products that support decision-making.
Ability to influence stakeholders, challenge assumptions and drive evidence-based conversations.
Experience working with large and complex datasets.
Strong analytical thinking, curiosity and problem-solving skills.
Experience coaching, reviewing or supporting other analysts through mentoring, feedback or collaborative working.
Practical experience using analytical and visualisation tools such as Power BI, Databricks, SQL, Alteryx or equivalent technologies.


Desirable


Experience working with modern cloud-based analytics platforms and data ecosystems.
Knowledge of semantic modelling, data products or scalable analytics architectures.
Exposure to advanced analytics, statistical techniques or predictive modelling.
Familiarity with data governance, analytical quality assurance or data ethics.
Experience working within a regulated or operationally complex environment.


Southern Water is at the forefront of transforming Britain’s water industry, investing significantly to enhance resilience, sustainability, and service excellence. With £7.8bn planned investment for 2025-30, this is an unparalleled opportunity to join a business committed to delivering a generational shift in the way water services are managed.

You will be joining at a time of significant change, working alongside a highly skilled leadership team with a clear vision for the future. We offer an environment where senior professionals can make a meaningful impact, influence major strategic decisions, and drive long-term value creation

At Southern Water, we believe diverse perspectives drive innovation. If you’re passionate about making a positive impact and think you can bring value to our team, we’d love to hear from you—even if you don’t tick every box. Your unique skills and experiences could be exactly what we need.

If this role isn’t quite what you’re looking for but are keen to be contacted about opportunities at Southern Water, you can register your details here: Introduce Yourself (myworkdaysite.com- Introduce Yourself)

Our Commitment to Diversity

We welcome applicants from all backgrounds, identities, and experiences. We do not discriminate based on race, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, age, disability, religion, or any other protected characteristic. If you need reasonable adjustments during the recruitment process, please let us know.

Additional Information

In line with Southern Water’s security requirements, successful candidates will be required to provide evidence of their identity, eligibility to work in the UK, criminal record check (DBS) and verification of their employment and/or education history for the past three years.

Appointment to this role is subject to the successful completion of all pre‑employment checks, including security vetting.

Please note that if a candidate does not meet the required security standards or fails to pass the vetting process, Southern Water reserves the right to withdraw the offer of employment. Some positions may also require higher levels of security vetting, which may involve providing additional documentation.

  • Experience coaching, reviewing or supporting other analysts through mentoring, feedback or collaborative working.
  • Practical experience using analytical and visualisation tools such as Power BI, Databricks, SQL, Alteryx or equivalent technologies.

Desirable

  • Experience working with modern cloud-based analytics platforms and data ecosystems.
  • Knowledge of semantic modelling, data products or scalable analytics architectures.
  • Exposure to advanced analytics, statistical techniques or predictive modelling.
  • Familiarity with data governance, analytical quality assurance or data ethics.
  • Experience working within a regulated or operationally complex environment.

Southern Water is at the forefront of transforming Britain’s water industry, investing significantly to enhance resilience, sustainability, and service excellence. With £7.8bn planned investment for 2025-30, this is an unparalleled opportunity to join a business committed to delivering a generational shift in the way water services are managed.

You will be joining at a time of significant change, working alongside a highly skilled leadership team with a clear vision for the future. We offer an environment where senior professionals can make a meaningful impact, influence major strategic decisions, and drive long-term value creation

At Southern Water, we believe diverse perspectives drive innovation. If you’re passionate about making a positive impact and think you can bring value to our team, we’d love to hear from you—even if you don’t tick every box. Your unique skills and experiences could be exactly what we need.

If this role isn’t quite what you’re looking for but are keen to be contacted about opportunities at Southern Water, you can register your details here: Introduce Yourself (myworkdaysite.com- Introduce Yourself)

Our Commitment to Diversity

We welcome applicants from all backgrounds, identities, and experiences. We do not discriminate based on race, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, age, disability, religion, or any other protected characteristic. If you need reasonable adjustments during the recruitment process, please let us know.

Additional Information

In line with Southern Water’s security requirements, successful candidates will be required to provide evidence of their identity, eligibility to work in the UK, criminal record check (DBS) and verification of their employment and/or education history for the past three years.

Appointment to this role is subject to the successful completion of all pre‑employment checks, including security vetting.

Please note that if a candidate does not meet the required security standards or fails to pass the vetting process, Southern Water reserves the right to withdraw the offer of employment. Some positions may also require higher levels of security vetting, which may involve providing additional documentation.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Murphy is recruiting for a Data Analyst delivering major water infrastructure projects in partnership with multiple clients across the UK.

We provide end-to-end services for major water and sewage companies such as Thames Water, Severn Trent Water, Northern Ireland Water, Yorkshire Water and United Utilities, and for several water-only companies including SES Water, Bristol Water and South East Water.

We also provide a full design, build, operate and maintain service for Uisce Éireann and we currently operate over 25 of their facilities, ensuring they run efficiently and comply fully with EPA regulations.

A day in the life of a Murphy Data Analyst:


Working closely with business reporting stakeholders to understanding reporting and analytical requirements to acquire and present relevant datasets.
Handle large and complex datasets from Primavera P6, SAP SuccessFactors and other key construction centric systems.
Verify the integrity of extracted data.
Awareness of, and support embedding and applying revised Data Governance and Information Security standards and best practices.
Applying Murphy best practices to code management, documentation and release.
Help establish good working practices for the work delivered.
Working as part of the Planning and Project Controls Function, alongside the Resource Management Team.
Project work to be allocated as required, during the duration of the project.
Liaising with planners, operational staff and other technical staff.
Assisting with the designing of maintenance procedures and putting them into operation.
Providing user training, support and feedback as required.
Build effective relationships with all relevant stakeholders.
Provide support and guidance to other team members.
Attend sites to assist remote application users as required.
Ad hoc activities as deemed appropriate by the Head of Planning.
Design and develop applications and workflows using POwer apps and Power Automate to streamline and automate business data processes.
Debugging, testing and troubleshooting systems and applications


Still interested, does this sound like you?


Detailed knowledge and practical experience with Primavera P6 and PowerBI, Power Automate, Power Apps, Dataverse
Experienced with connecting disparate data sources, particularly: Dataverse, Sharepoint, P6
Write and maintain python scripts for data processing, backend logic, API integration and workflow automation
Integrate python processes with Power Platforms solutions
Demonstrable experience with Visual Studio and Azure DevOps toolsets.
Working knowledge of ETL and data ingestion (API knowledge).
Solutions architecture
Understanding of relational databases and data modelling
Experience within construction and/or civil engineering processes and applications.
Happy to travel nationwide across multitude of Murphy projects


What’s in it for you?


27 days holiday, plus bank holidays with the option to buy an additional 2 days holiday each year, holidays increase with length of service
Discretionary annual bonus and annual salary review
Above market rate contributory pension scheme
Life assurance, health screening and enhanced sick pay
Enhanced maternity and paternity pay and a maternity returners bonus
Extra weeks holiday for all employees getting married and a wedding bonus
Subsidised canteen facilities in core locations
Dedicated and continued investment in your professional development
Other Murphy benefits include retail discounts and cashback, discounted gym memberships, cycle to work scheme etc


About Murphy

Murphy is formally recognised as a sector leading employer, Platinum Investors In People accredited along with reported operating profit up 57% on prior year. At Murphy, we are incredibly proud of our diverse workforce. Not only do our people represent over 60 different nationalities, but they also represent a wide range of backgrounds, ethnicities and ages. With a forward order book of £5.8bn and £23m Invested in environmentally friendly plant in 2024 there really is #MoretoMurphy

If you are unable to apply via the usual process, please call James Hing on 07353 134946 to discuss in more detail.

Murphy is unable to employ anyone who does not have the legal right to live and work in the UK

Title: Consultant, Advanced Analytics
Description:
Job Description:

Rakuten International is a division of Rakuten Group, Inc., a Japanese global technology leader in services that empower individuals, communities, businesses and society. Headquartered in San Mateo, California with more than 4,000 employees worldwide, the Rakuten International business portfolio includes market leaders in e-commerce, digital marketing, advertising, communications and entertainment. We create products and services that provide exceptional value by aligning members and the businesses that want to engage them in a shared community.

Rakuten Advertising provides advertising technology and consumer insights to the world’s leading brands and retailers. Working with agencies and brands around the world, Rakuten Advertising unites technology, client strategy and consumer insights to deliver advertising experiences that drive increased brand awareness and marketing performance. With access to Rakuten’s diverse media properties and audiences, combined with an award-winning performance network and proprietary consumer research, Rakuten Advertising creates the right conditions to reach new customers and sustain long-lasting loyalty.

Job Summary:

Responsible for the development and execution of analyses that address specific client issues or initiatives. This role proactively identifies trends and issues utilizing a comprehensive range of analytic techniques and tools, translating complex business questions into actionable analytical solutions. The Consultant liaises directly with client services, network development, and operational personnel, and engages directly with clients to ensure the appropriateness of underlying assumptions and interpretations, and to provide expert direction on client analytics solutions/services, both internally and externally.

Key Responsibilities:


Lead the development and delivery of client analytics and reporting activities, driving client relationship building, and managing the overall project lifecycle of externally focused analytic deliverables.
Independently develop and refine methodologies, tools, and templates to allow for the successful execution of analytic deliverables. Proactively identify opportunities for innovation and efficiency.
Support the ongoing development of the Advanced Analytics group capabilities by sharing innovative approaches and solutions with the broader team, and actively mentor junior team members.
Participate in various activities to support the growth of the Advanced Analytics group including:
Developing and delivering training content to Account Management and other internal functions.
Leading the development and maintenance of appropriate documentation regarding advanced analytic approaches, metric definitions, etc.
Proactively identify client business needs and translate them into robust analytical questions and solution designs.
Present complex analytical findings and strategic recommendations directly to clients, influencing their decision-making processes.

Specific tasks:


Client Insights & Solution Design:
Lead client discussions to define analytical objectives and scope, guiding them towards the most impactful solutions.
Design and execute advanced custom analyses leveraging internal tools/databases (e.g., benchmarking, publisher optimization, program performance analysis), delivering comprehensive insights and strategic recommendations.
Develop and optimize complex trend analyses and performance dashboards, ensuring they align with evolving client needs and provide actionable intelligence.
Data Quality & Operational Excellence:
Oversee and ensure the integrity and accuracy of recurring reports and data pipelines, proactively resolving complex data discrepancies.
Manage and adapt reporting structures to accommodate complex client requirements, including fiscal calendar challenges.
Investigate and resolve complex discrepancies between dashboards and manual reports, implementing long-term solutions for data consistency.
Strategic Support:
Independently assess and forecast sales potential for prospects based on comprehensive data analysis and market insights, providing strategic input to sales teams.
Contribute to the strategic development of new analytical products and services based on client needs and market trends.

Qualifications and Minimum Requirements:

Candidate will have at least 2 years of experience in analytics/reporting, with a strong focus on client-facing roles or significant project ownership. Successful candidates may bring experience from an agency, client, or consulting environment. Bachelor’s degree required.


Advanced Analytical Skills – Expert in analyzing and synthesizing complex information to understand issues, identify root causes, and propose optimal solutions. Demonstrated ability to independently translate ambiguous business requirements into precise analytical questions and comprehensive solution designs. Proven ability to utilize and expand prospective business models.
Technical Abilities - Proficiency in Microsoft Excel (including advanced functions, VBA), and strong experience with Business Intelligence (BI) tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI, MicroStrategy) for dashboard development and data visualization. Proficiency in SQL for data extraction and manipulation is required. Familiarity with Python or R for advanced analytics and automation is highly beneficial.
Client & Stakeholder Management – Proven ability to independently manage diverse client and internal stakeholder relationships, effectively communicating complex analytical concepts to both technical and non-technical audiences. Builds strong networks and acts as a trusted advisor.
Organizing and Planning - Demonstrated ability to independently plan, organize, and prioritize multiple complex projects, effectively balancing resources, skills, priorities, and timescales to achieve objectives.
Adaptability - Adaptable and proactive in responding to changing circumstances, effectively managing, solving problems, and providing innovative solutions in ambiguous environments.
Communicating Effectively – Written and verbal communication skills, with a proven ability to adjust messaging to fit diverse audiences and deliver compelling presentations that drive action.
Team Player – Proven ability to manage strong working relationships within the company, and actively contribute to team knowledge sharing, mentorship, and best practices.
Critical Thinking – Strong in using logic and reasoning to identify the strengths and weaknesses of alternative solutions, conclusions, or approaches to complex problems.
Plan for Contingencies – Proactively identifies potential problems and independently creates robust contingency plans or “work-arounds” to ensure project success.
Understanding the Business – Knows the mission-critical technical and functional skills needed; understands various types of business propositions and how businesses operate. Continuously learns new methods and technologies.


Five Principles for Success

Our worldwide practices describe specific behaviors that make Rakuten unique and united across the world. We expect Rakuten employees to model these 5 Shugi Principles of Success.

Always improve, Always Advance - Only be satisfied with complete success - Kaizen

Passionately Professional - Take an uncompromising approach to your work and be determined to be the best

Hypothesize - Practice - Validate – Shikumika - Use the Rakuten Cycle to succeed in unknown territory

Maximize Customer Satisfaction - The greatest satisfaction for our teams is seeing their customers smile

Speed!! Speed!! Speed!! - Always be conscious of time - take charge, set clear goals, and engage your team

Genuine interest in AI - ideally showcased by professional or side projects

Familiarity with cloud infrastructure (GCP preferred)
Experience working in Financial Services or consulting
Google Apps Script / Javascript
Working in small, fast-paced and scaling environments

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
At OQC, we aren't just theorising about the future; we're building it. Born from a philosophy of bold innovation, we've successfully transitioned quantum computing from an academic dream into a commercial reality. The most exciting thing is that we're just getting started and we've recently closed our £260 million Series C funding round - the largest fundraise ever completed by a quantum computing company in Europe.

The Purpose



As a Data Scientist at OQC, you'll help accelerate the development of next-generation quantum hardware through data-driven insight, predictive modelling, and simulation. Working at the intersection of science, engineering, and software, you'll transform complex datasets into actionable intelligence that shapes critical hardware decisions and drives technological progress.

The Role



We're looking for a Data Scientist to develop predictive models, analyse complex engineering datasets, and build the software tooling that enables informed decision-making across our hardware programmes. You'll collaborate closely with hardware, measurement, and software engineers to improve performance, optimise designs, and support the development of future quantum technologies.

What You'll Be Working On


Develop and optimise predictive models to support hardware design, specification, and engineering decision-making.
Analyse large, multi-parameter datasets to identify trends, quantify performance, and generate actionable insights.
Build, maintain, and improve software tools for data analysis, modelling, and simulation.
Apply software engineering best practices including version control, testing, code reviews, and documentation.
Collaborate with multidisciplinary engineering teams to define requirements and solve complex technical challenges.
Create clear visualisations, technical reports, and documentation to communicate findings and recommendations.
Identify opportunities to improve analytical workflows, modelling approaches, and data-driven processes across the business.
What We're Looking For


Bachelor's degree in Applied Mathematics, Statistics, Physics, Engineering, Computer Science, or a related discipline (or equivalent practical experience).
Experience applying statistical analysis and predictive modelling techniques to solve complex technical or business problems.
Proven ability to independently deliver data science projects or workstreams.
Experience developing software or analytical tools that support data-driven decision-making.
Strong experience working with large-scale datasets and data management infrastructures.
Strong proficiency in Python and scientific computing libraries including NumPy, SciPy, Pandas, and data visualisation tools.
Solid understanding of numerical methods, probability, and statistics.
Familiarity with machine learning techniques and large-scale data analysis.
Understanding of software engineering best practices including Git, code review, automated testing, and CI/CD.
Experience working with databases and object/blob storage technologies.
Strong communication skills with the ability to collaborate effectively across technical disciplines.
Understanding of data governance principles and best practices.
The 'Nice-to-Haves'


Postgraduate qualification in a relevant scientific, engineering, mathematical, or computing discipline.
Experience with scientific or systems modelling, including engineering, thermal, electromagnetic, or materials domains.
Experience with machine learning or deep learning frameworks.
Experience working within Agile software development environments.
Experience implementing automated testing frameworks or test-driven development practices.
Experience mentoring colleagues and engaging with a broad range of technical stakeholders.
Exposure to quantum technologies, radio-frequency electronics, thermodynamics, or systems engineering environments.
Ability to communicate complex technical concepts to non-technical audiences.
Why Join OQC



You will join a world-class team at the forefront of the next computational era. We offer a culture of bold innovation, the chance to work with unique lab infrastructure, and the opportunity to see your work redefine the limits of computation.

Learn more about our benefits and positive work culture here: https://oqc.tech/company/careers-at-oqc/

Title: Junior Business Planning Analyst
Description:
Company Background: Turtle Beach Corporation (www.turtlebeachcorp.com) is one of the world’s leading gaming accessory providers. The Company’s namesake Turtle Beach brand (www.turtlebeach.com) is known for designing best-selling gaming headsets, top-rated game controllers, award-winning PC gaming peripherals, and ground-breaking gaming simulation accessories. Innovation, first-to-market features, a broad range of products for all types of gamers, and top-rated customer support have made Turtle Beach a fan-favourite brand and the market leader in console gaming audio for over a decade. Turtle Beach Corporation acquired Performance Designed Products (www.pdp.com) in 2024. Turtle Beach’s shares are traded on the Nasdaq Exchange under the symbol: TBCH.




Turtle Beach believes diversity in the workplace creates an environment where different perspectives lead to improved creativity, productivity, team member engagement, and overall employee happiness. We’re simply looking for the best person for the job, and if that’s you…let’s talk!




Summary: As Junior Business Planning Analyst , you will work within the Business Planning team, covering the EMEA and APAC region, to enhance and standardize processes across demand planning, forecasting and reporting. This role is ideal for someone with a keen interest in data analytics and business planning and a desire to gain hands-on experience in developing comprehensive process flows, designing consistent templates, and improving the tracking, reporting, and forecasting of critical sales data that aligns with business needs and compliance requirements. You will assist in streamlining collaboration between multiple departments, ensuring seamless integration of processes across the regions and business teams; teams alongside preparation of forecasts, providing business intelligence, and validating business assumptions.




Duties and Responsibilities:

Demand Planning:

To drive accounts at a sell-through level where they are currently sell-in based.
Supporting smaller market account demand planning activities

Forecasting: Develop structured forecasting processes, create templates integrating historical data, and implement version control systems.

Reporting Insights and Optimisation:

Run and manage daily/weekly reports from customers, distributors, resellers and systems
Daily tableau and LOS refreshes
Sell-through insights, highlighting actual consumer demand
Process and optimize key reports to business and work with the senior team for ad-hoc requests

Market Data Reporting:

Review and Compile the external market data Turtle Beach subscribes to
Maintain existing data and report
EEBZ data maintenance

Cross-Departmental Integration & Documentation: Define and document process flows, develop templates for information sharing, implement centralized repositories, create flowcharts, refine templates, build dashboards, and develop training guides

AI Focus: Utilising AI to improve access to and analysis of data; improving output and efficiency




Education:

Bachelor’s Degree in Business Management, Operations, Finance, Statistics or Mathematics (preferred)

Process Improvement Experience: Ability to evaluate existing processes, recommend enhancements, and streamline operations.

Internal Networking: Partnering with the business units
Communication Skills: Clear documentation of processes, training users, and working collaboratively across multiple teams.
Attention to Detail: Ensuring data accuracy, consistency, and compliance across all tracking and reporting functions with the ability to meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.
Experience using analytics platforms Power BI, Tableau, and NetSuite is a plus.

Title: Lead Game Analyst - Old School RuneScape
Description:
Office location: Cambridge, UK - This position offers flexible working options, including predominantly remote work. However, applicants should be based (or willing to relocate) within a comfortable commuting distance of our office to attend onsite as required.

Are you a data-driven leader who wants to transform player behaviour into meaningful insights that shape the future of one of the world's most iconic MMORPGs?

As Lead Analyst on Old School RuneScape, you'll lead a team of analysts responsible for uncovering the insights that drive better player experiences, smarter product decisions, and long-term business success. Working closely with Product, Design, Engineering, and senior leadership, you'll help shape the roadmap of Old School RuneScape by turning complex data into clear, actionable recommendations.

This is a highly influential role where you'll combine technical expertise, analytical thinking, and leadership to ensure data sits at the heart of decision-making across one of gaming's most successful live service titles.

What you'll be doing:


Lead, mentor, and develop a team of Product Analysts, fostering a culture of curiosity, collaboration, and analytical excellence.
Build strong relationships with Old School RuneScape teams to develop a deep understanding of product strategy, player needs, and business priorities.
Deliver insights and business intelligence that directly influence product strategy, feature development, and roadmap planning.
Transform complex datasets into clear recommendations that support better decision-making across Product, Design, and Leadership.
Query, analyse, and interpret large datasets to uncover opportunities to improve player engagement, retention, and overall game experience.
Partner closely with Engineering and Design teams to develop and enhance game telemetry and data collection.
Collaborate with central Data teams to define data requirements and contribute to the evolution of shared data products.
Develop deep knowledge of Old School RuneScape's gameplay systems, mechanics, and player behaviours to provide meaningful context to analysis.
Champion data-driven thinking across the game team and promote best practices in analytics and reporting.


What we're looking for:


Experience leading or mentoring analysts within an analytics, business intelligence, or data-focused environment.
Strong SQL skills and experience working with large datasets and data cleansing techniques.
A demonstrable understanding of video game player behaviours, particularly in MMORPGs.
Familiarity with modern data platforms and architectures such as AWS, Snowflake, and OLAP technologies.
Experience using data visualisation tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or similar.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to communicate complex findings clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Excellent stakeholder management skills and the ability to influence decision-making across multiple disciplines.


What we offer:

When you join Jagex you can look forward to a generous Perks & Benefits package including:


Private Healthcare, including Dental Plan.
Minimum 6% Pension contributions.
Employee Assistance Programme & onsite Counselling.
Life Insurance.
Discretionary annual performance bonus.
Enhanced family leave policies from day 1.
Flexible working hours.
25 days annual leave + Bank holidays & the option to buy/sell holidays + so much more!


Inclusion & Accessibility Statement

We are committed to providing equal opportunities and creating an environment where everyone can thrive. We welcome applications from all backgrounds, and we recruit, develop, and promote based on merit and ability.

If you require any reasonable adjustments to support you during the recruitment process, please let us know when you're invited to interview.

About Jagex:

We are Jagex: The RuneScape Company- home to one of gaming's most enduring success stories. We create deep, community-powered games with worlds that evolve, inspire, and endure. Our flagship MMORPG franchise, RuneScape has welcomed over 300 million player accounts, inspiring generations of players across PC and mobile for 25 years.

As we begin our 26th year, we are accelerating our strategy to expand RuneScape into a broader ecosystem of forever games and connected experiences, grounded in a renewed commitment to integrity and fair play. This includes the reduction of monetisation mechanics, as well as the launch of RuneScape: DragonWilds into Steam Early Access in 2025 representing a major step in building a multi-genre future for the RuneScape universe. We remain focused on building enduring worlds, empowering player-led innovation, and laying the foundations for the boldest era in RuneScape's history. For more information, visit www.jagex.com.

Headquartered in Cambridge, United Kingdom, We are home to passionate creators, storytellers, engineers, and even a few dreamers, all united by a single mission- to shape the future of online worlds and empower players to make them their own. We're looking for people to join us who share our ambitions & values - We foster an inclusive workplace built on trust, autonomy, and a shared passion for creating experiences which inspire our communities. Our values guide everything we do: We Embrace Fellowship by collaborating and sharing openly. We Hone our Craft by always learning and improving; We Honour our Vows by being honest and transparent; We are our Customers' Heroes by striving to understand our team & player needs, and We Seize The Day by making the most of every opportunity.

Join us. The next era of RuneScape - and the next chapter of Jagex: The RuneScape Company - starts now.

We are committed to providing equal opportunities and creating an environment where everyone can thrive. We welcome applications from all backgrounds, and we recruit, develop, and promote based on merit and ability.

If you require any reasonable adjustments to support you during the recruitment process, please let us know when you're invited to interview.

Title: DEMAND ANALYST CITRUS UK
Description:
Who We Are

We are Fyffes, one of the world's leading tropical produce companies. For more than 130 years, we have sourced, shipped and delivered high-quality fresh produce globally. With over 4,000 employees and a strong international network of growers and suppliers, we are committed to shaping wellbeing for the world through sustainable growth, innovation and strong partnerships.

Now we're looking for a Demand Analyst Citrus UK to join our team, turning UK citrus demand into accurate forecasts, insight and planning support.

Here's What You Will Do

Support the commercial team by analysing UK citrus demand and translating orders, forecast trends, promotional activity, seasonality and market dynamics into actionable planning insight. Your work will support service, availability, waste reduction and weekly and seasonal planning.

You will connect demand with sourcing, supply chain and operational teams, providing clear analysis, early risk visibility and performance reporting. The role is analytical and detail-driven, with a strong focus on forecast accuracy, demand visibility and planning discipline.

Some Key Responsibilities


Analyse UK citrus demand using orders, historical sales, promotions, seasonality, weather impacts and market trends.
Prepare and maintain accurate weekly and seasonal citrus forecasts with clearly documented assumptions.
Track forecast versus actual demand and order movements, identifying variances, trends and root causes.
Produce demand, forecast, order and service reporting covering accuracy, bias, fill rate, availability, waste and key service drivers.
Support demand reviews, updates and planning inputs, providing clear insight for internal alignment.
Identify supply-demand gaps, shortfalls, oversupply, timing risks and demand changes early.
Support scenario analysis, mitigation planning, allocation discussions and improvements in reporting, communication and forecasting processes.


What You Need To Bring


Degree in Supply Chain, Business, Economics, Data Analytics, Agriculture or a related field.
1-3 years of experience in demand analysis, demand planning, supply chain, commercial analysis, fresh produce or FMCG.
Experience with retail demand, forecasts, orders, promotions or seasonal planning is advantageous.
Fresh produce, short shelf-life supply chain or citrus experience is beneficial.
Strong Excel and data analysis capability, with ability to translate large datasets into clear insight.
Fluent English; Spanish or another language is advantageous.
Strong attention to detail, proactive communication, structured problem-solving, ownership and ability to work in a fast-paced perishable product environment.


What It Is Like To Work At Fyffes

We are ambitious but grounded, combining high performance with a people-first culture. Our leadership principles guide us every day: we play to win as one Fyffes, shape the future, cultivate a growth mindset, are transparent and inclusive, and are moved by doing good. We thrive on diversity and treat all candidates equally.

Interested? We look forward to hearing from you!

Title: Customer Analyst
Description:
hackajob is collaborating with Dominos Pizza UK & Ireland to connect them with exceptional professionals for this role.

About The Role

Customer Analytics & Insights Analyst – Help us deliver for our customers, every day

At Domino’s, you bring the flavour. Together, we deliver delicious.

We’re looking for a Customer Analytics & Insights Analyst to help us better understand our customers, how they order, what they value, and where we can improve. Working as part of our Digital (Marketing) team, you’ll turn data into clear, practical insights that help shape campaigns, improve performance and support smarter decision-making across the business

What You’ll Bring


This role is perfect if you enjoy working with data to solve real business problems and want to build your career in customer analytics. You’ll be based in Manchester or Milton Keynes, working closely with marketing and digital teams to turn customer behaviour into meaningful insight.
Experience working with data in an analytics, data science or insights role, ideally within a retail or e-commerce environment
Strong SQL skills, with the ability to extract, manipulate and analyse large datasets
Experience with customer analytics, including segmentation (e.g. RF/RFM) and lifecycle analysis
Experience using reporting or visualisation tools (e.g. Excel, Power BI, Tableau, Python or R)


What You’ll Be Doing


In this role, you’ll help us understand what our customers are doing and why and turn that into insight teams can act on. You’ll work closely with marketing, CRM and commercial teams, supporting campaigns and helping improve how we connect with customers.
Analyse customer behaviour, performance and segmentation to identify trends, opportunities and risks
Support campaign analysis and recommend ways to improve targeting, engagement and customer value
Build and maintain reports, dashboards and datasets to support ongoing insight and decision-making
Work closely with marketing and digital teams to provide clear, structured insight that informs strategy
Identify ways to improve reporting, data quality and analytical approaches over time


How We Will Support You


A clear, confidence-building induction so you understand your role and how your work connects to the wider business
Support from your manager and team, with people who will help you build your skills and grow your confidence in analytics
Opportunities to develop your technical and commercial understanding, with clear progression pathways within the team
Recognition for great work, both in day-to-day moments and through wider company initiatives


About Us

Domino's UK & Ireland

Who are we? A forward-thinking, inclusive world's leading pizza company:

Domino's is the world's leading pizza company, with over 1,300 stores across the UK and Ireland. We're committed to using the freshest, highest-quality ingredients to create over 110 million delectable pizzas each year. Plus, we're a tech-savvy and data driven bunch – 90% of our system sales come through digital channels and 75% of our digital orders coming via the app we have 9.5 million active app customers!

Our mission? To be the go-to choice for food delivery and collection by crafting hot, freshly made pizzas that earn us accolades and the loyalty of millions of pizza enthusiasts worldwide. We're all about innovation, growth, and a commitment to excellence.

At Domino's, we embrace the power of diversity and foster an inclusive environment where everyone feels valued, respected, and empowered. We celebrate the uniqueness of each individual, regardless of their identity, background, or any other characteristic. When you join our team, you join a family that welcomes and celebrates authenticity!

Our recipe for success? Our DomiDNA:


We Mean Business
We Always Deliver
We’re Open to New Flavours
We’re Tastier Together


If you share our values and are ready to be part of a world-class team that's as passionate about people as we are about pizza, apply now and join the Domino's UK & Ireland family!

Title: APP Data Analyst
Description:
Job description:

INTRODUCTION

At Burberry, we believe creativity opens spaces. Our purpose is to unlock the power of imagination to push boundaries and open new possibilities for our people, our customers and our communities. This is the core belief that has guided Burberry since it was founded in 1856 and is central to how we operate as a company today.

We aim to provide an environment for creative minds from different backgrounds to thrive, bringing a wide range of skills and experiences to everything we do. As a purposeful, values-driven brand, we are committed to being a force for good in the world as well, creating the next generation of sustainable luxury for customers, driving industry change and championing our communities.

JOB PURPOSE

To provide data analytics and gap analysis of key areas of compliance in the business that would leave us exposed from a risk perspective if not highlighted. This reporting is to be provided across multiple departments within the business.

RESPONSIBILITIES

 

Maintain, run and improve automated KPI and trend analysis reporting and flagging relevant issues to key stakeholders.
Continue to build a suite of reports to highlight exceptions and/or breaches in key areas utilising both SAP and IntelliQ.
Supporting regional APP with evidence gathering during investigations.
System Maintenance to include: monitoring daily data loads and clearing of load errors; weekly gap analysis; regular updates of key data streams from HR, product and finance to ensure reporting is accurate and up to date. Troubleshooting as required to fix issues.
Continue to build a Sharepoint site for the APP team to provide training materials and support for using the system. 
Maintenance of the APP (Asset and Profit Protection) Operations database to include keeping store master and HR data up to date.
Supporting the wider regional team with a growing list of daily, weekly and monthly reports including :
Store KPI Reporting, Adjustment Reporting, Incident Reporting, Digital Fraud Reporting, AML Reporting, Retail Operations Exception Reporting.
Support the team with biannual stock loss reporting to include data cleansing, performing analysis on required areas of concerns and creating store level posters.
Provide ad hoc support to Digital Fraud team with chargebacks.
PERSONAL PROFILE

Essential

Previous Experience with IntelliQ or other data mining systems (This is a mandatory requirement for the role)
Advanced Office 365 Suite, in particular Excel
Data minded
Experience in retail fraud prevention 
Retail chargeback processing
Experience with SQL
Experience with Microsoft Access and Power BI
Previous experience with SAP
Experience using Power Platform
MEASURES OF SUCCESS FOOTER

Burberry is an Equal Opportunities Employer and as such, treats all applications equally and recruits purely on the basis of skills and experience.

 

Posting Notes: United Kingdom || Not Applicable || London || CORPORATE AFFAIRS || CORPORATE - ASSET & PROFIT PROTECTION || n/a ||

INTRODUCTION

At Burberry, we believe creativity opens spaces. Our purpose is to unlock the power of imagination to push boundaries and open new possibilities for our people, our customers and our communities. This is the core belief that has guided Burberry since it was founded in 1856 and is central to how we operate as a company today.

We aim to provide an environment for creative minds from different backgrounds to thrive, bringing a wide range of skills and experiences to everything we do. As a purposeful, values-driven brand, we are committed to being a force for good in the world as well, creating the next generation of sustainable luxury for customers, driving industry change and championing our communities.

JOB PURPOSE

To provide data analytics and gap analysis of key areas of compliance in the business that would leave us exposed from a risk perspective if not highlighted. This reporting is to be provided across multiple departments within the business.

RESPONSIBILITIES

 

  • Maintain, run and improve automated KPI and trend analysis reporting and flagging relevant issues to key stakeholders.
  • Continue to build a suite of reports to highlight exceptions and/or breaches in key areas utilising both SAP and IntelliQ.
  • Supporting regional APP with evidence gathering during investigations.
  • System Maintenance to include: monitoring daily data loads and clearing of load errors; weekly gap analysis; regular updates of key data streams from HR, product and finance to ensure reporting is accurate and up to date. Troubleshooting as required to fix issues.
  • Continue to build a Sharepoint site for the APP team to provide training materials and support for using the system. 
  • Maintenance of the APP (Asset and Profit Protection) Operations database to include keeping store master and HR data up to date.
  • Supporting the wider regional team with a growing list of daily, weekly and monthly reports including :
  • Store KPI Reporting, Adjustment Reporting, Incident Reporting, Digital Fraud Reporting, AML Reporting, Retail Operations Exception Reporting.
  • Support the team with biannual stock loss reporting to include data cleansing, performing analysis on required areas of concerns and creating store level posters.
  • Provide ad hoc support to Digital Fraud team with chargebacks.
PERSONAL PROFILE

Essential

  • Previous Experience with IntelliQ or other data mining systems (This is a mandatory requirement for the role)
  • Advanced Office 365 Suite, in particular Excel
  • Data minded
  • Experience in retail fraud prevention 
  • Retail chargeback processing
  • Experience with SQL
  • Experience with Microsoft Access and Power BI
  • Previous experience with SAP
  • Experience using Power Platform
MEASURES OF SUCCESS FOOTER

Burberry is an Equal Opportunities Employer and as such, treats all applications equally and recruits purely on the basis of skills and experience.

 

Posting Notes: United Kingdom || Not Applicable || London || CORPORATE AFFAIRS || CORPORATE - ASSET & PROFIT PROTECTION || n/a ||

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
WE ARE LANDSEC




We identify and shape places that create opportunity, enhance quality of life, and bring joy to the people connected to them.




This is how we’ve created the UK’s leading portfolio of urban places and one of the largest real estate companies in Europe.




Our £10 billion portfolio is built around premium workplaces, the country’s pre-eminent retail platform, and a residential pipeline that will redefine urban life.




We’ve honed this ability over 80 years. Spotting the opportunities, building the partnerships, and continually adapting to shape the places that meet the needs of a changing world.




Places where life happens. Where businesses grow. And where cities are defined.




LOCATION




This role is based at Victoria, London and offers hybrid working (3 days in the office).




THE TEAM




It’s an exciting time at Landsec for our Data Team. We have a lot to achieve in order to enable the business to achieve its over-arching strategy and objectives – using data as a foundation for the decisions our leaders make. It’s vital that our team has people with the right skills, experience and capability to do that – covering Data Science, Data Analytics, Data Engineering, Data Architecture, Data Operations and Data Governance.




THE ROLE




We are looking for Data Analysts to work with key stakeholders to ensure that our data and analytics products and capabilities drive and inform the business strategies across Landsec. You will work with all manner of data, including rental income, logistics and operational costs, market research, profit margins, finance operations and brand partner sales.




The successful data analyst will possess an interdisciplinary set of skills across data modelling, data management, data visualisation and data science/statistical analysis. You will have strong hands on skills in SQL and modern data visualisation tools, with the ability to build robust data models and transform complex datasets into clear, actionable insights.




You will have the communication skills required to understand business requirements and translate and present data insights to technical and non-technical stakeholders. You will build consensus around a common purpose, actively listen, persuade and network with key stakeholders. You will be curious, with an impulse to seek new information, explore how Landsec works and discover new ways to drive Landsec’s business strategy with data and analytics.




Salary: Up to £50,000 per annum




PRINCIPLE ACCOUNTABILITIES




Building and Embedding Data and Analytics Products in a Commercial Environment

Building data and analytics products (reports, dashboards, visualisations, analysis, data science) and working with multiple internal / external stakeholders to drive business adoption and performance.
Quickly build internal (business) and external (market) knowledge and assess new data and analytics opportunities (gathering ideas, research, carrying out analysis, refining and prioritising new requirements)
Analyse and translate business needs into data models, ensuring that they meet the data standards required for these to be a reusable and long-term data asset for Landsec
Measure impact, success and adoption using relevant metrics/KPIs, and use this to inform future enhancements of the data and analytics products
Understand governance and can participate in processes that enable good data management and quality practices. Can communicate and improve data management practices in the work being undertaken.




Working in Matrix Organisations and Influencing Stakeholders

Work with stakeholders across Landsec to ensure that data and analytics products drive performance in accordance to the business strategy, and managing expectations accordingly
Work with Director, Analytics, Data Science and Data Governance, to ensure that business value is maximised from the data and analytics products created
Provide data and analytics expertise and collaborate with Landsec internal teams to enable them to adopt data and analytics products
Communicate with stakeholders and share actionable insights through a variety of channels, including data visualisations, to articulate business value and drive business decision making




Problem Solving in a VUCA Environment

Analyse, prototype and validate work around a minimum viable product (MVP) for any new Analytics product/ feature.
Identify, analyse and interpret trends or patterns in complex data sets, and recognise where additional data is required to solve a problem
Identify opportunities to use data science to glean further business value and insights from the data model
Work in sprint/release planning sessions to deliver prioritised use cases and deliver maximum business value with the least effort
Collaborate with Data Engineers to productionise data and analytics products




ESSENTIAL CRITERIA

Proven experience as a Data Analyst (e.g., database management, data mining, analysis, reporting, data science)
Proven hands-on experience in data analysis using a programming language (e.g., Python, R)
Understanding of data modelling fundamentals (e.g. fact/dimension tables, relationships, star schema)
Experience creating or managing databases (e.g., SQL)
Expert knowledge of spreadsheet tools (e.g., Microsoft Excel, Google Sheets)
Sound knowledge of data visualisation software (e.g., PowerBI, Qlik, Tableau)
Strong communication skills to collaborate with stakeholders and articulate the value of data analysis
Team-working
Undergraduate degree in Data Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Engineering, Computer Science, Economics, Operational Research, or similar quantitative subjects (Masters degree preferred)




Life and Benefits at Landsec

We have a great benefits package, there to support you with your work-life balance and in moments that matter. We offer fantastic Learning and Development opportunities to help you grow and reach your potential and we also play our part in supporting the communities where we live and work.




Below are some of our core benefits, make sure to visit our My Total Reward page for more information.

Performance based annual bonus plan
25 days annual leave (plus bank holidays) rising to 27 days after 2 years and the option to purchase up to 10 additional days each year. Enjoy an additional three-day December festive break on top of your leave and a special ‘My Day’ to take off for whatever you choose.
Enhanced pension contributions – Landsec will contribute up to 10.5% subject to your contribution.
Private medical insurance, Health Cash Plan, Life assurance and income protection.
Enhanced maternity, adoption, shared parental leave policies (view our Family policies page for more details)
Two different share plans enabling you to share in the success you will help to build
Up to 4 working days to volunteer per year and 'Give As You Earn' programmes allowing you to donate to charities straight out of your pay and we top up by 20%




And when it comes to your health and wellbeing you will also have access to other benefits: our employee assistance programme, digital GP, cycle to work scheme, a mindfulness app and a health app supporting fertility and menopause.
