Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Established in 2004, OLIVER is the world’s first and only specialist in designing, building, and running bespoke in-house agencies and marketing ecosystems for brands. We partner with over 300 clients in 40+ countries and counting. Our unique model drives creativity and efficiency, allowing us to deliver tailored solutions that resonate deeply with audiences. 

As a part of The Brandtech Group, we're at the forefront of leveraging cutting-edge AI technology to revolutionise how we create and deliver work. Our AI solutions enhance efficiency, spark creativity, and drive insightful decision-making, empowering our teams to produce innovative and impactful results. 

Data Analyst

Location: Weybridge, Surrey, United Kingdom

 

Established in 2004, Inside Ideas Group is a rapidly growing global agency network that helps clients drive their businesses forward from the inside. We partner with over 300 clients in more than 40 countries, delivering ideas, technology and creativity that make a measurable impact. 

As part of The Brandtech Group, we are pioneers in using advanced AI technologies to transform the way we create, optimise and deliver work. Our AI capabilities enhance efficiency, support smart decision making and unlock new levels of creativity for our teams and clients. 

We are now looking for a skilled Data Analyst to join our onsite team supporting in London. 

About the Role 

As the Data Analyst, you will be responsible for delivering insight that drives improvements across the full commercial ecosystem spanning customer experience on, retailer investment effectiveness, marketing and sales performance, and the broader post-purchase, onboarding and promotional landscape. 

A core part of your remit will be the design, build and ongoing management of a marketing and sales performance dashboard, underpinned by Google BigQuery and delivered in Looker Studio. This will serve as a critical decision-making tool for marketing, e-commerce, retail and CX teams surfacing insight such as campaign uplift, halo effects, retailer investment impact and share performance across products and markets. 

Alongside this, you will analyse customer journeys, benchmark competitors, oversee content performance and support the planning and execution of onboarding and e-commerce promotions using platforms like GWI to create digital personas and audience profiles. 

You will collaborate closely with CX, marketing, product and e-commerce teams as well as HQ and local market stakeholders to ensure insight informs decisions at every stage. 

This role requires strong analytical capability, expertise in data modelling and SQL, and the ability to translate complex commercial questions into dashboard logic and actionable recommendations. 

What You Will Be Doing 

Marketing & Sales Performance Analytics  

Design, build and maintain a marketing and sales performance dashboard in Looker Studio, underpinned by BigQuery, combining sell-out sales data, market volume and share data, and campaign activity logs. 
Define the underlying data model, transform source files and design the reporting layer to support consistent, scalable KPI delivery. 
Identify and quantify the impact of retailer investment, including uplift in sales and market share during campaign periods. 
Analyse correlations between campaign activity and commercial performance to determine which marketing levers are most effective. 
Measure and report on halo effects across products and retailers resulting from promotional and campaign activity. 
Define and calculate key performance metrics including uplift, baseline, market share, activity intensity and halo effects. 
Work with GFK or other syndicated market data sources to enrich performance analysis with market volume and share context. 

Customer Experience & Digital Analytics 

Conduct analysis of the current customer experience, including user behaviour, content and campaign performance and conversion pathways. 
Benchmark key customer journeys against competitor websites to identify gaps and opportunities. 
Provide regular insight into paid promotions, post-purchase experience and customer satisfaction trends. 
Support planning, optimisation and operational management of post-purchase and onboarding experience. 
Analyse content utilisation, engagement and drop-off points, and recommend enhancements. 
Collaborate with HQ and local teams on onboarding content production and implementation. 
Support feature management including evaluation, testing performance and monitoring adoption, with ongoing analysis of content effectiveness and customer experience updates. 
Reporting, Dashboards & Stakeholder Insight 
Build and maintain dashboards, reports and ad hoc analyses across customer experience, onboarding, e-commerce, retail performance and promotion activity. 
Translate data into clear insights that guide decision-making within marketing, CX, digital and commercial teams. 
Present findings clearly to stakeholders at all levels, ensuring insight is embedded in planning and operational processes. 
Use platforms like GWI to build digital personas and audience profiles that support broader marketing planning. 

What You Need to Be Great in This Role 

Essential 

Proven experience as a data or insight analyst within digital, e-commerce, retail, marketing or agency environments. 
Strong proficiency in Google BigQuery and advanced SQL, including data modelling and reporting layer design. 
Hands-on experience building dashboards in Looker Studio, with a clear understanding of how to structure underlying data models for robust, scalable dashboard performance. 
Demonstrable experience combining sell-out sales, market share and campaign activity datasets to generate commercial insight. 
Ability to define and calculate metrics including uplift, baseline, market share, activity intensity and halo effects. 
Strong marketing analytics capability, including campaign effectiveness measurement across paid and promotional channels. 
Good understanding of digital customer journeys, content performance metrics and e-commerce KPIs. 
Experience with analytics platforms such as Google Analytics GA4, Adobe Analytics or similar. 
Excellent communication and storytelling ability — capable of translating complex business questions into dashboard logic and presenting findings clearly to non-technical audiences. 
Experience working with multi-market teams and managing stakeholder expectations across business functions. 
A passion for data-driven decision-making and an interest in how AI can enhance analytics workflows. 

Preferred: 

Proficiency in Python (pandas) for data transformation, automation and workflow scripting. 
Broader experience with Google Cloud Platform (GCP), including automating data ingestion and transformation workflows. 
Experience working in retail, e-commerce or retailer media environments. 
Familiarity with GfK or other syndicated market data sources. 
Experience with additional BI tools such as Tableau or Power BI. 

Our Values 

Be Ambitious to succeed Be Imaginative to push the boundaries of what's possible Be Inspirational to do groundbreaking work Be always learning and listening to understand Be Results-focused to exceed expectations Be actively pro-inclusive and anti-racist across our community, clients and creations 

INSIDE IDEAS GROUP, a part of the Brandtech Group, is an equal opportunity employer committed to creating an inclusive working environment where all employees are encouraged to reach their full potential, and individual differences are valued and respected. All applicants shall be considered for employment without regard to race, ethnicity, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, neurodivergence, disability status, or any other characteristic protected by local laws. 

INSIDE IDEAS GROUP has set ambitious environmental goals around sustainability, with science-based emissions reduction targets. Collectively, we work towards our mission, embedding sustainability into every department and through every stage of the project lifecycle. 

Our values shape everything we do: 

Be Ambitious to succeed   

Be Imaginative to push the boundaries of what’s possible   

Be Inspirational to do groundbreaking work   

Be always learning and listening to understand   

Be Results-focused  to exceed expectations   

Be actively pro-inclusive and anti-racist across our community, clients and creations

All applicants shall be considered for employment without regard to race, ethnicity, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, neurodivergence, disability status, or any other characteristic protected by local laws.   

 OLIVER has set ambitious environmental goals around sustainability, with science-based emissions reduction targets. Collectively, we work towards our mission, embedding sustainability into every department and through every stage of the project lifecycle.

Title: Salesforce Reporting Analyst
Description:
About the company

We are currently partnered with a leading UK business who provide a bespoke service across the entire country. As the UK's go to in their industry, they have thousands of customers and specialists to provide them with the service they need. They are invested in Salesforce and are looking for a data and reporting specialist to join their team who has excellent experience using PowerBI.







About the role

As a business, they are built upon two core platforms; Salesforce and Oracle, and with customer and business data spread across both systems, they need a reporting analyst with experience on both platforms to unify the data in PowerBI to create clear insights for the business to use. You will work within a team that has been built in-house to focus on the data and reporting elements, and you will play an important part in delivering actionable insights for the business.




Responsibilities

Building and enhancing reports and dashboards that drive operational, tactical, and strategic decision
Partnering with stakeholders to translate business needs into actionable insight
Ensuring accuracy and alignment across Salesforce, Oracle, Power BI and Snowflake output
Championing data quality, governance, and KPI consistency across the organisation
Acting as a subject matter expert in reporting best practice
Driving adoption of reporting by delivering training, guidance, and user support
Identifying opportunities to automate and improve reporting efficiency




Experience/Requirements

Proven experience building reports and dashboards in Salesforce is required
Strong understanding of data models, relationships, and reporting structures
Experience with enterprise systems (ideally Salesforce & Oracle / CRM & ERP)
Ability to engage stakeholders and translate complex requirements into clear reporting solutions




What's on offer?

Flexible hybrid working
Company bonus
Great pension
25 days holiday + Bank holidays
And more...

As a business, they are built upon two core platforms; Salesforce and Oracle, and with customer and business data spread across both systems, they need a reporting analyst with experience on both platforms to unify the data in PowerBI to create clear insights for the business to use. You will work within a team that has been built in-house to focus on the data and reporting elements, and you will play an important part in delivering actionable insights for the business.


Responsibilities

  • Building and enhancing reports and dashboards that drive operational, tactical, and strategic decision
  • Partnering with stakeholders to translate business needs into actionable insight
  • Ensuring accuracy and alignment across Salesforce, Oracle, Power BI and Snowflake output
  • Championing data quality, governance, and KPI consistency across the organisation
  • Acting as a subject matter expert in reporting best practice
  • Driving adoption of reporting by delivering training, guidance, and user support
  • Identifying opportunities to automate and improve reporting efficiency


Experience/Requirements

  • Proven experience building reports and dashboards in Salesforce is required
  • Strong understanding of data models, relationships, and reporting structures
  • Experience with enterprise systems (ideally Salesforce & Oracle / CRM & ERP)
  • Ability to engage stakeholders and translate complex requirements into clear reporting solutions


What's on offer?

  • Flexible hybrid working
  • Company bonus
  • Great pension
  • 25 days holiday + Bank holidays
  • And more...

Title: Data Scientist - Customer Focus
Description:
Your role

As a Data Scientist - Customer Focus, your mission is to turn complex data into practical insights and predictive solutions that help improve equipment performance, reduce downtime, and support better operational decision-making. In this role, you will work closely with customers, account teams, and technical specialists, giving you the chance to see the direct impact of your work in real operational environments while building experience at the intersection of technology, customer collaboration and business value.

This is an exciting opportunity to work at the forefront of data science and industrial innovation, applying advanced analytics to solve real customer challenges on a global scale.

You will


Explore, prepare, and deliver data analyses, predictive models, and reporting that create measurable value for global customers.
Work closely with customers and account teams to rapidly iterate on solutions using real data, feedback, and operational constraints.
Support the integration of analytics outputs into customer workflows, helping ensure solutions are practical, reliable, and adopted by users.
Investigate deployment or operational issues and translate feedback into technical improvements.
Present insights and recommendations clearly through reports, dashboards, workshops, and customer presentations.
Support pre-sales and delivery activity through demos, business reviews, and occasional on-site customer engagement.
Collaborate with Data Engineering, DevOps, and R&D teams to deploy, validate, and scale analytics solutions.
Contribute to the continuous improvement of models, data workflows, and delivery processes.


To succeed, you will need

We encourage you to apply even if you don't meet every single requirement. We value diverse experiences and perspectives and are excited to see what you bring to the role.

You have 2+ years of experience, working as a Data Scientist, Analytics Specialist, or in a similar role.


A degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Physics, Chemistry, or a related discipline.
Strong practical Python skills and experience with common data science libraries such as pandas, numpy, scipy, statsmodels, or scikit-learn.
The ability to turn data into clear, actionable insights for both technical and non-technical audiences.
Experience working with predictive modelling, ideally with time-series, sensor, manufacturing, or operational data.
Familiarity with cloud platforms such as Azure and an understanding of APIs or production environments.
Confidence working in customer-facing or consulting-style environments, with strong communication and collaboration skills.


In return, we offer


Culture of trust and accountability
Lifelong learning and career growth
Innovation powered by people
Comprehensive compensation and benefits
Health and well-being


Job location

This role offers a hybrid or remote working arrangement, there may be some travel required as part of the role.

Contact information

Talent Acquisition Team: Brett Hornby

About Edwards

Edwards is a leading developer and manufacturer of sophisticated vacuum products, exhaust management systems and related value-added services. Edwards solutions are integral to manufacturing processes for semiconductors, flat panel displays, LEDs and solar cells. They are also used within an increasingly diverse range of industrial processes including power, glass and other coating applications; steel and other metallurgy; pharmaceutical and chemical; and for scientific instruments in a wide range of R&D applications.

Edwards has over 4,000 employees worldwide engaged in the design, manufacture and support of high technology vacuum and exhaust management equipment. Edwards has state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities in Europe, Asia and North America.

Title: Data Analytics Analyst
Description:
Data & Analytics Analyst

Location: Maidenhead (Hybrid)

Full-time | Permanent

Here at Dignity Funerals, we're looking for a Data & Analytics Analyst who is passionate about data and driven to make an impact. This is a hands-on role where you’ll play a key part in shaping how data is used across our Legal Services and wider business—turning complex datasets into clear, actionable insight.

If you thrive in a fast-paced environment, enjoy ownership, and love bringing data to life through dashboards, this could be the role for you.

What You’ll Be Doing


Deliver business-critical reporting including weekly trading packs and month-end cycles
Build, maintain and enhance Tableau dashboards and reporting systems
Own the end-to-end data pipeline—from raw extraction to polished outputs
Bridge and streamline reporting between Legal Services and core business systems
Investigate and resolve data discrepancies across multiple sources
Respond to ad hoc analysis requests from senior stakeholders
Improve and document processes to drive efficiency and consistency
Support the transition from legacy reporting to modern, aligned data solutions



What Success Looks Like


Accurate, timely, reliable reporting that the business depends on
High-performing dashboards with minimal downtime
Clean, consistent master data and reporting structures
Continuous improvements in reporting efficiency and data quality



About You

You’re someone who:


Loves working with data and turning it into meaningful insight
Has a sharp eye for detail and takes pride in accuracy
Enjoys variety—from dashboards to deep-dive analysis
Communicates confidently with both technical and non-technical audiences
Is proactive, collaborative and always looking for smarter ways to work



What You’ll Bring


3+ years’ experience in data analytics or reporting
Strong experience with Tableau (dashboard development & maintenance)
Hands-on experience with BigQuery and dbt (SSMS is a bonus)
Solid understanding of data modelling and data quality
Experience working across multiple data sources and systems
Exposure to master data management and reporting hierarchies
Degree in a relevant field (or equivalent practical experience)
Experience in an FCA-regulated environment (desirable)



If you're ready to embark on a rewarding career where you can make a positive impact every day, we want to hear from you!

What are the next steps?

To be considered for this role, please submit your application via the ‘apply’ button, where a member of our Talent Team will review your application.

About Us

We are Dignity, one of the UK’s oldest and most trusted funeral providers, with over 200 years of history, 570 branches, and 46 crematoria. From launching the UK’s first funeral plan to helping shape modern regulation, we have led the way in supporting families with care, compassion, and professionalism.

Now we are building the UK’s leading end of life company. As part of the Dignity Group, we also operate Farewill, the country’s largest will writer and one of the UK’s top probate providers, giving families support not just at the funeral, but before and after as well.

Today we are over 4,000 people across the UK, all driven by the same goal: to care for families with compassion and make the UK a world leader in end of life care. Here to help, whenever you need.

Title: Data and Reporting Analyst
Description:
Full Time, Permanent

Package: £29,000 - £32,000 per annum + annual company bonus

Working Hours: Monday to Friday, 35 hours per week

Location: Winnersh, (RG41 5TP) - office based contract with flexibility




Are you a Power BI enthusiast who loves turning raw retail data into actionable commercial strategies?




At Smollan, we are the driving force behind some of the world’s biggest brands. We’re seeking a sharp, analytical Data & Reporting Analyst, expert with Power BI to join our Data & Insights team.

In this role, you won't just be managing and storing sales data, you will be the bridge between complex data warehouses and real-world retail strategy. If you love transforming dense datasets into slick, automated Power BI dashboards that help management teams make brilliant commercial decisions, we want to hear from you.




What You'll Be Doing




Power BI & Visualisation: Design, build, and maintain engaging dashboards, graphs, and visualisations that turn complex numbers into clear, compelling stories.
Process Automation: Identify opportunities to increase efficiency, setting up and managing automated data processes to eliminate manual repetition.
Data Mining & Analysis: Mine and analyse large datasets (including sales and field marketing metrics), drawing valid, actionable inferences for our management teams.
Survey Insights: Design and analyse field surveys, presenting key findings and performance indicators (KPIs) with clarity and impact.
Data Governance: Support and develop our reporting processes, maintaining robust records management policies to ensure data integrity across our databases.




What We’re Looking For




The Analyst: Proven experience working in a dedicated Data or Reporting Analyst role (ideally with exposure to FMCG, retail, or field marketing environments).
The Power BI Builder: Practical experience building reports and dashboards in Power BI. You know how to make data visually intuitive.
The Tech Stack: Strong knowledge of SQL, DAX, M-Code as well as Tabular Editor.
The Mindset: Strong logical, mathematical, and numerical aptitude with a genuine passion for problem-solving.
The Communicator: A creative flair for presenting data and the unique ability to translate technical data issues into simple, clear concepts for non-technical audiences.




In return we will offer you:




A fun working environment where performance and success are really rewarded
Great career opportunities
Annual leave: 24 days (increasing annually up to 29 days) + bank holidays
Benefits include: Medicash, enhanced company pension, enhanced sick pay, life insurance, enhanced paternity leave, enhanced maternity leave, adoption leave, cycle to work scheme, referral programme + Perkbox and Taste Card




Are you ready to make an impact as our next Data and Reporting Analyst? Apply now!

Early applications are strongly encouraged as we reserve the right to pause or close this vacancy prior to the listed closing date.




More About Us...




At Smollan, our colleagues are valued, supported, and set up for success by empowering them with the tools, training, and opportunities to excel in a dynamic environment. We are committed to fostering an inclusive, welcoming workplace where everyone can thrive and celebrate individuality.




Awards




We are an Award-Winning Workplace! We’re proud to be recognised as one of UK & Europe’s Best Workplaces™* every year since 2015, with additional accolades:

UK’s Best Workplaces™ for Women* (since 2018)
UK’s Best Workplaces™ for Wellbeing* (since 2018)
UK’s Best Workplaces™ for Development* (since 2024)
UK’s Best Workplaces™ in Consulting & Professional Services* (2023)
Happiest Workplace Award* (WorkL, 2023,2024 & 2025)




We’re also the most awarded agency in our industry, with multiple FMBE awards* (our industry awards) and more Agency of the Year* titles than any competitor.




Sustainability*

Looking for a company committed to a Sustainable Future? Our environmental achievements include...

Ecovadis – Silver Award
ISO 14001 & ISO 27001 Certifications
Cyber Essentials Accreditation
Partnership with Oblong Trees
awards may be listed under our previous name, REL Field Marketing.




Our Rebranding Journey

In January 2026, REL Field Marketing became part of the Smollan brand, marking an exciting next chapter in our journey. With a 30-year heritage and being part of the Smollan network since 2015, this change is about more than a new name - it brings together our strong local expertise with Smollan’s global scale, insight, and innovation. You’ll be working for a business that values its local teams, now supported by global best practices, shared knowledge, and opportunities to build your career as part of a truly international organisation.

Title: Insight Analyst
Description:
Location: Milton Keynes – based in office 1-2 days a week (hybrid)

Hours: Full time

Reporting to: Head of People Science

Salary: £33K-£38K

About WorkBuzz

WorkBuzz was founded in 2018, has raised £12.5m funding and has 400 clients, including iconic brands like Five Guys, Butlins and Virgin. Our mission is to lead a new era of employee listening: AI-first, beyond surveys and turning employee voice into competitive advantage.

Our AI-powered platform makes it easy for mid and large-sized companies to gather real-time employee feedback and uncover what truly matters. Our new AI-native product Dialogue launches us into operational listening, putting employee listening in the hands of operational leaders – where decisions are made and change actually happens.

Trusted across industries, we combine smart technology with a human approach - helping leaders make better decisions, faster. We’re growing fast and looking for mission-driven people to join us!

The Role

We're looking for a tech-savvy, AI-forward Analyst to join our People Science team, turning WorkBuzz employee listening data into clear, accurate and useful insight for clients.

This role is for someone who actively seeks smarter ways to work, freeing up time for the deeper analysis, thinking and storytelling that truly adds value. Working closely with our consultants and guided by the Lead Analyst, the role builds expertise in the WorkBuzz platform, employee listening data and analytical methods, developing towards greater ownership of client and broader business insight work over time.

Key Responsibilities

Turn data into insight at pace


Produce accurate analysis, charts, tables and slides from employee survey results for use in client reports and presentations
Ensure standard outputs including demographic cuts, trend comparisons and summary tables, following established templates and methods, and using automation to increase speed and consistency where possible
Support more advanced analysis, such as driver analysis, segmentation analysis and performance linkage with guidance from the Lead Analyst
Ensure appropriate checks to ensure accuracy of all outputs before they're shared


Support great client delivery


Work with People Science Consultants to understand specific client needs, outputs required and delivery timelines
Ensure report decks are populated with well-formatted, accurate data summaries, flagging interesting patterns or anomalies along the way
Respond to ad-hoc analysis requests quickly and reliably, ensuring consultants are equipped to do their best work
Use and help maintain reusable presentation templates, chart formats and analysis approaches to support consistency across client deliverables.


Support improvements in outcomes and efficiencies


Actively use and champion AI tools to accelerate analysis, charting, summarisation and quality checking
Identify repetitive or manual tasks in the reporting workflow in the development of smarter, automated approaches
Help establish and document AI-assisted methods that the wider team can adopt and build on
Follow, and where possible improve, documented methods, quality checks and analytical assumptions to ensure outputs are accurate and consistent with WorkBuzz standards
Contribute to reusable templates, automated workflows and shared knowledge that make the whole team more effective


Platform knowledge


Build a strong working knowledge of the WorkBuzz platform, including survey setup, data mapping, reporting structures and dashboards
Share feedback, recurring issues and improvement ideas from day-to-day analysis work to help improve reporting and workflows.
Support user testing of new reporting and analysis features when required
Keep up to date with platform releases and new reporting or analysis capabilities, applying them in client work where appropriate


Requirements For Role


Some experience in an analyst, insights, research or data-focused role, or a strong academic background with hands-on data analysis experience from placements, internships or project work
Degree in a relevant subject such as psychology, organisational psychology, business, economics, statistics, data science or adjacent
Solid quantitative analysis skills, comfortable working accurately with survey results, trends, demographic cuts and comparisons
Enthusiasm for AI tools and automation; you’re keen to experiment and find better ways to work
Hands-on experience with AI tools such as Claude or similar, applied to real analytical or reporting tasks
Strong Excel skills including formulas, pivot tables and charting; strong PowerPoint skills to ensure clear and polished client slides
Genuine attention to detail and a critical eye when reviewing outputs
Clear written communication, ensuring complex findings are summarised simply and accurately
Well organised, with the ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines in a fast-paced, client-focused environment
Awareness of data protection, confidentiality and responsible AI use when handling people data


Desirable


Experience working with employee survey, engagement or HR data and an interest in employee engagement, culture and the employee experience agenda
Experience with BI or data visualisation tools
Some experience of producing client-facing reports, charts or presentation materials


We seek people who chime with our values and demonstrate, in their own way, behaviours that bring them to life. We’re not looking for one “type” of person - we value individuality and diversity. What matters most is that candidates demonstrate behaviours that support success in-role and make our culture thrive.   

Aspire Higher - We set BIG goals, embrace growth and challenge limits. Reaching for new possibilities, we are curious and continuously learning, if there’s a better way, we’ll find it.  

Own with Impact- We take ownership and hold ourselves accountable, focused on creating meaningful impact. We lean in and own it with purpose and passion.  

Energised by Challenge- We never stand still! We approach challenges with energy and resilience. We thrive on the tough stuff, embracing change as a constant.  

Trust in Action- We build trust through our expertise and through consistent, high-quality delivery. With transparency, reliability and authenticity at our core, we act with integrity and do what we say, delivering on our promises.  

Win Together- We are fuelled by collaboration. We elevate each other, our clients and our partners showing up in meaningful ways. Sharing in success, we celebrate every victory- big or small.  

Why you’ll love working at WorkBuzz 


Our culture – WorkBuzz is fast-paced, dynamic and rewarding. Everyone has a voice and the smartest idea wins.  
Work at the frontier - Dialogue is genuinely new territory. You'll be shaping how AI transforms employee listening, not maintaining someone else's roadmap.
Clear purpose – be part of building something which matters.  
Be the best you can be – we will invest in your development and help you grow.  
Competitive salary, share options, private health insurance and a range of other benefits  
Flexible/ hybrid working – the opportunity to split your time between home working and our Milton Keynes office.  
Check out our Glassdoor reviews!  


We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to creating a workplace where everyone feels respected, valued, and empowered to thrive. Our team is stronger when it is diverse and inclusive.  

We welcome applicants from all walks of life and our hiring decisions are based on merit - regardless of age, disability, race, religion or belief, sex, gender, sexual orientation, gender reassignment, pregnancy or maternity, marital or civil partnership status.  

If you need any adjustments or support during the recruitment process, please don’t hesitate to let us know. We’re here to ensure a fair and accessible experience, removing barriers that may impede someone’s progress based on their unique circumstances.

  • Contribute to reusable templates, automated workflows and shared knowledge that make the whole team more effective

Platform knowledge

  • Build a strong working knowledge of the WorkBuzz platform, including survey setup, data mapping, reporting structures and dashboards
  • Share feedback, recurring issues and improvement ideas from day-to-day analysis work to help improve reporting and workflows.
  • Support user testing of new reporting and analysis features when required
  • Keep up to date with platform releases and new reporting or analysis capabilities, applying them in client work where appropriate

Requirements For Role

  • Some experience in an analyst, insights, research or data-focused role, or a strong academic background with hands-on data analysis experience from placements, internships or project work
  • Degree in a relevant subject such as psychology, organisational psychology, business, economics, statistics, data science or adjacent
  • Solid quantitative analysis skills, comfortable working accurately with survey results, trends, demographic cuts and comparisons
  • Enthusiasm for AI tools and automation; you’re keen to experiment and find better ways to work
  • Hands-on experience with AI tools such as Claude or similar, applied to real analytical or reporting tasks
  • Strong Excel skills including formulas, pivot tables and charting; strong PowerPoint skills to ensure clear and polished client slides
  • Genuine attention to detail and a critical eye when reviewing outputs
  • Clear written communication, ensuring complex findings are summarised simply and accurately
  • Well organised, with the ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines in a fast-paced, client-focused environment
  • Awareness of data protection, confidentiality and responsible AI use when handling people data

Desirable

  • Experience working with employee survey, engagement or HR data and an interest in employee engagement, culture and the employee experience agenda
  • Experience with BI or data visualisation tools
  • Some experience of producing client-facing reports, charts or presentation materials

We seek people who chime with our values and demonstrate, in their own way, behaviours that bring them to life. We’re not looking for one “type” of person - we value individuality and diversity. What matters most is that candidates demonstrate behaviours that support success in-role and make our culture thrive.   

Aspire Higher - We set BIG goals, embrace growth and challenge limits. Reaching for new possibilities, we are curious and continuously learning, if there’s a better way, we’ll find it.  

Own with Impact- We take ownership and hold ourselves accountable, focused on creating meaningful impact. We lean in and own it with purpose and passion.  

Energised by Challenge- We never stand still! We approach challenges with energy and resilience. We thrive on the tough stuff, embracing change as a constant.  

Trust in Action- We build trust through our expertise and through consistent, high-quality delivery. With transparency, reliability and authenticity at our core, we act with integrity and do what we say, delivering on our promises.  

Win Together- We are fuelled by collaboration. We elevate each other, our clients and our partners showing up in meaningful ways. Sharing in success, we celebrate every victory- big or small.  

Why you’ll love working at WorkBuzz 

  • Our culture – WorkBuzz is fast-paced, dynamic and rewarding. Everyone has a voice and the smartest idea wins.  
  • Work at the frontier - Dialogue is genuinely new territory. You'll be shaping how AI transforms employee listening, not maintaining someone else's roadmap.
  • Clear purpose – be part of building something which matters.  
  • Be the best you can be – we will invest in your development and help you grow.  
  • Competitive salary, share options, private health insurance and a range of other benefits  
  • Flexible/ hybrid working – the opportunity to split your time between home working and our Milton Keynes office.  
  • Check out our Glassdoor reviews!  

We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to creating a workplace where everyone feels respected, valued, and empowered to thrive. Our team is stronger when it is diverse and inclusive.  

We welcome applicants from all walks of life and our hiring decisions are based on merit - regardless of age, disability, race, religion or belief, sex, gender, sexual orientation, gender reassignment, pregnancy or maternity, marital or civil partnership status.  

If you need any adjustments or support during the recruitment process, please don’t hesitate to let us know. We’re here to ensure a fair and accessible experience, removing barriers that may impede someone’s progress based on their unique circumstances.

Title: Product Management Performance Analyst
Description:
Bring more to life.

Are you ready to accelerate your potential and make a real difference within life sciences, diagnostics and biotechnology?

At Abcam, one of Danaher’s 15+ operating companies, our work saves lives—and we’re all united by a shared commitment to innovate for tangible impact.

You’ll thrive in a culture of belonging where you and your unique viewpoint matter. And by harnessing Danaher’s system of continuous improvement, you help turn ideas into impact – innovating at the speed of life.

Progress happens together

At Abcam, we believe the scientific community goes further, faster when we go there together. That’s why we work with life scientists to provide biological reagents and solutions that enable faster breakthroughs in critical areas like cancer, neurological disorders, infectious diseases and metabolic disorders. Our talented team of over 1,400 colleagues worldwide is our greatest asset. By applying our expertise and pushing boundaries, together we strive to better serve our customers, strengthen our impact across our industry, and ultimately our positive impact on society.

Learn about the Danaher Business System which makes everything possible.

The Product Management Performance Analyst role is part of the wider Product Management team and focused on assisting colleagues and improving their productivity with the timely supply of product performance and planning data so that they can better serve our customers effectively and efficiently. Our vision is for this role to act as a catalyst for positive change across the Product Management team and the wider business.

Working within the Pricing team team and partnering with Product Management leaders within the business, you will be responsible for improving global performance levels of our team with data analysis tools, KPIs and reports to drive outstanding commercial activities.

This position reports to the Pricing Director and is part of the pricing team who support the Product Management organisation at Abcam. This is an on-site role located in Cambridge and an opportunity to work in a fast paced environment in a growing global business with a data driven mindset.

In This Role, You Will Have The Opportunity To


Maintain and improve Microsoft PowerBi performance reporting suites for the Product Management teams
Support with the identification of relevant KPIs and measures which reflect the performance of the Product Management teams
Use reporting and analysis to identify areas to drive change, course corrections or further iterations in product strategies and provide ad hoc analytics support to the function on data analysis, reporting and performance.
Prepare and present data/insights to senior stakeholders as needed
Maintain and improve our data sources to allow faster, more automated reporting, working with our Data Engineering team who are owners of our main data sources.


The Essential Requirements Of The Job Include


Experience in a data analytical position, delivering data management, data analysis, insight and reporting solutions and an expert in PowerBi and DAX, with knowledge of Tableau or equivalent data visualisation software with a commitment to learn quickly
Performance management experience – ability to propose new KPIs based on gaps in performance, innovative and inquisitive and someone who thrives in a dynamic, ever-changing environment with the ability to multitask, prioritise and meet deadlines.
Possess strong communication and presentation skills - passionate about storytelling via data and a proactive self-starter who can pick things up quickly and run with projects autonomously
Output focused - motivated by achieving goals


It would be a plus if you also possess previous experience in:


SQL coding
Salesforce CRM
Microsoft Excel and Powerpoint


Abcam, a Danaher operating company, offers a broad array of comprehensive, competitive benefit programs that add value to our lives. Whether it’s a health care program or paid time off, our programs contribute to life beyond the job. Check out our benefits at Danaher Benefits Info.

Join our winning team today. Together, we’ll accelerate the real-life impact of tomorrow’s science and technology. We partner with customers across the globe to help them solve their most complex challenges, architecting solutions that bring the power of science to life.

For more information, visit www.danaher.com.

Title: People Insight Lead
Description:
Role: People Insight LeadS...

Role: People Insight Lead

Salary: £47,000 - £52,000 dependant on skills and experience + up to 20% bonus + bens

Contract Type: Permanent

Location: Dunstable - LU5 5XE, 3 days working from the office, 2 days working from home

Role Overview

Are you ready to lead how data shapes people decisions at scale? As a People Insight Lead, you will play a critical role in driving a step-change in how insight informs strategy, partnering senior stakeholders to deliver impactful analytics that influence business direction.

This is more than a reporting role; it’s an opportunity to transform the way People data is used, moving from reactive reporting to proactive, insight-led decision making. You will lead the development of a modern, automated reporting ecosystem, building scalable dashboards, embedding self-service capability, and helping to create a unified “single view” of employee data.

Working at the intersection of People, Finance and Technology, you will translate business priorities into clear analytics roadmaps by delivering deep insights on areas such as retention, hiring, talent, engagement and productivity. You will also introduce more advanced and predictive techniques, ensuring data not only explains the past but shapes the future.

A key part of the role is data leadership: you will mentor analysts, elevate data literacy, and champion best practice in data storytelling and governance. You will work directly with senior leaders, influencing decision-making and ensuring insight is embedded into everyday thinking.

This is a highly visible role where you will see the direct impact of your work, building capability, driving innovation and helping create a truly insight-led organisation.

Minimum Criteria


Proven experience designing and delivering scalable reporting solutions (e.g. building Power BI dashboards, defining KPI frameworks, and standardising metrics across a business)
Demonstrable track record of partnering senior stakeholders to translate business challenges into analytics roadmaps and actionable insight outputs
Strong experience working with People/HR data and systems (e.g. Dayforce) and enterprise data platforms (e.g. Azure), including ensuring data quality, governance and compliance
Hands-on experience applying advanced analytical techniques (e.g. trend analysis, predictive modelling, statistical methods) to generate business insight
Experience leading or mentoring analysts and managing delivery pipelines/backlogs, including prioritisation of insight requests aligned to business impact


We believe that everyone is unique and there should be no barriers to entry and no limits to ambition. We are committed to being an inclusive organisation that values diversity and welcomes your application whatever your background or situation.

Under-represented groups such as women, ethnic minorities, people with disabilities & members of the LGBTQIA+ community (those who identify as lesbian, gay, bi, trans and non-binary or those who use a different LGBTQIA+ term), are strongly encouraged to build a career with us. Speak to us about workplace adjustments, part-time and flexible working. Where possible we will support this.

Advertised: 03 Jul 2026

Application closed: 20 Jul 2026

Title: Business Analyst
Description:
Karo Healthcare, a dynamic and growing personal-care retail company, is all about making smart choices for everyday healthcare. With a wide-ranging portfolio across seven categories including medical products, we own and sell over 80 brands in about 90 countries. Our portfolio includes such brands as E45, Pevaryl, Lamisil, Nutravita, Decubal and many others. Our core belief in empowering people to make intelligent health decisions drives our growth strategy, blending organic expansion and M&A.

Join us at Karo Healthcare on our exciting journey. We're currently looking for a Business Analyst for our office in Maidenhead (UK).

What's in it for you?


Make a real commercial impact - own and shape Revenue Growth Management analytics in an international consumer healthcare business
Be hands-on with modern tech - work daily with Power BI and Databricks in a scalable Data & Analytics setup
Influence decisions across 90 markets - partner with Commercial teams and turn data into pricing, promotion and portfolio actions
Accelerate your career - join a fast-growing, KKR-backed company where ambition, international exposure and development go hand in hand

What you'll be doing


Translate Revenue Growth Management (RGM) and Sales needs into clear analytical requirements, KPIs and functional specifications
Design, build and continuously enhance Power BI dashboards, reports and data models to drive pricing, promotion and assortment decisions
Collaborate closely with Data Engineers to connect front-end analytics with our Databricks Data Lakehouse
Partner with Commercial, Marketing, Finance and Operations stakeholders across markets to turn data into actionable insights
Ensure data accuracy and quality, test solutions and drive user adoption in an international environment
Contribute to broader commercial and performance analytics initiatives as the function grows



Requirements


3+ years' experience in Business Analysis, Data Analytics or similar roles within FMCG, consumer healthcare, retail or other fast-paced, commercially driven environments
Strong hands-on expertise in Power BI (data modelling, DAX, dashboard development, visualization best practices)
Solid understanding of Revenue Growth Management, Sales analytics and commercial performance metrics
Experience working cross-functionally across markets with senior Commercial, Marketing, Finance, Operations and IT stakeholders
Good knowledge of data modelling concepts, Power Query and data governance, with hands-on working ability in Databricks and SQL
Degree in Business, Finance, Engineering, Computer Science or a related field
Fluency in English, written and spoken



Benefits


Opportunity to create, grow, and encourage
There are lots of growth opportunities to meet your personal ambitions
Flexible schedule, hybrid model with the primary work place in the office, and life-work balance
Responsibility for exciting and challenging projects that have a direct, visible impact on our customers and the industry
Very positive work environment in a dynamic, international, and motivated team
Start-up spirit while being a part of the large international organization with strong values

Karo Healthcare has a Diverse & Inclusive environment. We are looking for qualified candidates irrespective of gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, ethnicity, race, religion, national origin, disability or age.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

The Opportunity

Hult International Business School is seeking a Data Analyst to join the External Affairs team based in London, UK. The post-holder will work closely with the Data and Analytics Manager and have a unique opportunity to positively impact the organisation by providing data analytics, developing actionable insights and delivering exceptional business value.

The role suits an individual with an analytical mindset. The successful candidate will have a few years’ experience working with data and analytics and be able to work under pressure. Attention to detail is critical as the work will support decision-making across the business.

Please note that you must have the right to work in the UK to be considered for this position.

Responsibilities


Supporting major statutory student returns, optimising the impact of those returns and using the resultant data to support the organisation.
Collecting and analysing internal and external data and preparing reports, forecasts, models and ‘what if’ scenarios.
Consulting with internal stakeholders (e.g., Sales, Marketing, Operations, Finance, Academic teams, etc.) to develop analyses that lead to actionable insights.
Collating data from multiple sources and databases including sales, marketing, operations, market trends and competition, finance and customer databases to create integrated views that can be used to drive decision making.
Designing and building reports, dashboards and analyses in Excel, SQL, Python, Salesforce, Tableau, Snowflake.


Essential Requirements


A few years’ experience (typically 2–3 years) in a data analyst or similar analytical role
Exceptionally motivated, curious and strong problem-solving skills
Ability to interpret industry trends and business needs – analytical mindset is key
Understanding of database capabilities and experience of integrating, structuring and analysing large amounts of data from diverse sources
Ability to prioritise, meet challenging deadlines and instil urgency
Flexible, open-minded and agile
Self-directed and self-motivated with a demonstrated strength in reporting, planning and project management skills to meet deadlines
Strong communication skills with the ability and desire to collaborate with key business stakeholders
Bachelor’s degree required
Strong SQL skills required; experience with Python, Tableau and Snowflake is highly desirable
Comfortable using AI tools (e.g., Codex, Claude code or similar) and modern development environments such as VS Code to accelerate analysis, coding and reporting workflows

Title: Yield Specialist
Description:
This role sits within Yield Management UK&I Region of our Commercial Division and reports into the Senior Yield Destination Manager

Have you ever wondered how the price of your package holiday is decided, why they sometimes change from the last time you looked or why the cost varies across different destinations?

Well, why not join our Yield department here at TUI to find out how it works in the world of travel! We are looking to recruit a team of Yield Specialists who get a buzz out of spotting patterns and love working with data to ensure a proactive approach to problem-solving and are commercially driven. You will have accountability for the revenue management of your own range of destinations, striving to optimise sales and profitability ensuring the right price is available to our customers at the right time.

We will ensure that you have first-class training and a support network of colleagues in place to help you succeed in the role. You’ll work through a development framework to support your long-term career goals, with progression opportunities available. A quote from one of our past specialists now in a manager role: ‘I really like being part of a big inclusive team and with a shared objective to sell as many seats/hotels at the best price. It means everyone really has to work together to get the best results'.


We offer flexible hybrid working, with one day a week on average in our Luton office to connect, collaborate and build strong relationships with the team. Brand new offices opening in early 2027, close to M1 Junction 10 and Thameslink train line.


For those shortlisted to the final stage we will host in-person assessment days.

Now taking applications until 26th July 2026, please apply as soon as possible, as we expect a high volume of applications for this role. This may lead us to close the vacancy before the application closing date.

About Our Offer


Personal benefits: Salary £31,000 per annum, trading incentive schemes, generous travel benefits, extensive health & well-being support, and more.
Flexible hybrid working: Work is something you do, not somewhere you go. We encourage a healthy *work-life balance with a dynamic working environment.
A career to shape: Access the TUI Learning Hub to level-up and reach your ambitions.
Broaden your network: We champion intercultural collaboration and provide opportunities to work on global projects and teams
Community: Get involved with incredible local charity and sustainability initiatives like the TUI Care Foundation.


About The Job


You'll be working within a team environment responsible for several holiday destinations, striving to optimise sales, profitability and maximise both flight and hotel performance to hit key performance indicators.
In this analytical role, you'll use yield systems and review booking trends to make informed recommendations and decisions that deliver results in line with your destination strategy.
We'll rely on you to constantly monitor and react to demand in the competitive holiday market, offering relevant pricing solutions to maximise opportunities and overcome sales barriers.
Your decisions will directly drive the price offering to our customers and ultimately feed into the overall financial performance of TUI UK&I.
Building strong internal relationships with key stakeholders across the commercial division and beyond will be essential to drive current and future business strategy.
We offer a clear career pathway for progression, giving you the opportunity to drive your own development while learning alongside some of the best in the industry.


About You


Ideally, you’re have proven analytical experience, with the ability to interpret complex data and translate it into actionable insights.
Your numeracy and data analysis skills allow you to spot trends and make informed pricing decisions.
A structured, focused and conscientious approach to work ensures you deliver consistent results.
Your proactive, curious nature drives you to continuously learn and improve your understanding of the travel market.
Understanding different perspectives – both customers' and colleagues' – comes naturally to you, helping you make balanced decisions.
Highly proficient in Microsoft Office, especially Excel for data analysis and reporting


And of course, it helps if you love holidays as much as we do!

We're passionate about innovation and the role AI can play in the future of work. However, for your application, we'd love to hear about your experiences, achievements and aspirations in your own words rather than AI-generated responses.

From a workplace to a place to belong. At TUI we embrace diversity, equity, and inclusion, encouraging everyone to come as you are, because together, our potential is limitless.

We are committed to supporting candidates with disabilities and impairments so if you require any support, please do let us know.

  • We offer flexible hybrid working, with one day a week on average in our Luton office to connect, collaborate and build strong relationships with the team. Brand new offices opening in early 2027, close to M1 Junction 10 and Thameslink train line.

For those shortlisted to the final stage we will host in-person assessment days.

Now taking applications until 26th July 2026, please apply as soon as possible, as we expect a high volume of applications for this role. This may lead us to close the vacancy before the application closing date.

About Our Offer

  • Personal benefits: Salary £31,000 per annum, trading incentive schemes, generous travel benefits, extensive health & well-being support, and more.
  • Flexible hybrid working: Work is something you do, not somewhere you go. We encourage a healthy *work-life balance with a dynamic working environment.
  • A career to shape: Access the TUI Learning Hub to level-up and reach your ambitions.
  • Broaden your network: We champion intercultural collaboration and provide opportunities to work on global projects and teams
  • Community: Get involved with incredible local charity and sustainability initiatives like the TUI Care Foundation.

About The Job

  • You'll be working within a team environment responsible for several holiday destinations, striving to optimise sales, profitability and maximise both flight and hotel performance to hit key performance indicators.
  • In this analytical role, you'll use yield systems and review booking trends to make informed recommendations and decisions that deliver results in line with your destination strategy.
  • We'll rely on you to constantly monitor and react to demand in the competitive holiday market, offering relevant pricing solutions to maximise opportunities and overcome sales barriers.
  • Your decisions will directly drive the price offering to our customers and ultimately feed into the overall financial performance of TUI UK&I.
  • Building strong internal relationships with key stakeholders across the commercial division and beyond will be essential to drive current and future business strategy.
  • We offer a clear career pathway for progression, giving you the opportunity to drive your own development while learning alongside some of the best in the industry.

About You

  • Ideally, you’re have proven analytical experience, with the ability to interpret complex data and translate it into actionable insights.
  • Your numeracy and data analysis skills allow you to spot trends and make informed pricing decisions.
  • A structured, focused and conscientious approach to work ensures you deliver consistent results.
  • Your proactive, curious nature drives you to continuously learn and improve your understanding of the travel market.
  • Understanding different perspectives – both customers' and colleagues' – comes naturally to you, helping you make balanced decisions.
  • Highly proficient in Microsoft Office, especially Excel for data analysis and reporting

And of course, it helps if you love holidays as much as we do!

We're passionate about innovation and the role AI can play in the future of work. However, for your application, we'd love to hear about your experiences, achievements and aspirations in your own words rather than AI-generated responses.

From a workplace to a place to belong. At TUI we embrace diversity, equity, and inclusion, encouraging everyone to come as you are, because together, our potential is limitless.

We are committed to supporting candidates with disabilities and impairments so if you require any support, please do let us know.

Title: Finance Systems Analyst
Description:
Job Title: Finance Business Intelligence Analyst




We’re looking for a Finance Business Intelligence Analyst to join our Finance team. This role is central to how Finance operates day‑to‑day, ensuring our systems, data, and reporting are accurate, reliable, and enabling better decision‑making across the organisation.




This is a hands‑on role for someone who enjoys working with data, systems, and reporting tools, and who takes pride in accuracy and structure. You’ll play a key part in maintaining finance systems, developing reporting solutions, and supporting the wider Finance function with the data they rely on.




The work is varied and detail‑focused. From system administration and data integrity to building Power BI reports and supporting finance processes, you’ll be involved in the operational backbone of Finance.




This role suits someone who enjoys working behind the scenes, improving how things work, and making sure the detail is right.




Finance Systems & Data Ownership

You’ll take ownership of key finance systems, ensuring data is accurate, up to date, and structured correctly. This includes systems related to timesheets, expenses, and company cards. You’ll play a critical role in maintaining data integrity so that Finance processes can run smoothly and without delays.




Reporting & Business Intelligence

You’ll design, build, and maintain reports using Power BI, working with stakeholders to understand requirements and translate them into clear, usable outputs. You’ll also develop and maintain Excel models to support financial analysis and reporting across the organisation.




System Development & Improvements

You’ll support ongoing system improvements and migrations, helping Finance evolve its tools and processes. This includes identifying opportunities to improve data flows, reporting capabilities, and system usability.




Finance Operations Support

You’ll contribute to the smooth running of Finance by supporting key operational processes, including delivering finance system inductions to new joiners and ensuring systems are set up correctly from day one.




Organisation & Delivery

This is a detail‑heavy role where you’ll manage multiple priorities at once. You’ll keep systems updated, track changes, respond to requests, and ensure reporting outputs are delivered accurately and on time.




About You




You May Be a Good Fit If You…

Have experience working with data, reporting, or finance systems in an analytical or operational role
Are confident using Excel and Power BI to build reports and models
Enjoy working with systems and ensuring data accuracy and consistency
Are highly organised and comfortable managing multiple tasks and deadlines
Naturally spot errors, inconsistencies, or gaps in data
Take pride in producing accurate, high‑quality work
Are comfortable working with stakeholders to understand reporting needs




Desirable (but not essential):

Experience with SQL, MS Access, or similar tools
Exposure to finance systems such as Sage, expenses platforms, or invoicing tools
Experience supporting system migrations or process improvements







Why GÉANT?

GÉANT is Europe’s leading collaboration on e‑infrastructure and services for research and education, connecting over 50 million users across 40+ countries. We develop and deliver high‑impact networking, identity, security, and cloud services that underpin scientific discovery and academic collaboration worldwide.




What We Offer

A mission-driven role at the heart of European R&E infrastructure
A collaborative, international working environment spanning 40+ countries
Flexible working arrangements with a remote‑friendly culture
Opportunity to shape services used by tens of millions of researchers and students




GÉANT is committed to diversity and inclusion. We welcome applications from candidates of all backgrounds.




Application Process

Applications close on 14th June 2026, although we may close earlier if we receive sufficient interest. Every application is reviewed by a member of our team.




Our selection process is designed to be transparent and respectful of your time, and includes:




An initial call with a recruiter (for longlisted candidates)
An interview with the hiring manager
A final stage interview




Data & Privacy

Once you apply, GÉANT Association will act as the controller of your data. Our full Candidate Privacy Notice can be found here:

https://geant.org/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/GEANT_Candidate_Privacy_Notice_V1.2.pdf

Title: Pricing and Revenue Strategist (E-commerce)
Description:
About Us

Located in Milton Keynes, F&F Stores is a rapidly expanding E-commerce business that thrives on bringing licensed products from beloved brands like Disney, Marvel, Minecraft and iconic football clubs directly to your doorstep.

Our high-quality products include a wide range for the whole family, from toys and fashion to home accessories and beauty items, found on popular E-commerce marketplaces and our own e-commerce website.

Location: Office-based, in Milton Keynes

Contract: Full-time, Permanent

Number of vacancies: 3

Salary: £35,000 - £38,000

Role Overview

We’re looking for a Pricing and Revenue Strategist with strong analytical capabilities, financial insight, and deep ecommerce pricing experience — especially within Amazon or marketplace environments. In this role, you will drive revenue growth by developing pricing frameworks, optimizing pricing performance, and translating market data into actionable strategies that boost profitability and competitiveness.

Key Responsibilities

Strategic Pricing & Revenue


Lead analysis of market trends, competitor pricing behaviour, and customer demand to shape pricing strategy.
Propose, model, and optimise pricing recommendations that balance growth, margin, and market positioning.
Develop pricing frameworks, elasticity models, and scenario simulations to support decision-making.
Support the implementation and adjustment of dynamic pricing rules where applicable.


E-commerce & Marketplace Focus


Monitor pricing performance and key metrics across major ecommerce platforms, with specific emphasis on Amazon (Seller Central experience desirable).
Collaborate with marketplace operations, growth, and account management teams to align pricing with promotions, buy box priorities, and inventory strategies.


Data & Financial Analysis


Dive deep into sales performance, cost structures, and profitability modelling to inform pricing decisions.
Build dashboards, forecasting models, and reporting tools that provide insights into price elasticity and revenue impact.
Present findings and recommendations clearly to senior management and cross-functional partners.


Cross-Functional Collaboration


Work closely with Finance, Marketing, Product, and Supply teams to ensure pricing strategies support broader business goals.
Influence pricing decisions through data storytelling, clear communication, and strategic thinking.


Continuous Improvement


Monitor pricing performance post-changes and iterate on strategies to maximise business impact.
Stay current on ecommerce pricing trends, competitor moves, and technology enhancements.


Required Qualifications


Degree in Business, Economics, Finance, Data Analytics, or related field.
3+ years of experience in pricing strategy, revenue management, finance, data analysis, or related ecommerce strategy roles.
Strong analytical skills; ability to work with large datasets using Excel, SQL, BI tools, or similar.
Demonstrated ability to build pricing and revenue models and translate insights into business recommendations.
Excellent communication skills; comfortable presenting to stakeholders and leadership.


Desirable Experience


Hands-on experience with Amazon marketplace tools (Seller Central, pricing APIs, repricers, etc.).
Knowledge of dynamic and competitive pricing strategies in a marketplace environment.
Experience in BI or visualisation tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI).
Understanding of promotional strategies and their revenue impact.


Benefits


Casual dress
On-site parking
Regular social events including summer & end of year parties.
Spontaneous employee well-being treats
An Amazon Birthday voucher
Vouchers at Christmas to shop with F&F
Staff Purchases
NEST Pension
Private medical insurance with Vitality health with various benefits including discounts on a gym membership.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
About Signal Ocean: Signal Ocean is the technology arm of the Signal Group. Our primary product, The Signal Ocean Platform, helps shipping and commodities professionals navigate their complex decision making. Driven by advanced machine learning and artificial intelligence, our technology suite provides tailored, exclusive insights that support our clients in achieving performance and efficiency. By securely handling and combining private and public shipping data flows, and applying advanced analytics, insights are delivered over web and mobile applications, as well as through a rich set of APIs and SDKs. Our backend architecture is abstracted to modularly offer deep analytics capabilities that are leveraged in the solutions that we offer or can be directly embedded in our client's system topologies.




About the role:

We are looking for a Business Intelligence Analyst to join our growing Analytics team and help shape the next generation of shipping intelligence through AI-driven workflows.

In this role, you will transform raw data into actionable insights, build intuitive dashboards that support data-driven decision making, and work closely with product and commercial teams to better understand and address client needs.

As AI becomes increasingly embedded into analytics workflows, we are especially interested in candidates who are comfortable leveraging modern AI tools (e.g. Claude Code, Copilot, ChatGPT, etc.) to accelerate analysis, automate workflows, and create more intuitive and impactful analytical products.




At Signal, we are committed to finding people who are respectful, inclusive, and team players, embodying these values in every role

What you will do in this role:

Analyze complex datasets to create meaningful data sources across diverse shipping topics
Leverage the latest AI tools to extract, analyze, and visualize actionable business insights
Build intuitive dashboards that deliver insightful storytelling and support strategic decision making
Translate client and business needs into analytical solutions and impactful reporting
Collaborate closely with product, data, engineering, and commercial teams
Use AI-powered tools to improve analytics workflows, dashboard creation, and insight generation
Identify trends, disruptions, and market patterns across shipping data
Contribute to the development of next-generation analytics products and reporting capabilities




Requirements

3+ years of experience in a Data Analyst / BI Analyst role
Strong analytical, problem solving and critical thinking skills
Hands-on experience with SQL and interpreting complex datasets
Proven experience building intuitive dashboards and visual storytelling solutions
Practical experience leveraging AI tools and prompting techniques to deliver product features
Strong communication and teamwork skills




Strong plus but not required:

Ability to work cross-functionally with other product teams using Github and Jira
Shipping or commodities industry knowledge

If you are passionate about analytics, AI-enabled workflows, and solving complex industry problems through data, we would love to hear from you.




Benefits

Generous compensation with additional performance incentives.
Coverage under the company's collective health insurance plan.
Opportunity to work alongside experienced people with deep knowledge in software engineering, data science & shipping business who are always eager to mentor.
Signal's hybrid remote work policy currently includes 9 working days at premises per month, during which happy hour events take place
2-4 weeks of onboarding training to prepare you for your new role, having the opportunity to meet about 30 trainers while diving deep into our products and/or the shipping world.
Career growth opportunities and a structured development discussion every 4 months.
Personal learning budget for training, seminars, conferences (750 to 2000 EUR annually depending on seniority).
Regular team bonding events and activities.




Strict adherence to Confidentiality, Intellectual Property and Non-Compete provisions is expected.

All applications will be considered under the terms and conditions of confidentiality in accordance with the regulations of personal data protection.

We are an Equal Opportunity Employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. At Signal, we believe that diversity of opinions, approaches and viewpoints is key to our innovation and success and we encourage that with our hiring, development and rewards practices. We prohibit discrimination and harassment of any kind based on race, color, sex, religion, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, genetic information, pregnancy, or any other protected characteristics by law and take actions to eliminate those from our workplace
