  • Analyze pricing proposals, prepare comparisons, and support recommendation development
  • Build impactful, client-facing proposal materials for both existing and prospective clients
  • Support RFP responses and finalist presentations in partnership with business development and client management teams

Insights & Strategy Enablement

  • Analyze plan performance, member behaviour, and utilization trends to identify opportunities for improvement
  • Develop data-driven insights that inform plan design, investment menus, and member engagement strategies
  • Research industry trends and competitive positioning to strengthen NIS market differentiation
  • Utilize AI tools to accelerate analysis and surface insights across large and complex datasets

Operational Support & Collaboration

  • Partner closely with the National Director, Group Retirement Strategy, and broader NIS leadership team
  • Prepare materials for client meetings, plan reviews, and internal strategic discussions
  • Support governance processes and ensure readiness of documentation and reporting requirements
  • Contribute to a collaborative, high-performing team environment aligned to NIS priorities

To be successful as a Retirement Analyst with the National Indigenous Solutions Practice, you will need:

  • Minimum 2 years of experience in data analysis, financial services, group retirement, or a related field
  • Foundational understanding of group retirement programs, pricing, and reporting (or strong ability to learn quickly)
  • Advanced Excel skills and experience with data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI)
  • Demonstrated hands-on experience using AI tools (e.g., Copilot, automation tools) to enhance analysis, improve workflows, and generate insights
  • Strong ability to work with large, multi-source datasets and translate findings into clear business narratives
  • Experience supporting pricing, proposals, or client-facing deliverables is an asset
  • High attention to detail, organization, and ability to manage multiple priorities
  • Strong communication and presentation skills, including building client-ready materials
  • Curiosity, adaptability, and a mindset of continuous improvement through technology

What’s In It For You

  • An opportunity to make a real impact in Indigenous communities across Canada
  • Involvement in meaningful work supporting Truth and Reconciliation efforts
  • Ongoing learning and collaboration with a supportive and experienced team
  • Competitive compensation
  • Comprehensive employee benefits and retirement programs
  • A fun, flexible, and productive work environment

Our Commitment

National Indigenous Solutions is committed to building a team that reflects the communities we serve. We welcome and encourage applications from First Nations, Métis, and Inuit candidates. Applicants are invited to self-declare Indigenous identity in their application.

The National Indigenous Solutions Practice, a division of People Corporation, is a trusted Group Insurance & Retirement practice proudly serving Indigenous employers across Canada. The National Indigenous Solutions Practice is comprised of three sister companies – Eagle Bay Financial (Burnaby, BC), Life Benefit Solutions (Winnipeg, MB) and WP Pension and Benefits (Burlington, ON) who all specialize in designing benefit and group retirement programs that thoughtfully integrate Non-Insured Health Benefits (NIHB), supporting equity between Status and non-Status employees. Their mission is to deliver customized, community-conscious solutions that meet the unique needs of Indigenous and non-Indigenous employees alike.

We are committed to fostering a respectful, inclusive, and accessible environment where all employees and clients feel supported and valued. We are proud to build a workforce that reflects the diversity of the communities we serve and to create an environment where everyone has the opportunity to thrive.

Providing an inclusive, accessible environment, where all employees and clients feel valued, respected and supported is something we're committed to. We are dedicated to building a workforce that reflects the diversity of the communities in which we live, and to creating an environment where every employee has the opportunity to reach their potential. If you require an accommodation or an alternative format of any posting please reach out to careers@peoplecorporation.com.

The base salary range for this position is $55,000 - $75,000 per annum.

This represents base salary only and does not represent other variable compensation components of our total compensation ( i.e. annual bonus, etc). The actual base salary will vary depending on local market conditions, geography and relevant job-related factors such as knowledge, skills, qualifications, experience, and education/training. If you are selected to move forward in our recruitment process, the Talent Acquisition Specialist will be able to provide additional details of the total compensation for this role.

Title: Continuous Improvement Analyst
Description:
S'more About The Team

Reporting to the Senior Manager, Operational Excellence (OpEx), you will support OpEx teams in delivering continuous improvement projects across distribution center operations, from defining opportunities, data analysis, solution design, implementation and process change management. As a part of the OpEx team, you will play a key role in improving our operational quality, efficiency & waste, and help us deliver the best possible value to our customers by developing a deep understanding of our processes, data and technology.

Lettuce Share What This Role Will Be Responsible For


Drive the Operational Excellence vision of continuously improving quality, efficiency and engagement
Quickly understand and communicate complex processes to others
Take ownership of core warehouse and production processes by driving standardization and continuous improvement
Act as a champion of site operations, working closely with stakeholders both on the shop floor and in the office alike
Leverage large internal datasets to drive decisions, identify and quantify opportunities, and prioritize to deliver improvements in cost & quality
Support root cause and failure mode & effects analyses to mitigate risks and prevent recurring issues related to product quality, pick and pack accuracy, minute-per-box efficiency, and waste
Support the development and delivery of communication and change management plans to relevant stakeholders to identify and remove project implementation risks and effectively launch and sustain operational changes
Create standard operating procedures, work instruction documentation and other visual management tools
Track and provide project progress updates in the OpEx Roadmap
Use personal protective and safety equipment and clothing as directed by the employer and report workplace hazards and dangers to the supervisor or employer.
Other duties, as assigned.


Sound a-peeling? Here's what we're looking for:


Bachelor’s degree in business, engineering, supply chain, or a similar field
2+ years of experience working in operations management or a related field
A natural curiosity to understand how different systems/processes work and a bias for action to improve them
A proactive and ambitious mindset: you take ownership of your work and think beyond the scope of a given assignment
A charismatic attitude to getting buy-in on your ideas from the design stage to implementation on the shop floor
Quantitative and analytical experience (either through education or professional experience), including expertise with G-Suite, Excel, and data visualization systems
Experience with AI tools is a plus
A willingness to travel within Canada up to 15% of your working time
You are highly interested in learning about or already have experience in:
Root Cause Analysis tools: 5 Whys, Fault Tree Analysis, 6W2H, etc.
Familiarity with Lean/Business Process Mapping
Basic coding and/or data visualization (e.g. SQL, Tableau). If you know how to write code and pull data, that’s awesome. If not, don’t worry as long as you have an interest in it and aptitude for learning it.

Let’s cut to the cheese, this is why you'll love it here:


Box Discount - Amazing discounts on 1 box per week! 75% discount on weekly HelloFresh and Chefs Plate meal kits AND 50% off weekly Factor meal box.
Health & Wellness - Health & Dental benefits from day 1, a Health Spending Account, unlimited access to the Headspace app to meet your self-care needs, and 25% discount on GoodLife fitness memberships!
RRSP Matching - Competitive RRSP matching program offered
Vacation & PTO - Time off is also an important part of self-care! We offer generous vacation and PTO to help you create a good work-life balance.
Family Benefits - A parental leave top-up program for expectant parents.
Growth & Development - We support your career progression, provide development opportunities, and invest in your continued learning through our organization wide L&D fund.
Work Hard & Have Fun - From team socials to engaging company days, you’ll have plenty of opportunity to experience the fun!
Diversity & Inclusion Initiatives - With impactful ERG’s like FreshPride, Women Empowered and LIMES, we are committed to our diversity, equity & inclusion efforts.
Food Puns - this one is kind of a big dill if you haven’t already noticed. We even have some punny meeting room names!


Working Conditions

It’s no surprise that as a food company, we use many ingredients that may be considered common allergens (i.e. peanuts, tree nuts, milk, etc.). Whether this role is based out of our headquarters or one of our distribution centers, it is possible that you may be exposed to such allergens in this role. If you have any concerns with being exposed to any particular food ingredients, including meat, please disclose this during the hiring process.

If this sounds like you, please click the "Apply Now" button. If this role doesn't quite match what you're looking for, but you'd like to know more about joining the HelloFresh team, please visit our Jobs page to view our other vacancies. We'd love to hear from you!

HelloFresh Canada uses AI-integrated technology to help us process and evaluate applications more efficiently. This includes tools that screen and assess candidate qualifications based on the requirements for this role. While these tools assist our workflow, all final selection decisions are made by our hiring team.

This is a posting for an existing vacancy. We are actively seeking to fill this position.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Are you a proactive Senior Data Analyst skilled in analytics, from data transformation to statistical analysis and machine learning? Do you thrive on solving problems and driving innovation? If you’re eager to turn insights into action and empower others, join us to shape the future of data-driven decision-making in a collaborative, forward-thinking team. In this key role, you'll work with business leaders and cross-functional teams to harness data, deliver actionable insights, and drive strategy, smarter decisions, and innovation.

We are currently hiring for a 12-month fixed-term opportunity within our Data Analytics Team.

We practice a remote first working model, leveraging in person interactions at our head office, in beautiful Vancouver, BC, for strategic, collaborative and social purposes.

Apply now and grow your career with Best Buy as a Senior Data Analyst.

What You’ll Do As a Senior Data Analyst


Be the go-to data expert for business decision support: Conduct problem research, deep-dive analysis, apply advanced analytics and ML techniques to uncover trends and insights, design high-impact reports and visuals to tell compelling data stories that inspire action and shape our offerings and operations.
Lead the way on strategic projects: Tackle complex challenges and enable data solutions using standard and advanced analytics, automation, business intelligence, opportunity analysis, forecasting, and simulations.
Innovate for tomorrow: Design and implement experiments that directly influence our customer experience, helping us stay ahead of the curve. Spot new analytics opportunities that propel our business forward, improve our practices to increase business outcomes and be the catalyst for positive change.
Build the tools of transformation: Help us get to the future by gradually developing and maintaining scalable reporting and machine learning models that empower business and tech teams with self-serve access to actionable descriptive and predictive insights.
Become a trusted advisor: Build a deep understanding of needs and goals of our stakeholders, help see possibilities, guide through complexity, influence decisions and develop data solutions to help business succeed.
Champion for growth and collaboration: Nurture the analytics community, help establish new standards, best practices and frameworks, mentor and inspire fellow analysts by sharing knowledge, peer reviews, and brainstorming sessions.


What We’re Looking For


A minimum of 4+ years of senior analytics experience executing enterprise analytics initiatives, managing data transformation, automation, analysis, reporting, ML simulations, and business insights in a retail setting.
Mastery of analytics tools and techniques to drive results using SQL (including optimization), Python (data prep and wrangling, analysis, visualization, ML, automation), and Power BI (data modeling, DAX, M, dashboard UX design).
Experience breaking down complex business problems into actionable data solutions using statistical methods, modeling, or machine learning.
Experience partnering with diverse stakeholders where you make complex data concepts clear and engaging for everyone from executives to frontline teams.


Why You’ll Love It Here


Remote-first work environment.
Employee discounts on awesome tech.
Flexible health benefits and wellness program.
Development opportunities to build new and transferable skills.


Join our amazing team!

We are looking for people just like you.

About Best Buy

As Canada's destination for the coolest tech, Best Buy offers one of the best work environments in the country! Our culture is one of inclusivity and belonging and provides employees with an environment where they can bring their whole selves to work.

Annualized Compensation Range

$95,000.00 - $105,000.00

Starting pay offered will consider skills and experience. Your pay can grow as you build your career with us.

Suite 102 - 425 West 6th Avenue, Vancouver, BC V5Y 1L3

This posting is for a vacancy.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
About Legend

At Legend, we're building next-level player experiences. With 20+ years of proven success we’re the world's leading Sports and Gaming marketing company.

Our people are our edge. 600+ sharp, ambitious Legends across 22+ countries who move fast, learn quickly and win together. Here growth isn’t a perk, it’s the standard. You’ll stretch, level-up and carve out a career as bold as the products we build. We celebrate wins and create experiences that people talk about for years - for our players & for each other. If you’re ready to shape the future of player experience and enjoy the ride while you do it, this is where you’ll thrive.

The Role

Legend is hiring a Senior Data Analyst.

This is a highly visible, hands-on role where you'll partner with teams across Product, Commercial, Marketing, SEO and Performance to deliver the insights that shape strategic business decisions. You'll own complex analytical projects from end-to-end, build advanced forecasting models, improve reporting capabilities and help evolve our analytics function. We're looking for someone who combines exceptional technical expertise with strong commercial acumen and the ability to influence stakeholders through data.

In this role, we value diverse perspectives and encourage you to apply even if you don't meet every qualification listed.

Your Impact


Deliver end-to-end analytical projects that help shape commercial, product and marketing decisions across our Sports portfolio.
Build advanced forecasting models that combine financial planning, historical performance, seasonality and sporting events to improve business forecasting.
Redesign and enhance our Tableau dashboard suite, providing stakeholders with faster, more actionable performance insights.
Improve the quality and accessibility of data by identifying gaps, optimising reporting and partnering with Data Engineering to strengthen data pipelines.
Apply statistical analysis, behavioural analytics and experimentation to uncover opportunities that drive revenue, conversion and long-term business growth.
Mentor team members while proactively identifying future analytical opportunities that help mature our Performance & Insights function.


What You'll Bring


Expert-level SQL alongside strong Python (preferred) or R, Tableau and statistical analysis skills.
Proven experience independently delivering end-to-end analytical projects from scoping through to implementation.
Strong stakeholder management skills with the ability to translate business challenges into analytical solutions and communicate technical concepts to non-technical audiences.
Commercial mindset with experience using data to influence business strategy and decision-making.
Experience mentoring or supporting team members while driving high standards across analytical delivery.
Experience within sports betting, iGaming or another digital business with strong web analytics exposure is highly desirable.


The Interview Process

1st: Initial Chat with Talent Partner (30 mins via Zoom)

2nd: Interview with our team, Hiring Manager and Senior People Partner (1 hour video via Zoom)

3rd: Take home task - you will be given a task to complete and return to us for assessment

4th: Final interview with our team, Hiring Manager and Sports Stakeholder (1 hour video via Zoom)

Why Legend


Super smart colleagues to work alongside and learn from.
Engaging development opportunities at all levels.
Tailored flexibility for your work-life balance.
Annual discretionary bonus to reward your efforts.
Paid annual leave PLUS a well-deserved break to recharge your batteries during the festive season! Our offices are closed between Christmas and New Year's, allowing you to enjoy downtime without dipping into your annual allowance.
Exciting global Legend events, where we unite in person to ignite our shared passion and unveil the exciting strategies for the year ahead!


Unlock your full potential by joining the Legend team. To support you on this journey, we provide an extensive array of benefits and perks, as outlined in our global offerings above. For country specific benefits please reach out to your talent partner.

Legend is an Equal Opportunity Employer, but that’s just the start. We believe different perspectives help us grow and achieve more. That’s why we’re dedicated to hiring and developing the most talented and diverse team- which includes individuals with different backgrounds, abilities, identities and experiences. If you require any reasonable adjustments throughout your application process, please speak to your Talent Partner or contact the team on  [email protected] , and we'll do all we can to support you.

Title: Evaluation and Measurement Analyst
Description:
Overview

At KPMG in Canada, our people bring their unique perspectives to Canada’s most important challenges. Here, you can build momentum that reaches beyond our business, develop skills for the future, and take ownership of your career with support at every stage. Join a firm where your career can make a difference.

The Training Evaluation & Measurement Analyst is responsible for assessing the effectiveness and impact of learning programs by supporting the design of measurement strategies, analyzing training data, and providing actionable insights to improve learning outcomes and business performance. This role works closely with course owners, SMEs, and stakeholders to ensure training delivers measurable value.

Data Collection & Analysis


Collect and validate data from multiple sources (e.g., LMS, surveys, assessments)
Analyze quantitative and qualitative data to identify trends, gaps, and improvement opportunities
Perform statistical analysis (e.g., pass rates, item difficulty, distractor analysis) to assess assessment quality


Reporting & Insights


Review dashboards and reports to communicate training performance and outcomes
Translate data into clear, actionable insights for course owners and stakeholders
Provide recommendations for course improvements based on findings


Continuous Improvement


Monitor training effectiveness throughout the lifecycle (pre-, during, and post-delivery)
Partner with SMEs and course owners to refine content, assessments, and delivery methods
Support post-course evaluations and follow-up analysis (e.g., behavior and performance impact)


What You Will Do

As a Training Evaluation & Measurement Analyst, you will play a key role in helping KPMG Business School understand and improve the effectiveness of its learning programs. Working closely with course owners, subject matter experts (SMEs), and other stakeholders, you will transform learning data into meaningful insights that drive better learning experiences and business outcomes.

In this role, you will


Collect, validate, and analyze data from multiple sources, including learning management systems, surveys and assessments.
Identify trends, patterns, learning gaps, and opportunities for improvement through quantitative and qualitative analysis.
Conduct measurement and evaluation activities, including assessment analysis, learner feedback reviews, pass rate analysis, item difficulty reviews, and other evaluation methodologies.
Maintain reporting dashboards and metrics to monitor learning effectiveness and learner performance.
Translate complex data into clear, actionable insights and recommendations for stakeholders and course owners.
Support the evaluation of learning initiatives throughout the training lifecycle, from pre-deployment through post-course analysis.
Collaborate with course owners and SMEs to identify opportunities to improve course content, assessments, learner engagement, and overall learning impact.
Contribute to the development and continuous improvement of evaluation frameworks, measurement strategies, and reporting standards.
Support the preparation and presentation of findings, recommendations, and evaluation results to various stakeholders.
Assist with special projects and initiatives related to learning analytics, measurement, and continuous improvement.


What You Bring To The Role

You are a curious and analytical professional who enjoys turning data into meaningful insights. You have a passion for learning and development and are motivated by the opportunity to improve learning experiences through evidence-based decision-making.

You bring


3 years experience in learning analytics, training evaluation, data analysis, research, or a related field.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to identify trends, interpret data, and make practical recommendations.
Experience working with data visualization and reporting tools such as Excel, Power BI, Tableau, or similar platforms.
Knowledge of learning measurement and evaluation methodologies, such as the Kirkpatrick Model or other evaluation frameworks, is an asset.
Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy and quality.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to present findings to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Ability to manage multiple priorities and work effectively in a fast-paced, collaborative environment.
Strong stakeholder management and relationship-building skills, with the ability to work across teams and influence decision-making through data-driven insights.
Experience working with Learning Management Systems (LMS) and learning technologies is considered an asset.
A continuous improvement mindset and a passion for enhancing learning outcomes through measurement and evaluation.


KPMG Ontario Region Pay Range Information

Overview

At KPMG in Canada, our people bring their unique perspectives to Canada’s most important challenges. Join a firm where your career can make a difference.

The Training Evaluation & Measurement Analyst is responsible for assessing the effectiveness and impact of learning programs by supporting the design of measurement strategies, analyzing training data, and providing actionable insights to improve learning outcomes and business performance. This role works closely with course owners, SMEs, and stakeholders to ensure training delivers measurable value.

Data Collection & Analysis

  • Collect and validate data from multiple sources (e.g., LMS, surveys, assessments)
  • Analyze quantitative and qualitative data to identify trends, gaps, and improvement opportunities
  • Perform statistical analysis (e.g., pass rates, item difficulty, distractor analysis) to assess assessment quality

Reporting & Insights

  • Review dashboards and reports to communicate training performance and outcomes
  • Translate data into clear, actionable insights for course owners and stakeholders
  • Provide recommendations for course improvements based on findings

Continuous Improvement

  • Monitor training effectiveness throughout the lifecycle (pre-, during, and post-delivery)
  • Partner with SMEs and course owners to refine content, assessments, and delivery methods
  • Support post-course evaluations and follow-up analysis (e.g., behavior and performance impact)

What You Will Do

As a Training Evaluation & Measurement Analyst, you will play a key role in helping KPMG Business School understand and improve the effectiveness of its learning programs. Working closely with course owners, subject matter experts (SMEs), and other stakeholders, you will transform learning data into meaningful insights that drive better learning experiences and business outcomes.

In this role, you will

  • Collect, validate, and analyze data from multiple sources, including learning management systems, surveys and assessments.
  • Identify trends, patterns, learning gaps, and opportunities for improvement through quantitative and qualitative analysis.
  • Conduct measurement and evaluation activities, including assessment analysis, learner feedback reviews, pass rate analysis, item difficulty reviews, and other evaluation methodologies.
  • Maintain reporting dashboards and metrics to monitor learning effectiveness and learner performance.
  • Translate complex data into clear, actionable insights and recommendations for stakeholders and course owners.
  • Support the evaluation of learning initiatives throughout the training lifecycle, from pre-deployment through post-course analysis.
  • Collaborate with course owners and SMEs to identify opportunities to improve course content, assessments, learner engagement, and overall learning impact.
  • Contribute to the development and continuous improvement of evaluation frameworks, measurement strategies, and reporting standards.
  • Support the preparation and presentation of findings, recommendations, and evaluation results to various stakeholders.
  • Assist with special projects and initiatives related to learning analytics, measurement, and continuous improvement.

What You Bring To The Role

You are a curious and analytical professional who enjoys turning data into meaningful insights. You have a passion for learning and development and are motivated by the opportunity to improve learning experiences through evidence-based decision-making.

You bring

  • 3 years experience in learning analytics, training evaluation, data analysis, research, or a related field.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to identify trends, interpret data, and make practical recommendations.
  • Experience working with data visualization and reporting tools such as Excel, Power BI, Tableau, or similar platforms.
  • Knowledge of learning measurement and evaluation methodologies, such as the Kirkpatrick Model or other evaluation frameworks, is an asset.
  • Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy and quality.
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to present findings to both technical and non-technical audiences.
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities and work effectively in a fast-paced, collaborative environment.
  • Strong stakeholder management and relationship-building skills, with the ability to work across teams and influence decision-making through data-driven insights.
  • Experience working with Learning Management Systems (LMS) and learning technologies is considered an asset.
  • A continuous improvement mindset and a passion for enhancing learning outcomes through measurement and evaluation.

Title: Methods Analyst I -ECOMB Methods Analyst
Description:
The Edmonton Police Service requires one (1) permanent full-time organized, detail-oriented, individual to provide analysis and call center reporting to the Data and Technology Unit of the Emergency Communications and Operations Management Branch. Responsibilities will include:


Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly reports and dashboards to support data driven decision making by the leadership team.
Produce ad-hoc reporting on-demand to support internal initiatives, projects, and investigations.
Lead the design, development, automation and maintenance of statistical data and information for a wide range of internal and external stakeholders.
Provide expert advice and guidance on statistical analytics and establish robust mechanisms to assess the effectiveness of the emergency call center.
Manage the Extraction, Translation, and Loading of data from various sources, including telephony and dispatch data, to serve the needs of Reporting and Analytics.
Develop and maintain Key Performance Indicators by applying statistical and mathematical modelling techniques to inform operational efficiency and effectiveness.
Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly reports and dashboards to support data driven decision making by the leadership team.
Present complex concepts and analyses to stakeholders leveraging sound written and verbal communication skills complemented by striking and informative visual storytelling.
Provide subject matter expertise regarding data literacy, use of reporting tools, statistical concepts, and practices.
Create and maintain process maps, documentation, and training material; deliver training to stakeholders as needed.
Liaise with internal and external partners to research and share best practices and methodologies.
Other related duties as required.



Qualifications:


University graduation in Criminology, Sociology and Applied Mathematics or other related fields of study.
A minimum of two (2) years directly related working experience is required.
Familiarity with Microsoft applications and services including Word, Excel, Access, Fabric, and Power BI.
Strong written and verbal communication skills, and ability to effectively communicate complex technical details to non-technical audiences.
Familiarity with Python, R, SQL-based query languages, DAX, and PowerShell languages an assets.
Experience providing analysis of metrics for call centers or public safety answering points (PSAP) would be an asset.
Strong organizational, interpersonal and time management skills.
Proven technical ability working with various computer software programs and databases.
Demonstrated self-initiative and the ability to work with minimal supervision.
Mature individual with the ability to adapt to change and work in a multidisciplinary team setting.
Experience transforming data within Snowflake would be an asset.



Hours of Work: 40 hours per week, Monday – Friday.

Salary Range: 21B, Salary Grade: 024, $41.17 - $51.44 (Hourly), $3,294.20 - $4,115.50 (Biweekly), $85,977.58 - $107,415.07 (Annually). The rates quoted are in accordance with a collective agreement between the Union and the City of Edmonton.

General:


Civic Service Union 52 members are asked to send a copy of their application to the union office.
The City of Edmonton thanks applicants for their interest in this opportunity. Candidates considered for the position will be contacted.
We are an equal opportunity employer. We welcome diversity and encourage applications from all qualified individuals.
This option to work remotely is not available at this time.
As part of your pre-hire screening, you will be required to complete an Enhanced Security Clearance. Should you accept employment with the Edmonton Police Service you acknowledge and agree to participate in future Enhanced Security Re-Clearances at scheduled intervals or in any other circumstances, exigent or otherwise, as required by Human Resources Division.
Applicants may be tested.



Applicants who have completed post-secondary education outside of Canada are encouraged to obtain an assessment of their credentials through the International Qualifications Assessment Service (IQAS) ( https://www.alberta.ca/international-qualifications-assessment.aspx) or another recognized Canadian credential evaluation service. For a list of designated evaluators, please visit the Alliance of Credential Evaluation Services of Canada ( https://canalliance.org/en/default.html ). Including an IQAS certificate or other credential assessment certificate from a recognized Canadian credential evaluation service with your application is recommended to support the evaluation of the applicant’s educational qualifications.

The Province of Alberta is a party to the Federal Agreement on Internal Trade, the Trade, Investment and Labour Mobility with British Columbia and the New West Partnership Trade Agreement with British Columbia and Saskatchewan. All of these agreements promote labour mobility between the Provinces. Applicants may obtain information regarding recognition of extra provincial credentials at www.newwestpartnershiptrade.ca .

Edmonton rests in the heart of Treaty Six territory in Alberta and the homelands of the Metis Nation.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
At the Trudell Medical Group of Companies our vision is to provide patients throughout the world with medical devices and services that make their lives better. The Group manufactures and globally markets some of the leading brands in respiratory care, including the AeroChamber® brand of valved holding chambers, the Aerobika® brand of OPEP devices, and the AeroEclipse® brand of nebulizers. Trudell Medical Limited is the Corporate entity within the Trudell Medical Group of Companies and operates out of our offices in London, Ontario Canada.

Data Analyst is a role in our Corporate IT team.


Must be available to work 4 days/week in office (Monday - Thursday) with the option to work from home Friday.


Your success in this role will be grounded in your values as a person who always acts with integrity and who treats everyone with respect. You continuously improve the systems and processes around you, and you take accountability for your work. You thrive in a team environment where you are open to learning from others, adjust to others’ styles, and drive hard to reach shared objectives.

You have a degree of post-secondary diploma in Data Analytics, Business Information Systems, Computer Science, or a related field.

Your Skills


Experience in data analysis, business intelligence, reporting, or similar analytical roles.
Proficiency with Microsoft Excel and data analysis techniques.
Experience with Power BI report development
Knowledge of Microsoft Fabric and Power Platform
Ability to gather requirements and translation business needs into analytical, reporting, automation, or low-code solutions.
Strong problem-solving, critical thinking, and communication skills.
Experience with SQL, relational databases, or querying data from enterprise systems.


As part of the Corporate IT team, you will support data driven decision making through reporting, analysis, and dashboard development. You will work closely with business stakeholders to gather requirements, analyze data, produce actionable insights, and develop reporting solutions to improve visibility into operational and strategic performance.

Specifically, You Will Focus On

Data Analysis and Reporting


Collect, clean, transform, and analyze data from multiple business systems and data sources
Develop recurring reports to support operational, financial, and strategic decision-making.
Identify trends, patterns, risks, and opportunities through data analysis.
Translation business requirements into meaningful KPIs and dashboards.
Clearly present insights to both technical and non-technical audiences.


Dashboard Development


Design, build, and maintain dashboards and reports using Power BI
Support the development and use of Microsoft Fabric workspaces, semantic models, dataflows, lakehouse, pipelines, and other analytics assets where applicable.
Assist with data preparation, transformation, and modeling within Microsoft Fabric and Power BI.
Create and maintain data models, measures, and visualizations that support business performance monitoring.
Support the development of self-service analytics tools for business users.
Work with stakeholders to continuously improve reporting, usability, adoption, performance, and accuracy.


Power Platform & Process Improvement


Use Power Automate to streamline repetitive reporting, notification, approval, and data collection processes
Support the creation and maintenance of Power Apps to improve data gathering.
Partner with business teams to identify opportunities where Power Platform tools can improve productivity


Trudell Medical Limited has been named one of Canada’s Best Managed Companies each year since 2022. The award is a mark of excellence for privately-owned Canadian companies.

Trudell Medical Limited is committed to providing fair and equitable compensation opportunities to all teammates. The range displayed on each job posting reflects the target for new hire salaries for the advertised position. Within the range, individual compensation is determined by job-related skills and knowledge, relevant professional, lived experience, and/or work location. Our compensation policies and practices are designed to allow our colleagues’ growth and salary progression as they develop in their role. As a candidate, you are encouraged to ask compensation-related questions and have an open dialogue with your recruiter, who can provide specific details for this role and share more information about our total rewards package.

As part of our recruitment process, we may use AI tools to screen applicants and assist in evaluating candidate qualifications. All final hiring decisions are made by our recruitment team.

We are an equal opportunity employer and it is important to our Company that all its employees, including those with disabilities, find our workplace to be welcoming and supportive. If you are a candidate with a disability who requires accommodations during the recruitment process, please let us know.

Title: Intermediate Mining Data Analyst
Description:
Be part of something incredible. Metals are necessary for our world, but mining historically faced issues with waste, sustainability, and inefficiencies. MineSense is changing that. Our solutions reduce mining waste and environmental impacts while increasing profits and efficiencies. Backed by the best in venture capital and government funds, and recognized by tech industry and sustainability programs, MineSense is doing what many said could not be done: improving mining for people and planet.







We are looking to add an Intermediate Mining Data Analyst to our GeoScience team. The role will report to the Manager, GeoScience and be based out of the Vancouver office. The ideal candidate will have a strong interest in detailed data analysis and statistics within the mining industry. You will work with clients globally, to incorporate MineSense solutions into their ore control processes and show them the value of using our systems. If you enjoy working with data, collaborating with multidisciplinary groups, and have a mining, geology, or geophysics background, we want to hear from you!







Job Responsibilities:



Helping new and existing clients leverage MineSense technology in their grade control engineering processes, which includes developing client facing reports and presentations.


Collaborating with internal teams to develop prediction models by correlating mine grade measurements or estimates with our sensor results to identify patterns, trends or other data features that relate to grade, texture, mineralogy, or lithology.


Working with the Field Service and Product Development team to further enhance our products and services through system data analysis and data quality assurance/control.


Determining and tracking data model accuracy and precision through comparison with sampled assay grades or grade estimates.


Reporting and presenting data results/trends to internal teams, as well as external clients.


Working closely with the Data team to create queries, scripts, and reports while ensuring data is well organized and maintained.


Building and maintaining good working relationships with our clients.


Promotes a positive company culture through sharing of information and supporting the learning and development of all colleagues.


Additional duties as occasionally required by your manager.









Qualifications:



Degree in Earth Sciences (Geology, Geophysics), Geostatistics, Data Analytics, Mining Engineering or related field.


Minimum of 2-4 years of relevant working experience, where experience in mining is preferred.


Experience performing statistical analysis or data science in a work setting is preferred.


Mining experience with in-grade control or working with ore control models is preferred.


Experience with data analysis tools such as python or other statistical packages.


Experience with SQL is preferred.


Experience and passion for solving analytical problems involving diverse data sets using quantitative approaches to generate insights.


Attention to detail and an interest in problem solving.


Oral and written proficiency in English is required.


Additional oral and written proficiency in Spanish is strongly preferred.


The ability to travel and support global offices is required (10-30%).


Legally able to work in Canada.












The salary offered to the successful candidate will be based on their experience, knowledge and skills they bring to MineSense, market data based on their location as well as internal equity. Keeping this mind, the salary offered can be outside of the range posted. 

 

Our total rewards package offers market-researched benefits, promotes internal equity, and supports team members at all levels of our organization, and includes the following:  

 

Our total rewards package includes: 

·  A competitive salary and bonus.

·  Matching RRSP or 401K program (for Canadian and US employees)

·  A comprehensive benefit program including an employee assistance program. 

·  Learning and career development programs and resources (live sessions and on-demand)

·  Transit Subsidy (for roles based in Vancouver, BC)

·  Hybrid work (for roles that do not require to be onsite)

·  3 weeks' vacation or more depending on the position

·  Global and local company organized social events and celebrations to connect with your team

 

Here's what you can expect as a part of the MineSense Team: 

 

When you join MineSense, a trailblazing Canadian mining technology company, you become a part of our purpose to maximize global metals and minimize global impacts. We’re not just a company; we’re a Community of Innovators who forge a path toward a sustainable future. Here’s why we want you to be excited: 




Purpose-Driven: At MineSense, we don’t just clock in—we live our purpose. We’re on a mission to revolutionize the mining industry, and every day, we’re one step closer to a cleaner, greener world. 

Values That Resonate: Our core values—Innovation, Belonging, Resilience, and Adding Value—aren’t just buzzwords. They’re the bedrock of our culture. When you step into our team, you’re not just an employee; you’re a steward of these values. 

Impactful Work: Imagine contributing to a cleaner, more sustainable planet. At MineSense, you’re shaping the future of cleantech and developing cutting-edge technology that literally hasn't been developed before. Your work matters, and it echoes beyond our walls. 

People-Centric: We’re not just about profits; we’re about people. Our organization cares deeply about its employees. When you join us, you become part of a community that supports growth, well-being, and success. 

 

Ready to make your mark? Join MineSense and be part of something bigger than yourself. 

  

MineSense is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to providing accommodation to individuals with disabilities. Should you require accommodation during the recruitment or hiring process, please feel free to indicate your needs while applying. Information received related to accommodation needs of applicants will be addressed confidentially. 

  

All applications are welcome, even if you don't meet all the requirements, so please do not hesitate to apply. 

 







We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

If you enjoy working with data, collaborating with multidisciplinary groups, and have a mining, geology, or geophysics background, we want to hear from you!



Job Responsibilities:

  • Helping new and existing clients leverage MineSense technology in their grade control engineering processes, which includes developing client facing reports and presentations.

  • Collaborating with internal teams to develop prediction models by correlating mine grade measurements or estimates with our sensor results to identify patterns, trends or other data features that relate to grade, texture, mineralogy, or lithology.

  • Working with the Field Service and Product Development team to further enhance our products and services through system data analysis and data quality assurance/control.

  • Determining and tracking data model accuracy and precision through comparison with sampled assay grades or grade estimates.

  • Reporting and presenting data results/trends to internal teams, as well as external clients.

  • Working closely with the Data team to create queries, scripts, and reports while ensuring data is well organized and maintained.

  • Building and maintaining good working relationships with our clients.

  • Promotes a positive company culture through sharing of information and supporting the learning and development of all colleagues.

  • Additional duties as occasionally required by your manager.




Qualifications:

  • Degree in Earth Sciences (Geology, Geophysics), Geostatistics, Data Analytics, Mining Engineering or related field.

  • Minimum of 2-4 years of relevant working experience, where experience in mining is preferred.

  • Experience performing statistical analysis or data science in a work setting is preferred.

  • Mining experience with in-grade control or working with ore control models is preferred.

  • Experience with data analysis tools such as python or other statistical packages.

  • Experience with SQL is preferred.

  • Experience and passion for solving analytical problems involving diverse data sets using quantitative approaches to generate insights.

  • Attention to detail and an interest in problem solving.

  • Oral and written proficiency in English is required.

  • Additional oral and written proficiency in Spanish is strongly preferred.

  • The ability to travel and support global offices is required (10-30%).

  • Legally able to work in Canada.





The salary offered to the successful candidate will be based on their experience, knowledge and skills they bring to MineSense, market data based on their location as well as internal equity. Keeping this mind, the salary offered can be outside of the range posted. 

 

Our total rewards package offers market-researched benefits, promotes internal equity, and supports team members at all levels of our organization, and includes the following:  

 

Our total rewards package includes: 

·  A competitive salary and bonus.

·  Matching RRSP or 401K program (for Canadian and US employees)

·  A comprehensive benefit program including an employee assistance program. 

·  Learning and career development programs and resources (live sessions and on-demand)

·  Transit Subsidy (for roles based in Vancouver, BC)

·  Hybrid work (for roles that do not require to be onsite)

·  3 weeks' vacation or more depending on the position

·  Global and local company organized social events and celebrations to connect with your team

When you join MineSense, a trailblazing Canadian mining technology company, you become a part of our purpose to maximize global metals and minimize global impacts. We’re not just a company; we’re a Community of Innovators who forge a path toward a sustainable future. Here’s why we want you to be excited: 


Purpose-Driven: At MineSense, we don’t just clock in—we live our purpose. We’re on a mission to revolutionize the mining industry, and every day, we’re one step closer to a cleaner, greener world. 

Values That Resonate: Our core values—Innovation, Belonging, Resilience, and Adding Value—aren’t just buzzwords. They’re the bedrock of our culture. When you step into our team, you’re not just an employee; you’re a steward of these values.

Title: Business Analyst (Asset Management focus)
Description:
Halifax Regional Municipality (HRM) is inviting applications for two position for Business Analyst (Asset Management focus) in Finance & Asset Management.




Halifax Regional Municipality (HRM) is committed to reflecting the community it serves & recognizing Diversity & Inclusion as one of the Municipality’s core values. We have an Employment Equity Policy & we welcome applications from African Nova Scotians & Other Racially Visible Persons, Women in occupations or positions where they are underrepresented in the workforce, Indigenous/Aboriginal People, Persons with Disabilities & 2SLGBTQ+ Persons. Applicants are encouraged to self-identify on their electronic application.




Reporting to the Manager Asset Management Systems, the Business Analyst is responsible for monitoring and improving the quality of data stored in the asset management systems, collecting new and updated data, and providing advice and support to end users.




Working as part of the Asset Management Systems team, the Analyst works closely with teams throughout the municipality to provide dedicated support to capital projects, operational activities, and data collection exercises. They are trusted partners that link the day-to-day operations of the municipality with the long-term strategic direction of the Asset Management policy, helping ensure that municipal assets are well managed.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:




Business Unit Partnerships

Develop relationships within operational business units to share asset management knowledge, stay aware of asset-related initiatives, and proactively advance asset management priorities.




Asset Data Management

Evaluate existing asset data using criteria for completeness, quality, and currency
Produce and distribute standardized reports outlining the quality of the asset data and highlighting priority areas for improvement
Investigate and correct discrepancies and errors in the data
Assist in the development of new or changed asset data types
Keep current with new developments in the asset management systems, asset management practices, and GIS tools
Collaborate with the Asset Management team and management in the establishment of long-term strategic directions for asset data collection




Asset Data Collection

Provide technical and process assistance to data collection exercises led by other departments
Lead data collection exercises carried out by the Asset Management Office, including collection of data from existing sources and in the field
Evaluate technical options for data collection and make recommendations




System Configuration

Provide technical advice on the configuration of the asset management systems in relation to the GIS
Liaise with the Information Technology department to evaluate proposed changes to the GIS and advise on impacts on the asset management systems




Process Management & Documentation

Evaluate existing processes related to the management of asset data and provide advice and recommendations on improvements
Develop new processes as required
Document new and existing processes
Work closely with employees at all levels of the organization to identify opportunities for improvements
Monitor and report on the outcome of processes




Training & Support

Provide training to users of the asset management systems. This may be as informal one-on-one training or structured group sessions
Create training materials
Respond to user inquiries and problem reports
May perform other related duties as assigned




QUALIFICATIONS

Education & Experience

Diploma or Degree at the post-secondary level in a discipline related to spatial data, asset management, or public administration.
3+ years of experience in managing spatial data and performing/supporting data collection
3+ years experience developing reports using tools such as Crystal Reports, Excel, or Power BI
3+ years experience providing customer service and support to non-technical users
Some experience with building strong relationships with stakeholders including senior management, clients, and technical staff
Translating business needs into technical requirements and solutions
Performing education / training
Some experience with process design, development, and implementation
Basic project management skills
General knowledge and some formal training in Business Analysis Skills and Asset Management is preferred




Technical/Job Specific Knowledge and Abilities

Proficiency in Esri tools and technology
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel
Good understanding of reporting and presentation tools
Good understanding of general data management practices
General understanding of business process analysis

Security Clearance Requirements: Applicants may be required to complete employment security screening check




Please note–Testing may be conducted as a component of the selection process to assess technical & job specific knowledge. Candidates who are selected for testing may be tested in a group setting scheduled at the employer’s discretion.




Competencies - Valuing Diversity, Analytical Thinking, Communications ,Decision Making ,Managing Change ,Organizational Awareness ,Risk Management ,Teamwork & Cooperation ,Value & Ethics ,Visioning, Strategic Thinking & Innovation




WORK STATUS: Permanent, Full time




Hours of Work: Monday to Friday, 8:30 am-4:30 pm 35 hours/week




SALARY: Non-Union,NU6 ($73,094-$94,587). Salary will be determined based on relevant experience and qualifications related to the specified pay band and job. Placement above the midpoint of the pay band is typically reserved for candidates who significantly exceed the minimum qualifications, such as those with exceptional experience, specialized skills, or demonstrated achievements relevant to the role. This ensures fair and consistent compensation practices across the organization.




WORK LOCATION: 40 Alderney Drive, Dartmouth




CLOSING DATE: Applications will be received up to 11:59pm on Tuesday, July 21, 2026.




Please note: We thank all applicants for their interest in this position. Only those applicants selected for interview/testing will be contacted.




To ensure a fair and equitable hiring process, candidates are expected to complete all parts of the recruitment process, including assessments, assignments, and interviews independently and without the use of artificial intelligence (AI) tools or other forms of external assistance. The use of AI to complete any component of the hiring process is not permitted and may result in disqualification from consideration.




Shortlisted candidates will be required to provide proof of their qualifications, including, but not limited to, educational credentials, professional licenses, and relevant certifications.




During the recruitment process, applicants have the right to request an accommodation. Applicants invited to participate in an assessment process (such as interview or testing) and who require an accommodation, should discuss their needs with the Talent Acquisition Advisor when invited to the assessment process. For more information on our accommodation process please click on the link; Accommodations | Hiring | Employment | Halifax.

Title: Evaluation and Measurement Analyst
Description:
Overview

At KPMG in Canada, our people bring their unique perspectives to Canada’s most important challenges. Here, you can build momentum that reaches beyond our business, develop skills for the future, and take ownership of your career with support at every stage. Join a firm where your career can make a difference.

The Training Evaluation & Measurement Analyst is responsible for assessing the effectiveness and impact of learning programs by supporting the design of measurement strategies, analyzing training data, and providing actionable insights to improve learning outcomes and business performance. This role works closely with course owners, SMEs, and stakeholders to ensure training delivers measurable value.

Data Collection & Analysis


Collect and validate data from multiple sources (e.g., LMS, surveys, assessments)
Analyze quantitative and qualitative data to identify trends, gaps, and improvement opportunities
Perform statistical analysis (e.g., pass rates, item difficulty, distractor analysis) to assess assessment quality


Reporting & Insights


Review dashboards and reports to communicate training performance and outcomes
Translate data into clear, actionable insights for course owners and stakeholders
Provide recommendations for course improvements based on findings


Continuous Improvement


Monitor training effectiveness throughout the lifecycle (pre-, during, and post-delivery)
Partner with SMEs and course owners to refine content, assessments, and delivery methods
Support post-course evaluations and follow-up analysis (e.g., behavior and performance impact)


What You Will Do

As a Training Evaluation & Measurement Analyst, you will play a key role in helping KPMG Business School understand and improve the effectiveness of its learning programs. Working closely with course owners, subject matter experts (SMEs), and other stakeholders, you will transform learning data into meaningful insights that drive better learning experiences and business outcomes.

In this role, you will


Collect, validate, and analyze data from multiple sources, including learning management systems, surveys and assessments.
Identify trends, patterns, learning gaps, and opportunities for improvement through quantitative and qualitative analysis.
Conduct measurement and evaluation activities, including assessment analysis, learner feedback reviews, pass rate analysis, item difficulty reviews, and other evaluation methodologies.
Maintain reporting dashboards and metrics to monitor learning effectiveness and learner performance.
Translate complex data into clear, actionable insights and recommendations for stakeholders and course owners.
Support the evaluation of learning initiatives throughout the training lifecycle, from pre-deployment through post-course analysis.
Collaborate with course owners and SMEs to identify opportunities to improve course content, assessments, learner engagement, and overall learning impact.
Contribute to the development and continuous improvement of evaluation frameworks, measurement strategies, and reporting standards.
Support the preparation and presentation of findings, recommendations, and evaluation results to various stakeholders.
Assist with special projects and initiatives related to learning analytics, measurement, and continuous improvement.


What You Bring To The Role

You are a curious and analytical professional who enjoys turning data into meaningful insights. You have a passion for learning and development and are motivated by the opportunity to improve learning experiences through evidence-based decision-making.

You bring


3 years experience in learning analytics, training evaluation, data analysis, research, or a related field.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to identify trends, interpret data, and make practical recommendations.
Experience working with data visualization and reporting tools such as Excel, Power BI, Tableau, or similar platforms.
Knowledge of learning measurement and evaluation methodologies, such as the Kirkpatrick Model or other evaluation frameworks, is an asset.
Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy and quality.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to present findings to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Ability to manage multiple priorities and work effectively in a fast-paced, collaborative environment.
Strong stakeholder management and relationship-building skills, with the ability to work across teams and influence decision-making through data-driven insights.
Experience working with Learning Management Systems (LMS) and learning technologies is considered an asset.
A continuous improvement mindset and a passion for enhancing learning outcomes through measurement and evaluation.


KPMG Ontario Region Pay Range Information

The expected base salary range for this position is $55,000 to $82,000 and may be eligible for bonus awards. The determination of an applicant’s base salary within this range is based on the individual’s location, skills & competencies, and unique qualifications. In addition, KPMG offers a comprehensive and competitive Total Rewards program.

KPMG BC Region Pay Range Information

The expected base salary range for this position is $55,000 to $82,000 and may be eligible for bonus awards. The determination of an applicant’s base salary within this range is based on the individual’s location, skills & competencies, and unique qualifications. In addition, KPMG offers a comprehensive and competitive Total Rewards program.

Integrity, we do what is right | Excellence, we never stop learning and improving | Courage, we think and act boldly | Together, we respect each other and draw strength from our differences | For Better, we do what matters

KPMG in Canada is a proud equal opportunities employer and we are committed to creating a respectful, inclusive and barrier-free workplace that allows all of our people to reach their full potential. A diverse workforce is key to our success and we believe in bringing your whole self to work. We welcome all qualified candidates to apply and hope you will choose KPMG in Canada as your employer of choice.

Adjustments and accommodations throughout the recruitment process

At KPMG, we are committed to fostering an inclusive recruitment process where all candidates can be themselves and excel. We aim to provide a positive experience and are prepared to offer adjustments or accommodations to help you perform at your best. Adjustments (informal requests), such as extra preparation time or the option for micro breaks during interviews, and accommodations (formal requests), such as accessible communication supports or technology aids, are tailored to individual needs and role requirements. You will have an opportunity to request an adjustment or accommodation at any point throughout the recruitment process. If you require support, please contact KPMG’s Employee Relations Service team by calling 1-888-466-4778.

AI Usage

We embrace the use of artificial intelligence (AI) to enhance the candidate experience and streamline our recruitment processes. AI tools may help with organizing applications or surfacing relevant qualifications. However, no hiring decisions are made using AI. Every hiring decision is made by our hiring managers and recruitment professionals, who are equipped with training that empowers them to use these tools responsibly. AI technologies used in our recruitment process undergo detailed risk assessments, including security and privacy requirements, that align with KPMG’s Trusted AI framework.

We believe technology should empower human judgment, not replace it. It’s one of the many ways we’re delivering on our vision of being a technology-first, people-driven firm.

Title: Business Intelligence Engineer, IT PnD Data and Analytics
Description:
Description

IT PnD Data and Analytics is seeking a Business Intelligence Engineer to join our BI team.

As a Business Intelligence Engineer with our team, you will be working in a large, complex and dynamic, data-driven environment. You will be responsible for developing automated metrics that provide relevant insight to organizational leaders, building and maintaining dashboards, and performing complex data analytics.

This position requires the candidate to develop a deep understanding of the organization and key goals, earn trust with senior leadership, influence without authority, and provide relevant data insights.

To succeed, you must have the ability to engage deeply with diverse and talented business partners, earn trust using facts and data, and advise and influence decisions of senior business leaders through effective verbal and written communication.

This candidate must possess excellent analytical skills, project management experience, and a track record of delivering results in a complex and ambiguous environment.

Key job responsibilities


Perform complex business analysis to help drive organizational decision-making
Complete root cause analysis and provide recommendations to senior leadership to address opportunities
Develop automated metrics that will give senior business leaders insight into the performance of their respective teams and the organization as a whole
Develop and maintain Tableau dashboards
Develop key metrics and shared goals that determine success for a particular area
Take on shared ownership of the weekly organizational metrics scorecard


A day in the life

A day in the life of a BI engineer at IT PnD involves building large-scale reporting solutions using tools like Tableau and QuickSight. The day typically starts with a team stand-up to align on priorities, followed by deep work on developing and refining data transformation, building reports and automating processes. Collaboration with data scientists, analysts, and stakeholders is key to ensure the delivery of right metrics. The day ends with monitoring, code reviews, and documenting progress, all while keeping scalability, efficiency, and Amazon’s high standards for data quality in mind.

Basic Qualifications


3+ years of analyzing and interpreting data with Redshift, Oracle, NoSQL etc. experience
Experience with data visualization using Tableau, Quicksight, or similar tools
Experience with data modeling, warehousing and building ETL pipelines
Experience writing complex SQL queries
Experience in Statistical Analysis packages such as R, SAS and Matlab
Experience using SQL to pull data from a database or data warehouse and scripting experience (Python) to process data for modeling


Preferred Qualifications


Experience in the data/BI space
Experience in analyzing and interpreting data with Redshift, Oracle, NoSQL etc.

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. Description

IT PnD Data and Analytics is seeking a Business Intelligence Engineer to join our BI team.

As a Business Intelligence Engineer with our team, you will be working in a large, complex and dynamic, data-driven environment. You will be responsible for developing automated metrics that provide relevant insight to organizational leaders, building and maintaining dashboards, and performing complex data analytics.

This position requires the candidate to develop a deep understanding of the organization and key goals, earn trust with senior leadership, influence without authority, and provide relevant data insights.

To succeed, you must have the ability to engage deeply with diverse and talented business partners, earn trust using facts and data, and advise and influence decisions of senior business leaders through effective verbal and written communication.

This candidate must possess excellent analytical skills, project management experience, and a track record of delivering results in a complex and ambiguous environment.

Key job responsibilities

  • Perform complex business analysis to help drive organizational decision-making
  • Complete root cause analysis and provide recommendations to senior leadership to address opportunities
  • Develop automated metrics that will give senior business leaders insight into the performance of their respective teams and the organization as a whole
  • Develop and maintain Tableau dashboards
  • Develop key metrics and shared goals that determine success for a particular area
  • Take on shared ownership of the weekly organizational metrics scorecard

A day in the life

A day in the life of a BI engineer at IT PnD involves building large-scale reporting solutions using tools like Tableau and QuickSight. The day typically starts with a team stand-up to align on priorities, followed by deep work on developing and refining data transformation, building reports and automating processes. Collaboration with data scientists, analysts, and stakeholders is key to ensure the delivery of right metrics.   • Experience with data modeling, warehousing and building ETL pipelines
  • Experience writing complex SQL queries
  • Experience in Statistical Analysis packages such as R, SAS and Matlab
  • Experience using SQL to pull data from a database or data warehouse and scripting experience (Python) to process data for modeling

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience in the data/BI space
  • Experience in analyzing and interpreting data with Redshift, Oracle, NoSQL etc.

Job ID: A10445925

Title: Inventory Analyst
Description:
Reporting to the Merchandise Manager, the Inventory Analyst is responsible for ensuring inventory is in the right place, at the right time, to maximize sales, maintain inventory health, improve inventory productivity, and support an exceptional customer experience across all channels. This role owns retail allocations, inventory movement, purchase order allocation, and inventory reporting while partnering closely with Merchandising, Planning, Retail, Wholesale and Operations to drive inventory performance.




Must be based in Vancouver, BC

Hybrid: 3 days in office, 2 days remote

Salary band: $65,000 - $80,000 CAD




Core Responsibilities

Allocation & Inventory Management

Own the day-to-day health of inventory across all channels, proactively identifying stock imbalances, inventory risks and opportunities, and taking action to optimize inventory productivity.
Execute daily and weekly inventory allocations across retail stores to maximize sales and inventory productivity.
Monitor store inventory levels, sell-through and weeks of supply to ensure inventory is balanced across the retail channel.
Identify opportunities to rebalance inventory through inter-store, cross-channel and country transfers.
Support store grading and size-level allocations to ensure inventory is distributed effectively.

Inventory Operations

Manage oversell reporting and coordinate timely resolution with cross-functional teams.
Execute inventory transfers between Canada and the US to support demand across all channels.
Manage country splits on purchase orders, ensuring inventory is allocated accurately prior to receipt.
Maintain inventory accuracy by investigating discrepancies and partnering with Operations to resolve issues.
Support inventory reconciliations and ongoing inventory health initiatives.

Inventory Analysis & Reporting

Produce weekly inventory reporting highlighting key risks, opportunities and recommendations.
Develop and maintain inventory health dashboards, tracking key metrics across retail, wholesale and ecommerce.
Monitor inventory productivity, stock availability, sell-through and weeks of supply across all channels.
Analyze sales and inventory trends to make recommendations that improve allocation decisions.
Support seasonal inventory forecasting and pre-season allocation planning.
Provide reporting and insights to support merchandising, planning and executive decision-making.

Cross-Functional Partnership

Partner closely with Merchandising, Planning, Retail, Wholesale, Operations and Logistics to execute inventory strategies.
Communicate inventory risks, opportunities and recommendations proactively.
Support new store openings, wholesale launches, promotional events and other cross-functional initiatives.
Continuously look for opportunities to improve inventory processes, reporting and operational efficiencies.

Key Traits & Experience We're Looking For
Bachelor's degree in Business, Commerce or a related field.
3–5 years of experience in allocation, inventory management, merchandise planning or a related retail role.
Strong understanding of retail inventory management, allocation principles and retail math.
Advanced Excel skills with the ability to analyze large datasets and translate data into actionable insights.
Experience working with ERP and inventory management systems is considered an asset.
Highly organized with exceptional attention to detail and accuracy.
A proactive problem solver who enjoys identifying opportunities and continuously improving processes.
Able to prioritize multiple competing deadlines in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.
Strong communication skills with the ability to build collaborative relationships across cross-functional teams.
Curious, analytical and driven by using data to make informed business decisions.
