Title: Data Science Officer
Description:
Information Technology

Manitoba Hydro is consistently recognized as one of Manitoba's Top Employers! We are a leader among energy companies in North America, recognized for providing highly reliable service and exceptional customer satisfaction. Join our team of Manitoba's best as we continue to build a company that champions safety, supports innovation, and delivers on our commitment to customer service - while actively fostering a diverse, equitable, and inclusive workplace reflective of the communities we serve.

Great Benefits


Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits package.
Defined-benefit pension plan for long-term financial security.
Nine-day work cycle, typically resulting in every other Monday off to support a balanced approach to work, family life and community.


Position Overview

Reporting to the Enterprise Data & Analytics Lead, the Data Science Officer is responsible for planning, executing, and delivering data science and advanced analytics solutions by modeling complex business problems through statistical, algorithmic, mining, and visualization techniques. The focus of this role will be in machine learning (ML), artificial intelligence (AI), modelling and associated data solution development, utilizing and enabling hypothesis-based problem analysis, data exploration and preparation, data collection and integration, and operationalization for both everyday AI (optimization) and future AI (innovation). Further, this role will support business leaders by creating business insights, reports, and analyses to aid in the decision-making process.

The Data Science Officer is expected to be visionary and strategic with an ability to turn innovative ideas for the utility industry into real business value through an open mindset. The Data Science Officer also provides technical leadership in the development of enterprise policies and strategies to leverage ML and AI to achieve business outcomes. There is a clear expectation that ML and AI solutions will be developed and deployed using explainable, responsible and ethical practices to ensure these technologies are used appropriately and to effectively manage associated risks and biases. Success also hinges on the ability to collaborate effectively with a diverse range of stakeholders including other Data Scientists, Data Engineers, Data Developers, Data Architects, Enterprise Architecture, Cyber Security, and Cloud Platform teams, to design and implement robust, secure, data science and advanced analytics solutions.

Responsibilities


Designs and conducts data analyses with the highest standard of rigor and scientific accuracy, including study design, methodology, algorithms, and statistical modeling
Performs large-scale experimentation and builds data-driven models to identify hidden relationships between variables in large datasets and to answer business questions
Proactively mines data sources to identify trends and patterns and generates insights for business stakeholders and senior leadership
Leads projects that implement complex ML and AI solutions, leveraging complex and advanced tools and techniques
Anticipates internal customers' short and long-term needs by proactively identifying Machine Learning (ML) and Artificial Intelligence (AI) opportunities; innovates for utility industry solutions and shares these opportunities with business stakeholders to validate relevance and value
Collaborates with business stakeholders, providing consultative advice and expertise to translate complex business needs into analytics requirements to support business decisions
Develops and maintains strong working relationships while growing utility industry knowledge to assess longer-term more strategic needs of internal customers across the organization
Grows knowledge through developing working relationships with industry and academic contacts to research classical and cutting-edge techniques and tools in machine learning, deep learning, artificial intelligence, statistical analysis, and visualization techniques and keep abreast of industry best practices
Documents and mentors team members in guidelines and standards, ensuring process alignment, shares technical expertise, provides training, performs code reviews, directs the execution of their tasks, and provides feedback on their technical performance
Partners with D&T and business stakeholders to develop ML and AI policies and strategies
Delivers formal presentations to internal business stakeholders at various levels including executives


Qualifications


A four year degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics, Artificial Intelligence, Applied Mathematics, or a related quantitative field from a university of recognized standing with a minimum of five years general IT experience, including three years of directly applicable Data & Analytics programming experience launching, planning, and executing data science projects, including statistical analysis, data engineering, and data visualization


Or


A two year diploma in Data Science, Statistics, Artificial Intelligence, or a related quantitative field with a minimum of seven years general IT experience, including three years of directly applicable Data & Analytics programming experience launching, planning, and executing data science projects, including statistical analysis, data engineering, and data visualization


Or


Alternate experience and education in equivalent areas such as economics, engineering, or physics is acceptable; experience in more than one area is strongly preferred
A specialization in ML, AI, cognitive science or data science is preferred
Microsoft and/or Databricks certifications in AI and ML preferred
Fluency in multiple programming languages and statistical analysis tools such as Python, Jupyter Notebook, C++, JavaScript, R, Scala, SAS, Excel, SQL, MATLAB, SPSS
Experience with relational database programming languages including SQL and PL/SQL as well as nonrelational databases such as NoSQL/Hadoop-oriented databases including MongoDB, Cassandra, etc.
Knowledge of distributed data/computing tools such as Spark, MapReduce, Hadoop, Hive, or Kafka
Experience working across multiple deployment environments including cloud, on-premises, and hybrid, and multiple operating systems and containerization techniques such as Docker, Kubernetes, Azure, etc.
Strong understanding of AI domains such as ML, Generative AI, Optimization, Graphs, and Simulation, and their potential roles in solving business problems such as prediction/forecasting, planning, computer vision, recommendation, natural language processing, content generation, and knowledge discovery.
Experience in one or more of the following commercial/open-source data discovery/analysis platforms: RStudio, Spark, KNIME, RapidMiner, Alteryx, Dataiku, H2O, SAS Enterprise Miner (SAS EM) and/or SAS Visual Data Mining and Machine Learning, Microsoft AzureML, Databricks, IBM Watson Studio or SPSS Modeler, Amazon SageMaker, Google Cloud ML, SAP Predictive Analytics
Experience in statistical and data mining techniques such as generalized linear model (GLM)/regression, random forest, boosting, trees, text mining, hierarchical clustering, deep learning, convolutional neural network (CNN), recurrent neural network (RNN), T-distributed Stochastic Neighbor Embedding (t-SNE), graph analysis, etc.
Experience in applying DevOps/MLOps methods to the construction of ML and data science pipelines
Knowledge of Responsible AI with demonstrated experience aligning to Responsible AI best practices
Understanding of Data Privacy regulations and best practices
Experience in DevOps and Agile (Scrum/Kanban), preferred
Willingness and ability to learn new technologies on the job
Ability to communicate complex projects, models, and results to a diverse audiences with a wide range of technical and non-technical understanding
Ability to work in diverse, cross-functional teams in a dynamic business environment
Good presentation skills, including storytelling and other techniques to guide and inspire
Ability to create relationships quickly and strengthen relationships confidently
Demonstrated ability to be the technical lead on multiple projects or activities with competing priorities


Salary Range

Starting salary will be commensurate with qualifications and experience. The range for the classification is $47.22-$65.19 Hourly, $90,484.68-$124,925.58 Annually.

Apply Now!

Title: Data Analyst, Governance & Resilience
Company: West Edmonton Mall Property Inc. (West Edmonton Mall and Fantasyland Hotel)
Description:
The Data Governance & Resilience Analyst is responsible for discovering, cataloging, and classifying organizational data assets to support security, governance, compliance, business continuity, and AI readiness.

This role operates within the information security and risk management domain, with a primary focus on data governance and oversight. The position is responsible for identifying where data resides, understanding what data exists across the organization, and ensuring it is appropriately classified. It supports compliance by assessing data against operational requirements, regulatory obligations, and risk management standards, helping safeguard the organization’s information, assets and maintain strong governance practices.

The role collaborates closely with the data analytics team and other subject matter experts to ensure accurate data discovery and alignment across data sources.

Primary Responsibilities

Data Discovery & Asset Management


Conduct systematic data discovery and inventory across on-premises, cloud, and hybrid environments to identify and catalog all data repositories.
Map data flows between systems to support impact analysis and architectural decision-making.
Maintain an authoritative data inventory and data lineage documentation to ensure visibility remains current as systems, integrations, and data flows evolve.


Data Governance & Classification


Implement and maintain data classification frameworks aligned with organizational risk appetite and regulatory requirements.
Establish governance policies, roles, and accountability structures in collaboration with data owners.
Define data quality standards and support the tracking of metrics for completeness, accuracy, and consistency in collaboration with data owners and system teams.
Facilitate data stewardship activities and enforce metadata management practices.


Compliance & Regulatory Alignment


Ensure data handling practices comply with federal and provincial privacy legislation and sector-specific regulations, in collaboration with Legal, Privacy, Security, and IT stakeholders.
Document data processing activities, retention schedules, and disposal procedures for regulatory requirements.
Support audit and compliance reporting with evidence of governance controls.


Business Continuity & Data Availability


Assess criticality of data assets to support Business Impact Analysis (BIA) and recovery prioritization.
Monitor data availability metrics and coordinate with IT teams to address resilience gaps.
Identify data dependencies for critical business processes to support continuity planning.


AI Readiness Assessment


Identify data gaps, bias risks, or compliance barriers to responsible AI adoption.
Establish data readiness scoring frameworks and document governance, ethics, and risk considerations for AI-enabled applications.


Desired Qualifications


A diploma or bachelor's degree in Information Management, Information Systems, Computer Science, Cybersecurity, or a related field is required.
Three (3) to five (5) years of experience in data governance, information management, compliance roles is desired.
Foundational knowledge of data governance frameworks, data classification schemes, and privacy regulations such as PIPEDA, GDPR.
Familiarity with data catalog platforms, metadata management tools, SQL databases, and cloud data storage environments is an asset.
Strong analytical and documentation skills with the ability to create clear process maps, data flow diagrams, and governance documentation.
Effective written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to translate technical concepts for non-technical stakeholders and collaborate across IT, security, legal, and business teams.
Industry certifications such as CDPSE or CIPP/C are considered an asset.


Terms Of Employment


This is a full-time position based on a 37.5 hour work week from Monday through Friday, with potential participation in after-hour duties as required.
This position requires a physical presence at the office, with opportunities for hybrid work based on team availability and management discretion.
This is a salaried position, commensurate with experience and qualifications.
Must be able to pass a basic security clearance.

  • This is a full-time position based on a 37.5 hour work week from Monday through Friday, with potential participation in after-hour duties as required.
  • This position requires a physical presence at the office, with opportunities for hybrid work based on team availability and management discretion.
  • This is a salaried position, commensurate with experience and qualifications.
  • Must be able to pass a basic security clearance.

Title: Project Portfolio Analyst 2
Description:
BCLC exists to generate win-wins for the greater good.

For our people, our players, our communities, our industry, and our planet.

Lottery | Casino | Sports

Being a social purpose company, we are not only able to better align our business decisions with our purpose, but more importantly, we are committing to doing our part in creating a better world.

We bring our purpose to life by ensuring all our actions, behaviours and decisions create benefits for communities and the planet.

Motivated and guided by our social purpose, everything we do must benefit the greater good. And we encourage our employees, partners, players, industry and communities to engage with us on this ambition.

We want you to be where you feel you can do your best work. Most of our jobs can be done remotely providing you reside in BC.

For those who prefer working in a community with others, we have two beautiful offices in convenient locations:


2940 Virtual Way, Vancouver
74 Seymour Street W, Kamloops


This is a Permanent, Full Time opportunity

Expected Salary Range: $72,633.00 - $90,792.00 - $113,490.00


Our typical hiring range will be +/- 5% of the midpoint shown above
Factors influencing this decision include qualifications and market conditions for the role


The Company

For nearly four decades, BCLC has delivered exceptional gambling entertainment for British Columbians. To fulfill our social purpose we have a vision to revolutionize gambling entertainment through engaging experiences that build and benefit communities. We operate national and provincial lotteries in partnership with 3500 retail partners, provide the games, technology & oversight to 37 brick & mortar casinos, and operate a safe secure and 100% legal online gambling presence PlayNow.com. In our recently completed fiscal year, BCLC generated a record $1.4 billion in net income to the Province of B.C. to support First Nations, host local governments, health care, education and community programs across the province.

Job Summary

The Project Portfolio Analyst 2 is part of BCLC’s Enterprise Portfolio team within the division of Business Technology. The role partners with business sponsors, project leads, product owners and organizational teams to maintain the accuracy and reporting of the strategic programs and projects that advance BCLC’s corporate strategy. The role helps translate complex project, resource, and portfolio data into reliable reporting and practical insights for leaders and decision-makers. Additionally, the role supports the tools and systems required to manage and support our Portfolio capability.

Key Accountabilities

Portfolio Reporting and Insights


Build reoccurring portfolio reports and dashboards for the Executive and Senior Leadership teams, the Board of Directors, Project Managers, Product Owners, and other stakeholders.
Review project status updates and capacity information for completeness, clarity, consistency, and alignment with portfolio reporting expectations; provide constructive feedback to Project Managers, Project Leads, and Product Owners to improve the quality and reliability of submitted information.
Analyze portfolio data to identify themes, risks, dependencies, and capacity constraints; translate findings into clear, fact-based insights for senior leadership consumption.


Resource and Capacity Management Support


Conduct monthly demand & capacity estimation cycles with Product Owners, including communications, escalations, and data validation.
Analyze capacity and demand data analysis to identify themes, trends, potential project collisions and provide scenario analysis.


Technical & Platform Work


Conduct moderately complex technical work, including Power BI report development, Power Automate flow design and maintenance, OnePlan/Jira configuration and integration support, and data visualizations.
Maintain and support the portfolio tools, dashboards, reports, and data sources used to manage Enterprise Portfolio information.


Continuous Improvement & Knowledge Sharing


Recommend process efficiencies and improve project and portfolio management methodologies, updating supporting documentation regularly.
Bring best practices, lessons learned, emerging technologies, and tools to the broader team to support capability improvements.
Leverage generative AI and emerging analytical tools to improve the speed, quality, and consistency of portfolio reporting and analysis, in alignment with BCLC's AI governance and data handling standards.


Stakeholder Engagement & Leadership


Build relations and coordinate with Product Owners, Project Managers, and business stakeholders to collect, validate, and clarify portfolio, project, capacity, and reporting information.


Qualifications

A combination of education, experience, and demonstrated skills may be considered.

Education & Experience


A Bachelor’s degree in a related discipline;
3 to 5 years or relevant portfolio management, project management, data analytics or IT experiences.


Knowledge & Technical Skills


Solid knowledge of program/project management methodologies;
Strong technical writing skills, and able to write reports and document procedures;
Advanced Excel and intermediate Power BI skills.
Working knowledge of PPM platforms (e.g., OnePlan, Jira) or equivalent.
Strong verbal communication, able to translate technical concepts for non-technical stakeholders.
Strong time management, organizational, and multi-tasking skills.
Solid working knowledge of the tools within the Microsoft M365 ecosystem.


COMPETENCIES


Excellent attention to detail and accuracy;
Excellent facilitation and stakeholder engagement skills;
Strong business acumen;
Strong verbal communication skills to effectively convey technical concepts to both technical and non-technical stakeholders, facilitating clear collaboration within cross-functional teams;
Strong time management, organizational and multi-tasking skills to manage multiple concurrent objectives, projects, groups, or activities;
Strong interpersonal and relationship-building skills, and able to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams;
Solid demonstration of problem solving and analytical thinking skills in addressing complex technical challenges.


BCLC has organizational Values that reflect the culture we strive to maintain. All employees are expected to ensure their actions, decisions, and interactions consistently align with BCLC’s Values, Respect, Integrity, and Community.


Building Trust;
Customer Focus;
Welcome change and adapt quickly;
Hold oneself and others accountable for their behaviours.


Preference may be given to individuals who have the following:


Experience with OnePlan, Jira, Power BI, Power Automate, and SharePoint.
Understanding of Agile and PMI methodologies.
Understanding of the B.C. gaming industry.
Project or portfolio management certification (PMP, PMI-ACP, SAFe, or equivalent).


WORKING CONDITIONS (IF APPLICABLE)


Hybrid work arrangement; based in Kamloops or Vancouver.
Occasional travel between BCLC offices may be required.
Standard business hours, with occasional flexibility required during key reporting cycles.


What’s In It For You


Defined benefit pension plan which provides a recurring income you can depend on for life throughout retirement
We pride ourselves on our flexible working model which supports work-life integration and our 37.5 hour work week
Professional development including education/certification sponsorship, in house leadership cohorts, LinkedIn Learning
See all our rewards here


However you identify, or whatever your path in life, if you see something here that makes you excited to get to work every day, please apply. We hire people for skills, capabilities and potential, not just education and experience.

We value Respect, Integrity and Community, and we provide an inclusive environment where everyone can feel like they belong .

Our social purpose is much more than returning 100% of net income to the province in the form of healthcare & education programs, and community gaming grants. Check it out !

Did you know BCLC is an industry leader in player health and safe & responsible gambling? Find out more !

If you require accommodation so you can be at your best in the interview, please let us know: recruitment@bclc.com .

All candidates must be at least 19 years of age and legally eligible to work in Canada

Title: Supply Chain Data Analyst
Description:
Convergint is looking for a team-oriented, results-driven, Supply Chain Data Analyst, to join our amazing culture. As the Supply Chain Data Analyst, you will be responsible for maintaining vendor item master catalogs and provide reporting and analytics to support the source to pay process. This role involves tasks like data entry, updates, and resolving discrepancies, with a strong focus on data integrity and efficiency in operations. This role has an emphasis on support of the supply chain and sales teams, and motivation in building effective business relationships with vendors and customers alike.

Who You Are

You have a passion for providing world-class service to customers, colleagues and communities. You are a person of integrity with a commitment to growth, accountability and delivering results. You want to join an organization with a positive culture that embraces inclusion and allows everyone to be the best version of themselves at work and home.

Who We Are

With 20-years of proven growth and exceptional performance, our mission is to be our customer’s best service provider. We realize the importance of diversity in achieving that goal. Our company was built upon a solid foundation of 10 Values and Beliefs which drive our unmatched culture, making us the #1 global, serviced-based systems integrator in the industry. We take great pride in protecting the lives and assets of our customers and their communities with the solutions we provide.

What you’ll do with “Our Training and Your Experience”


Creating, updating, and managing item records within relevant systems.
Ensure the accuracy, completeness, and consistency of item data, including descriptions, classifications, units of measure, vendor information, pricing, and other relevant details.
Conduct regular audits to identify and correct errors, duplicates, or outdated records.
Manage the lifecycle of item records, including creation, updates, and decommissioning.
Support for item master data management, including troubleshooting issues and assisting with system enhancements.
Interact with suppliers, manufacturers, and distributors on an on-going basis, which may include negotiating prices, discounts, credit terms, and logistics.
Participating in the development and implementation of best practices for item master data management.
Work with Supply Chain leadership to develop and implement best practices, demand planning, and other optimizations in order to help grow the business and to ensure process transparency, fairness, impartiality, and confidentiality.
Collaborate with global counterparts to align with process standardization and consistency in reporting across all regions.
Provide reporting and data analytics to Supply Chain leadership to show trends, address discrepancies, and generate accurate forecasting.
Develop constructive and cooperative working relationships with internal stakeholders and vendors alike and maintain them over time.
Executes all work in accordance with Provincial/State, local regulations, general best practices as well as company health and safety policies and procedures.
Acts as “our customer’s best service provider” at all times thereby ensuring Convergint Technologies is the customer’s first choice for service.
Communicates with and works with the other team members consistently to improve overall operations of the Convergint Technology Center, and company.
Keeps up to date and informed on all company policies.
Presents a professional image of Convergint Technologies at all times in appearance and behavior.
Performs other duties and responsibilities as requested or required.


What You’ll Need


Strong attention to details.
Strong verbal, written and interpersonal communication skills and ability to deal with a wide cross section of people both internal and external to the business.
Excellent Excel, Word, and Outlook skills.
Excellent proficiency in financial software specifically related to purchase order entry, accounts payable, and inventory management.
Strong negotiation skills.
Strong financial knowledge and skills—ability to understand basic financials such as accounts receivable/payable, purchase orders, work orders, etc.
Excellent organizational skills and the ability to handle multiple projects tasks simultaneously.
Excellent attention to detail.
Strong flexibility to adapt to changing priorities, work processes, department structure, environment and technology in a dynamic work environment.
Self-driven and motivated to meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.
Strong analytical skills are necessary to resolve problems and look for solutions.
Strong ability to demonstrate independence, resourcefulness and tenacity.
Shows initiative – regularly engages in proactive behavior and looks for opportunities.


Requirements


Diploma and/or certificate in Business Administration or Purchasing required.
SCMP designation highly desirable.
Minimum of five years of procurement experience including elements of the supply chain
preferred.
Minimum of five years of negotiation and vendor management experience preferred.
Minimum of five years of customer service experience is required.
Experience in Ivalua, Salesforce, and NetSuite highly desirable.
Relevant industry experience in electrical, security, building automation, or fire is required


Company Benefits And Perks

Convergint fosters a supportive, accessible, and inclusive environment in which individuals of different backgrounds and identities are able to realize their maximum potential within the company. We offer a variety of programs and exceptional benefits to all of our colleagues:


Company Holidays and Paid Time Off
Immediate comprehensive benefit plan (medical, dental, etc.)
Employer contribution to RRSP
Work environment committed to safety.
Colleague Assistance Program
Tuition reimbursement
Competitive salary and compensation plan
Corporate Social Responsibility Day
And much more…


Convergint is committed to a culture of Inclusion and Diversity and is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

Visit our Convergint careers site to learn more about the company and the exciting opportunities available.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Role: Data Governance Analyst

Location: Calgary, AB, Canada

Type: Contract

Duration: 12 Months




Job Responsibilities:

Seeking a Data Governance Analyst to support a large-scale SAP implementation project. This role will be responsible for capturing requirements and establishing data governance standards to ensure high-quality, consistent, and compliant data. The analyst will work closely with business stakeholders, data stewards, and technical teams to define data ownership, improve data quality, and support project readiness and long-term sustainability.

Top 3 Outcomes:

Establish and maintain data governance standards
Support data migration activities for master data governance tool implementation
Develop and track data quality KPIs and metrics




Responsibilities:

Define and document data governance policies, and standards for SAP data objects (e.g., customer, vendor, material, finance).
Establish and support data ownership and data stewardship models across business domains
Collaborate with business and IT teams to define data definitions, business rules, and data standards.
Support data migration activities, including data cleansing, validation, and reconciliation for master data governance tool implementation
Develop and track data quality KPIs and metrics.
Ensure compliance with internal policies and regulatory requirements related to data management.
Facilitate data governance workshops with stakeholders to align on definitions, rules and ownership.
Review SOX Compliance documents and work closely with control / audit teams to gather SOX requirements.
Maintain data structures, metadata, and documentation in approved data catalogues.
Support the implementation of data governance tools and workflows.
Collaborate with functional teams (Finance, Procurement, Supply Chain, etc.) to ensure alignment on data requirements.
Assist with project closure activities, ensuring data-related deliverables are complete and signed off.




Qualifications & Skills Required

Required Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Information Systems, Business, Computer Science, or a related field.
3–7 years of experience in data governance, data management, or data quality roles.
Experience supporting or working on SAP implementations (e.g., S/4HANA preferred).
Strong understanding of master data domains (Customer, Vendor, Material, Finance).
Experience with data migration, data cleansing, validation processes, and data management methods.




Technical Skills

Knowledge of SAP tables and data structures.
Strong understanding of databases and data structures, independent of specific platforms or technologies.
Experience with SAP transactions (e.g., SE16N) and data extraction tools.
Familiarity with data profiling and data quality tools.
Strong Excel and data analysis skills.
Experience with data governance or metadata tools (e.g., Prospecta MDO, SAP MDG, Purview, DataSphere, or Information Steward) is an asset.




Preferred Experience

Prior experience in large-scale ERP transformation projects.
Experience in regulated industries (e.g., energy, finance, utilities).
Understanding of data privacy and compliance requirements. Knowledge of regulatory or compliance-driven data environments.




Soft Skills

Strong stakeholder management and communication skills.
Ability to translate business requirements into data rules and standards.
Detail-oriented with strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Ability to facilitate workshops and drive alignment across teams.
Strong organizational skills and ability to manage multiple priorities.

Title: Analyst, Data Traceability and Controls
Description:
Job Description

What is the opportunity?

As part of the Chief Data Office (CDO) Data Shared Services team, the Analyst, Data Lineage & Control will be responsible for documenting all aspects of the data lineage lifecycle from the original source of the data to the point of consumption of the data into a report, including any associated data transformations.

The role will maintain relationships with various business & IT stakeholders to understand & communicate details of data lineage

What will you do?


Understand and document the lineage of Critical Data Elements (CDEs) from originating source to point of consumption of data into a report or other risk management activity
Collaborate with other Data Management Offices, Data Custodians and Data Stewards to understand data lineage reporting standards and best practices.
Leverage data lineage artifacts captured by other Data Management Offices and share artifacts captured by Risk Data Management Office to avoid duplication of effort.
Identify & communicate options to optimize the process of data lineage capture and documentation.
Develop knowledge and become valuable team resource, supporting development of Data Lineage in general.


What do you need to succeed?

Must Have


1 – 2 years’ experience in data management, data lineage capture, data analysis or any other related data programs
Post-Secondary education or equivalent in Finance, Data Analysis or other related programs
Experience with data manipulation and analysis tools such as Datameer, SQL, Python

Title: Methods Analyst I -ECOMB Methods Analyst
Description:
The Edmonton Police Service requires one (1) permanent full-time organized, detail-oriented, individual to provide analysis and call center reporting to the Data and Technology Unit of the Emergency Communications and Operations Management Branch. Responsibilities will include:


Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly reports and dashboards to support data driven decision making by the leadership team.
Produce ad-hoc reporting on-demand to support internal initiatives, projects, and investigations.
Lead the design, development, automation and maintenance of statistical data and information for a wide range of internal and external stakeholders.
Provide expert advice and guidance on statistical analytics and establish robust mechanisms to assess the effectiveness of the emergency call center.
Manage the Extraction, Translation, and Loading of data from various sources, including telephony and dispatch data, to serve the needs of Reporting and Analytics.
Develop and maintain Key Performance Indicators by applying statistical and mathematical modelling techniques to inform operational efficiency and effectiveness.
Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly reports and dashboards to support data driven decision making by the leadership team.
Present complex concepts and analyses to stakeholders leveraging sound written and verbal communication skills complemented by striking and informative visual storytelling.
Provide subject matter expertise regarding data literacy, use of reporting tools, statistical concepts, and practices.
Create and maintain process maps, documentation, and training material; deliver training to stakeholders as needed.
Liaise with internal and external partners to research and share best practices and methodologies.
Other related duties as required.



Qualifications:


University graduation in Criminology, Sociology and Applied Mathematics or other related fields of study.
A minimum of two (2) years directly related working experience is required.
Familiarity with Microsoft applications and services including Word, Excel, Access, Fabric, and Power BI.
Strong written and verbal communication skills, and ability to effectively communicate complex technical details to non-technical audiences.
Familiarity with Python, R, SQL-based query languages, DAX, and PowerShell languages an assets.
Experience providing analysis of metrics for call centers or public safety answering points (PSAP) would be an asset.
Strong organizational, interpersonal and time management skills.
Proven technical ability working with various computer software programs and databases.
Demonstrated self-initiative and the ability to work with minimal supervision.
Mature individual with the ability to adapt to change and work in a multidisciplinary team setting.
Experience transforming data within Snowflake would be an asset.



Hours of Work: 40 hours per week, Monday – Friday.

Salary Range: 21B, Salary Grade: 024, $41.17 - $51.44 (Hourly), $3,294.20 - $4,115.50 (Biweekly), $85,977.58 - $107,415.07 (Annually). The rates quoted are in accordance with a collective agreement between the Union and the City of Edmonton.

General:


Civic Service Union 52 members are asked to send a copy of their application to the union office.
The City of Edmonton thanks applicants for their interest in this opportunity. Candidates considered for the position will be contacted.
We are an equal opportunity employer. We welcome diversity and encourage applications from all qualified individuals.
This option to work remotely is not available at this time.
As part of your pre-hire screening, you will be required to complete an Enhanced Security Clearance. Should you accept employment with the Edmonton Police Service you acknowledge and agree to participate in future Enhanced Security Re-Clearances at scheduled intervals or in any other circumstances, exigent or otherwise, as required by Human Resources Division.
Applicants may be tested.



Applicants who have completed post-secondary education outside of Canada are encouraged to obtain an assessment of their credentials through the International Qualifications Assessment Service (IQAS) ( https://www.alberta.ca/international-qualifications-assessment.aspx) or another recognized Canadian credential evaluation service. For a list of designated evaluators, please visit the Alliance of Credential Evaluation Services of Canada ( https://canalliance.org/en/default.html ). Including an IQAS certificate or other credential assessment certificate from a recognized Canadian credential evaluation service with your application is recommended to support the evaluation of the applicant’s educational qualifications.

The Province of Alberta is a party to the Federal Agreement on Internal Trade, the Trade, Investment and Labour Mobility with British Columbia and the New West Partnership Trade Agreement with British Columbia and Saskatchewan. All of these agreements promote labour mobility between the Provinces. Applicants may obtain information regarding recognition of extra provincial credentials at www.newwestpartnershiptrade.ca .

Edmonton rests in the heart of Treaty Six territory in Alberta and the homelands of the Metis Nation.

Title: Decision Science Analyst
Description:
Come join Pattison Food Group Ltd., a Jim Pattison business, and Canada’s largest Western-based provider of food and health products.

Pattison Food Group Ltd. is a certified Great Place to Work® and consists of Save-On-Foods and other well-known grocery banners. With nearly 300 retail locations, we proudly employ nearly 30,000 team members.

We have an exciting opportunity for a full-time Decision Science Analyst to join our More Rewards team in Langley, BC.

Reporting to the Senior Manager, Customer Data & Analytics, you will be responsible for leveraging all aspects of data and analytics to support large-scale projects, experimentation, and continuous improvement initiatives that guide decision-making and deliver high-quality insights. In this role, you will support the development and management of customer data and insights to support the More Rewards Loyalty Program, Marketing, Retail Operations and Merchandising.

You will be responsible for:


Collaborating with stakeholders to understand business questions, processes and KPIs.
Formulating testable hypotheses aligned with business questions and conducting statistical experiments in a live environment.
Writing SQL scripts to automate data pipelines and workflows.
Using Python scripts in a Jupyter Notebook environment for exploratory data analysis, data wrangling and statistical testing.
Developing and maintaining dashboards and visualizations in Power BI to convey insights, report on KPIs and test results.
Learning cloud-based analytics environments and big data tools to build analytics workflows on large datasets.
Discovering unique and creative insights to identify new opportunities for driving business value.
Becoming an expert in the relevant datasets within your analytics domain.
Supporting the Data & Analytics team with key projects.



You have:


A degree in a STEM discipline with 3+ years of relevant experience in data and analytics.
Advanced Python coding skills and understanding of SQL.
Experience with additional programming languages is an asset.
Experience using experimental design principles such as A/B and multivariate testing and working with business stakeholders to formulate testable hypotheses.
An understanding of advanced analytics and data science methods, such as anomaly detection, forecasting, and supervised & unsupervised learning.
Working knowledge of cloud environments, such as Azure, GCP or AWS. Experience with big data tools such as Spark and Databricks is an asset.



You are:


A confident communicator, both verbally and written, with strong interpersonal skills and the ability to communicate a story with data and insights to non-technical stakeholders.
A problem solver with strong analytical and critical thinking skills.
Effective in task management and delivering high-quality work.
Capable of multitasking and thriving in a fast-paced environment.
Self-motivated with the ability to work independently and collaboratively.



What we offer:

In addition to a highly competitive salary, we offer a great range of benefits, a company pension plan, free parking, exclusive team member offers, and opportunities for development and career progression.

If you’re looking to join a team that puts people first, you’re in the right place. Apply online today!

Compensation Details:

$83,394.00 - $104,243.00

The compensation offered for this position will take into consideration location, education, skills, experience and other factors.

Title: Intermediate Business Analyst
Description:
Job Description

Fleetway is seeking a qualified Intermediate Data Analyst to join our Technical Publications team, which is leading the development of cutting-edge documentation for the next generation of Royal Canadian Navy ships. We offer a modern, collaborative environment that leverages advanced technology to produce high-quality technical publications.

As an Intermediate Data Analyst, you will play a key role in supporting Irving Shipbuilding’s strategic goals and operational efficiency by analyzing data, identifying business needs, and recommending actionable solutions. This position is central to the development and delivery of the Technical Data Package (TDP) for the River Class Destroyer Program.

You’ll work closely with cross-functional teams to coordinate and maintain TDP deliverables, metrics, and dashboards. Your insights will help stakeholders and leadership make informed decisions at both project and management levels.

We're committed to Diversity, Equity and Inclusion

Fleetway is committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace. We welcome applications from all qualified individuals, including those from underrepresented groups, and we strive to create an environment where everyone feels respected, supported, and empowered to thrive.

We encourage applicants to self-identify if they belong to an underrepresented group, as part of our commitment to equitable hiring practices and fostering a truly inclusive workforce.

If you require accommodations during the recruitment process, please let us know—we're happy to help.

Responsibilities


Collaborate with stakeholders to gather and document business requirements.
Analyze data to support the development and delivery of Technical Data Packages (TDPs).
Maintain dashboards and reports to track project progress and performance.
Support project planning, testing, and change management activities.
Identify process improvements and recommend data-driven solutions.
Ensure alignment with compliance standards and organizational goals.


Qualifications

Education


A post-secondary degree in Computer Science, Business, Information Systems, or a related field.


Skills & Experience


2–5 years of experience as a Data Analyst or in a similar role.
Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
Ability to work collaboratively across teams and disciplines
Detail-oriented with a focus on accuracy and quality
Adaptable and proactive in a dynamic project environment


Due to the nature of our work, successful candidates must be eligible to obtain required government security clearances.

About Us

Founded in 1882, J.D. Irving, Limited (JDI) operates in Eastern Canada (New-Brunswick, Nova Scotia, Prince Edward Island, Québec, Ontario, Newfoundland and Labrador), as well as in USA. We are a team of 20,000 dedicated employees. At J.D. Irving, Limited, you are part of a dynamic team of professionals who lead with integrity and are passionate about delivering results. With a culture that nurtures talent and creates an inclusive workplace, you can grow your career across our various operating divisions.

We are committed to creating accessible environments for our colleagues, candidates, and customers. Requests for accommodation due to a disability (which may be visible or invisible, temporary, or permanent) can be made at any stage of application and employment. We encourage candidates to make their accommodation needs known so that we can provide equitable opportunities.

Worried you do not meet all the requirements? Studies have shown that many applicants apply only to job opportunities when they feel they meet 100% of the criteria. We promote an inclusive environment where everyone is appreciated, recognized, and empowered to contribute fully. If you are interested in this position but are worried you did not meet all the requirements, we would encourage you to still apply.

Our recruitment advisors and hiring teams will utilize human screening combined with AI technology to help identify the skills and qualities that matter most to our business, while safeguarding your privacy and using AI responsibly.

We appreciate your interest in our company however only those candidates selected for an interview will be contacted.

About The Team

We are Fleetway! We believe Canada’s maritime fleets need to be reliable and fit for purpose, with crews that are fully trained and supported. We work as a trusted technical partner during design, construction, in-service, and end of life. We are proud to deliver quality professional technical services including Engineering, Integrated Logistics Support, Technical Editing, Configuration Management, Project Management, Training, Technical Translation, and Marine Technical Services.

Over the past 40+ years, we have built an outstanding team of people and partners, invested in the tools, and earned an exemplary track record servicing our customers. Everything we do is aimed at developing our people and improving our use of innovation to be the leading professional services provider in the marine industry. We are proud to have earned national recognition as a Nova Scotia and Atlantic Canada’s Top Employer.

All successful applicants must meet requirements for Canadian Controlled Goods Program (CGP), and Canadian Government Security Clearance.

What We Offer:

The successful candidate will receive a starting salary based on their applicable experience and demonstrated knowledge, skill, and abilities. Additionally, we offer the following:


We are regularly adding new departments, positions, and functions within Fleetway, as such, we can offer you growth and development opportunities for future career advancement
We provide direct support to your training and development plans, including support towards your professional certifications, memberships, or designations
Flexible work hours (with core hours)
We have Mental Health first aid trained staff in all offices
A flexible medical, dental, and vision plan, with an enhanced focus on mental health and wellness
Ability to save for your future with our company matching RRSP program
15-week maternity leave top-up program
Employee & Family Assistance Program
Annual health & wellness reimbursements
Business casual work environment, with casual Fridays
A slick company jacket that will definitely make you the envy of your friends

  • Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
  • Ability to work collaboratively across teams and disciplines
  • Detail-oriented with a focus on accuracy and quality
  • Adaptable and proactive in a dynamic project environment

Due to the nature of our work, successful candidates must be eligible to obtain required government security clearances.

About Us

Founded in 1882, J.D. Irving, Limited (JDI) operates in Eastern Canada (New-Brunswick, Nova Scotia, Prince Edward Island, Québec, Ontario, Newfoundland and Labrador), as well as in USA. We are a team of 20,000 dedicated employees. At J.D. Irving, Limited, you are part of a dynamic team of professionals who lead with integrity and are passionate about delivering results. With a culture that nurtures talent and creates an inclusive workplace, you can grow your career across our various operating divisions.

We are committed to creating accessible environments for our colleagues, candidates, and customers. Requests for accommodation due to a disability (which may be visible or invisible, temporary, or permanent) can be made at any stage of application and employment. We encourage candidates to make their accommodation needs known so that we can provide equitable opportunities.

Worried you do not meet all the requirements? Studies have shown that many applicants apply only to job opportunities when they feel they meet 100% of the criteria. We promote an inclusive environment where everyone is appreciated, recognized, and empowered to contribute fully. If you are interested in this position but are worried you did not meet all the requirements, we would encourage you to still apply.

Our recruitment advisors and hiring teams will utilize human screening combined with AI technology to help identify the skills and qualities that matter most to our business, while safeguarding your privacy and using AI responsibly.

We appreciate your interest in our company however only those candidates selected for an interview will be contacted.

About The Team

We are Fleetway! We believe Canada’s maritime fleets need to be reliable and fit for purpose, with crews that are fully trained and supported. We work as a trusted technical partner during design, construction, in-service, and end of life. We are proud to deliver quality professional technical services including Engineering, Integrated Logistics Support, Technical Editing, Configuration Management, Project Management, Training, Technical Translation, and Marine Technical Services.

Over the past 40+ years, we have built an outstanding team of people and partners, invested in the tools, and earned an exemplary track record servicing our customers. Everything we do is aimed at developing our people and improving our use of innovation to be the leading professional services provider in the marine industry. We are proud to have earned national recognition as a Nova Scotia and Atlantic Canada’s Top Employer.

All successful applicants must meet requirements for Canadian Controlled Goods Program (CGP), and Canadian Government Security Clearance.

What We Offer:

The successful candidate will receive a starting salary based on their applicable experience and demonstrated knowledge, skill, and abilities. Additionally, we offer the following:

  • We are regularly adding new departments, positions, and functions within Fleetway, as such, we can offer you growth and development opportunities for future career advancement
  • We provide direct support to your training and development plans, including support towards your professional certifications, memberships, or designations
  • Flexible work hours (with core hours)
  • We have Mental Health first aid trained staff in all offices
  • A flexible medical, dental, and vision plan, with an enhanced focus on mental health and wellness
  • Ability to save for your future with our company matching RRSP program
  • 15-week maternity leave top-up program
  • Employee & Family Assistance Program
  • Annual health & wellness reimbursements
  • Business casual work environment, with casual Fridays
  • A slick company jacket that will definitely make you the envy of your friends

Title: Transportation Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description




Position: Transportation Data Analyst

Type: Full-Time | Hybrid

Location: Brampton, ON

Reports to: Senior Manager, Inbound Transportation




About Us

Pet Valu is Canada’s leading pet specialty retailer, dedicated to strengthening the bond between dedicated pet lovers and their pets. With 50 years of animal care expertise, we operate over 800 stores across Canada under several banners, including Pet Valu, Bosley’s, Total Pet, Paulmac's Pet, Tisol & Chico. Guided by our mission to deliver products, care, expertise, and memorable moments, Pet Valu is a place where people build meaningful careers while making a positive impact on pets and communities.




What will you do?

Pet Valu’s supply chain has grown significantly as the company has expanded, supported by a $100M investment in supply chain technology and facilities. As part of this investment, Pet Valu launched Manhattan Active Transportation Management to improve network planning, centralize transportation data, and strengthen reporting and operational visibility. Pet Valu’s transportation team is responsible for the movement of all collect inbound freight to the company’s three distribution centres across Canada, and outbound to 800+ stores.

The Transportation Data Analyst is responsible for ensuring the accuracy, integrity, and cost-effectiveness of inbound and outbound transportation data and spend. This role oversees transportation data within the TMS, supports freight audit activities by validating carrier charges and logistics metrics, and develops reporting and KPI insights that drive data-based decision-making. The analyst also helps maintain system configurations, support cost allocation processes, identify opportunities to improve transportation performance and reduce expenses, and partner closely with internal supply chain and IT teams, as well as external freight carriers, to maintain efficient and effective transportation operations across Canada.




Responsibilities

Data Management and Governance

Own the data for the transportation management system by being the subject matter expert for all TMS data, working with IT teams on data flows, and implementing technical business rules, including maintenance carrier, lane, and rate data
Developing and maintain KPI reports for supply chain, and communicate performance results and insights to stakeholders
Manage setup, collection, and analysis of bid data for carrier RFPs




Data Analysis and Reporting

Conduct in-depth data analysis to identify trends, insights, and opportunities for optimization. Develop and maintain comprehensive, automated reports and dashboards using advanced data visualization tools. Present complex data insights to stakeholders in a clear and concise manner
Develop common metrics and data processes that drive business outcomes. Stay abreast of industry trends and best practices in data management and analytics, and recommend innovative solutions to improve data-driven decision-making.




Freight Invoice Audit & Validation

Review and audit outbound freight invoices to ensure accuracy against contracted rates, routing guides, and shipment details
Identify and resolve discrepancies such as incorrect rates, accessorial charges, or duplicate billing
Approve validated invoices for payment processing




Training and Support

Provide training and support to end-users on TMS data tools and best practices. Mentor junior analysts and other team members to enhance their analytical and technical skills.
Vacation / sick time coverage as necessary




Qualifications & Experience

Minimum of 4 years' experience in supply chain / logistics activities
Experience with TMS, WMS, optimization tools, or network modeling preferred
Experience with Excel, PowerBI, Access, Cognos, Snowflake, and other database and analytics tools
Experience with data analysis and building KPI tools




Compensation

The targeted salary range for this position is $65,000-$75,000 annually. The final offer will be based on factors such as market location, relevant skills, experience and internal equity.




Ready to join the team?

We’re looking for passionate people who bring strong skills and live our core values of safety, compassion, expertise, and efficiency every day. If you’re energized by doing meaningful work, taking pride in what you deliver, and making a real impact for pets, people, and communities, you’ll feel right at home here. Join us and build a career where purpose, care, and excellence come together - because love lives here™.




About

At Pet Valu, our people are at the heart of everything we do. We’re passionate about creating an environment where you feel supported, included, and empowered to grow - both personally and professionally. Some of the perks of working with us include:




Hybrid, Flexible Work Options
Comprehensive health and dental benefits
Employee Share Purchase Plan with company matching
Learning and Development support tailored to you
An inclusive, collaborative culture where your voice matters
Fun company events that keep us connected
Preferred rates for car rentals, hotels, phone plans and gym discounts
Generous employee discounts on our products




Pet Valu is an equal opportunity employer committed to inclusion and accessibility. Accommodations are available upon request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the recruitment and selection process, in accordance with the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act (AODA).




At Pet Valu, we are committed to transparency and fairness in our hiring practices. This posting is for an existing vacancy. Pet Valu may use AI enabled technology to assist in the recruitment process for positions. The use of AI helps us ensure we are providing an equitable and streamlined recruitment experience for all applicants. While AI tools support our recruitment process, all decisions throughout the process are made exclusively by Pet Valu’s hiring team. At no stage does AI determine recruitment outcomes.

Title: Retirement Analyst
Description:
We are searching for a Retirement Analyst to join our National Indigenous Solutions Practice in Winnipeg, Burlington, or Burnaby.

The Retirement Analyst plays a central role in advancing the Group Retirement practice within National Indigenous Solutions (NIS) Practice. This role blends data management, pricing support, market analysis, and insight generation to support both client retention and growth.

Working closely with the National Director, Group Retirement Strategy, the Retirement Analyst will act as a key contributor to how we collect, analyze, and apply data across a national block of business. The role is equal parts analytical and strategic. They will automate processes by leveraging AI, and deliver differentiated insights to Indigenous organizations and communities.

This is an excellent opportunity for a high-potential analyst to develop deep expertise in group retirement while helping shape a modern, data-driven practice.

We strongly encourage First Nations and Indigenous applicants to self-declare in your cover letter.

The Retirement Analyst Will

Data Management & Integrity


Maintain and validate client and member data across group retirement programs, ensuring accuracy, consistency, and completeness
Identify and resolve data gaps, inconsistencies, and anomalies across multiple data sources
Build and maintain structured datasets to support reporting, pricing, and insight generation
Establish disciplined processes to improve data quality and accessibility across the practice


Reporting & Benchmarking


Develop and maintain reporting frameworks and benchmarking models across the NIS block of business
Produce recurring and ad-hoc reports for client reviews, internal leadership, and new business opportunities
Leverage AI tools to automate reporting processes and enhance data visualization
Translate complex datasets into clear, client-ready insights that demonstrate value and market positioning


Pricing & New Business Support


Coordinate the preparation and management of group retirement quote requests with insurance carriers
Analyze pricing proposals, prepare comparisons, and support recommendation development
Build impactful, client-facing proposal materials for both existing and prospective clients
Support RFP responses and finalist presentations in partnership with business development and client management teams


Insights & Strategy Enablement


Analyze plan performance, member behaviour, and utilization trends to identify opportunities for improvement
Develop data-driven insights that inform plan design, investment menus, and member engagement strategies
Research industry trends and competitive positioning to strengthen NIS market differentiation
Utilize AI tools to accelerate analysis and surface insights across large and complex datasets


Operational Support & Collaboration


Partner closely with the National Director, Group Retirement Strategy, and broader NIS leadership team
Prepare materials for client meetings, plan reviews, and internal strategic discussions
Support governance processes and ensure readiness of documentation and reporting requirements
Contribute to a collaborative, high-performing team environment aligned to NIS priorities


To be successful as a Retirement Analyst with the National Indigenous Solutions Practice, you will need:


Minimum 2 years of experience in data analysis, financial services, group retirement, or a related field
Foundational understanding of group retirement programs, pricing, and reporting (or strong ability to learn quickly)
Advanced Excel skills and experience with data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI)
Demonstrated hands-on experience using AI tools (e.g., Copilot, automation tools) to enhance analysis, improve workflows, and generate insights
Strong ability to work with large, multi-source datasets and translate findings into clear business narratives
Experience supporting pricing, proposals, or client-facing deliverables is an asset
High attention to detail, organization, and ability to manage multiple priorities
Strong communication and presentation skills, including building client-ready materials
Curiosity, adaptability, and a mindset of continuous improvement through technology


What’s In It For You


An opportunity to make a real impact in Indigenous communities across Canada
Involvement in meaningful work supporting Truth and Reconciliation efforts
Ongoing learning and collaboration with a supportive and experienced team
Competitive compensation
Comprehensive employee benefits and retirement programs
A fun, flexible, and productive work environment


Our Commitment

National Indigenous Solutions is committed to building a team that reflects the communities we serve. We welcome and encourage applications from First Nations, Métis, and Inuit candidates. Applicants are invited to self-declare Indigenous identity in their application.

The National Indigenous Solutions Practice, a division of People Corporation, is a trusted Group Insurance & Retirement practice proudly serving Indigenous employers across Canada. The National Indigenous Solutions Practice is comprised of three sister companies – Eagle Bay Financial (Burnaby, BC), Life Benefit Solutions (Winnipeg, MB) and WP Pension and Benefits (Burlington, ON) who all specialize in designing benefit and group retirement programs that thoughtfully integrate Non-Insured Health Benefits (NIHB), supporting equity between Status and non-Status employees. Their mission is to deliver customized, community-conscious solutions that meet the unique needs of Indigenous and non-Indigenous employees alike.

We are committed to fostering a respectful, inclusive, and accessible environment where all employees and clients feel supported and valued. We are proud to build a workforce that reflects the diversity of the communities we serve and to create an environment where everyone has the opportunity to thrive.

Providing an inclusive, accessible environment, where all employees and clients feel valued, respected and supported is something we're committed to. We are dedicated to building a workforce that reflects the diversity of the communities in which we live, and to creating an environment where every employee has the opportunity to reach their potential. If you require an accommodation or an alternative format of any posting please reach out to careers@peoplecorporation.com.

The base salary range for this position is $55,000 - $75,000 per annum.

This represents base salary only and does not represent other variable compensation components of our total compensation ( i.e. annual bonus, etc). The actual base salary will vary depending on local market conditions, geography and relevant job-related factors such as knowledge, skills, qualifications, experience, and education/training. If you are selected to move forward in our recruitment process, the Talent Acquisition Specialist will be able to provide additional details of the total compensation for this role.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Artificial Intelligence. Actual Impact.

At Docebo, we’re using AI to change how people learn at work—and we mean actually change it. We’re an AI-powered learning platform that helps organizations create, deliver, and manage training all in one place. But our real mission goes deeper: we help teams move faster, work smarter, and focus on the work that truly matters. Our platform is built with intelligent, time-saving tools that personalize learning, eliminate busywork, and turn training from a checkbox into a superpower. The result? Better experiences for learners and real results for businesses.

We’re shaping the future of learning with a team that isn’t afraid to challenge the status quo. If you're excited by the idea of using AI to make work-life better for real people–you’ll feel right at home here. And it’s not just what we build, it’s how we show up. At Docebo, our values aren’t just posters on the wall—they guide how we work every day. We call it the Docebo Heart: trust by default, assume positive intent, and create space for different perspectives to thrive.

So… what are you waiting for? Join 900+ Docebians around the world and help us reinvent the way people learn, because learning never stops.

The Adventure Ahead 🚀

As a Data Analyst within the Product Engineering family, you will hold the keys to transforming raw information into the strategic compass that guides our entire organization. Reporting to the Manager of Data Analytics, your mission is to leverage company-wide data to generate actionable insights and build beautiful visualizations that enhance performance across the business. You will act as a critical collaborator with the Data team, serving as a vital contributor to our core data-driven decision-making processes while ensuring the ultimate accuracy and reliability of our data. This is your opportunity to turn complex datasets into a superpower that drives impactful, real-world business outcomes.

The Day-to-Day 📈


Decode the Data: Manipulate and analyze various complex datasets to uncover actionable insights and support key business decisions across the company.
Architect the Visuals: Design and deploy interactive dashboards using advanced data visualization tools to clearly visualize data and track performance metrics.
Bridge the Teams: Partner closely with the Data team and cross-functional teams across the organization to deeply understand business requirements and deliver tailored analytical solutions.
Predict the Future: Conduct advanced analytics, including predictive modeling, correlation analysis, and statistical evaluations to identify upcoming trends and strategic opportunities.
Guard the Governance: Implement rigorous data validation and governance processes to maintain the absolute highest standards of data accuracy and reliability.
Streamline and Automate: Reverse engineer data requirements to help Data Engineers streamline and automate data collection, processing, and reporting to improve efficiency and scalability.
Champion Clarity: Translate complex data into clear insights and actionable recommendations to improve business strategies, delivering findings through effective verbal and written communication for both technical and non-technical audiences.
Master the Databases: Query and manage databases with precision, utilizing tools and languages like SQL and Snowflake to extract and manipulate data for analysis.
Optimize the Engine: Continuously refine and improve reporting systems and workflows to align seamlessly with evolving business needs


Your Superpowers ⚡


The Data Track Record: 3+ years of proven experience in business intelligence, data analysis, or reporting, showing a passion for solving business problems through data.
The Academic Foundation: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Finance, Economics, Analytics, Business, Math, Statistics, or a related field.
The Tech Toolkit: Strong SQL and Python skills, with a proficiency in data visualization tools and their advanced functionalities (experience with Snowflake is a big plus!).
The Math Magician: Deep expertise in statistical analysis, data pivots, predictive modeling, and designing scalable data solutions.
The Detail Detective: High attention to detail, a detail-oriented mindset, and an unyielding commitment to maintaining data accuracy and quality.
The Proactive Collaborator: A self-motivated, proactive individual with a collaborative mindset who thrives in a fast-paced environment and is driven by process improvement and innovation.
The Master Communicator: Strong communication skills with a proven ability to deliver analytical insights effectively to diverse, cross-functional audiences.


Our Hybrid Work Philosophy 🤝

Great work can happen anywhere but coming together helps us go further. Our team spends three days a week in the office (Tuesday-Thursday) to collaborate, solve problems, and learn from each other. With flexibility the rest of the week, it’s a balance designed to help everyone do their best work and keep growing.

Our Total Rewards Philosophy 🎉

Our Total Rewards Philosophy centers around three core areas to reward and care for our People:


Rewarding Impact: We lead with competitive pay to reward the impact, skills and traits that fuel our success.
Fostering Holistic Wellbeing: We care deeply about and invest in the whole person with programs that support our people’s physical, mental, and financial well-being.
Empowering Our Talent Culture: We build a culture of trust and empowerment by designing our rewards and benefits with transparency, equity, and flexibility, enabling our people to do their best work and stay for the long haul.


Our Promise to You 😍


Financial Wellness: Own a piece of Docebo through our Employee Share Purchase Plan (ESPP) at a 15% discount, plus a competitive compensation package.
Your Well-Being, Covered: You’ll get access to health benefits, so you can get the care you need when you need it.
Rest, Relax, Repeat: Rest and recharge with paid vacation days, two company-wide Docebo Days, floating holidays for cultural celebrations, and your birthday off!
Family First: We provide coverage offering you time with your little one(s) so you can soak up all those precious moments. Fun fact: we had 30 Docebian babies join the family in 2025!
Connections That Count: Connect with global communities through our Employee Resource Groups (including PRIDE, DWA, BIDOC, and Green Ambassadors) and company-wide events that keep the fun rolling all year long.


About Docebo 💙

At Docebo, we create seamless, AI-powered learning experiences for over 3,000 customers worldwide. We have successfully achieved two IPOs (TSX: DCBO & NASDAQ: DCBO), been recognized as a top SaaS e-learning solution, and are growing exponentially in the process. We're a global company, with office across North America, EMEA, APAC, and beyond. Our team is guided by five core values—Grow Together Win Together, Build with Our Customer, Clear is Kind, Own Outcomes, Progress Over Perfection—that shape everything we do. If this resonates with you, now is the perfect time to join one of the fastest-growing learning technology companies in the world.

Docebo is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. We are committed to diversity and inclusion in our workforce. All qualified applicants and employees will receive consideration for employment regardless of their race, colour, religion, sex (including pregnancy, gender identity, and sexual orientation), national origin, citizenship status, age, disability, genetic information, or any other category protected under applicable law.

As a federal contractor, Docebo is committed to the principles of affirmative action and equal employment opportunity for protected veterans and individuals with disabilities. Docebo does not discriminate because of protected veteran status or on the basis of disability, and Docebo takes affirmative action to employ and advance in employment qualified protected veterans and individuals with disabilities.

Any individuals requiring a reasonable accommodation or would like to voluntarily disclose a disability or protected veteran status to assist with their employment application should send an e-mail to recruiting_accommodations@docebo.com. The email should also include the position you’re interested in.

Compensation Range: CA$54.1K - CA$72.1K
