  • Ability to work and deliver strong results in a dynamic team environment.

Qualifications

  • Post-secondary education in Business, Information Technology, or another related field.
  • At least 3-5 years analytical experience, preferably within a large organization dealing with large data sets.
  • Proficient in Microsoft SQL platform with extensive knowledge of T-SQL development (SSIS, SSAS, SSRS and Power BI).
  • Experience working directly with business users to build reports, dashboards and solving business questions with data.
  • Expertise with front end business intelligence solutions, such as Service Now.
  • High degree of competence with MS Office – good data analysis abilities.
  • Has high level of knowledge of commonly used concepts, practices, and procedures within a particular field.
  • Possesses comprehensive knowledge of subject matter.
  • Experience developing workflow apps in Service Now
  • Primary job functions that require exercising independent judgment.
  • Certification and experience in Service Now development

Other

  • Performs other tasks as assigned.

Company Overview

Serco Canada Inc. strives to make an impact every day across 100+ sites in the areas of Defense, Citizen Services, and Transportation. We help our clients deliver vital services more efficiently while increasing the satisfaction of their end customers. Serco serves the Canadian Federal and Provincial governments and commercial clients. While your place may look a little different depending on your role, we know you will find yours here. Wherever you work and whatever you do, we invite you to discover your place in our world. Serco is a place you can count on and where you can make an impact because every contribution matters.

Pay Transparency

Our Total Rewards package includes competitive pay, performance-based incentives, and benefits that promote well-being and work-life balance—so you can thrive both professionally and personally. Program availability may vary based on factors such as contract type, location, hire date, and applicable collective bargaining agreements.

Salary range: The range for this position can be found at the top of the posting. This range is provided as a general guideline and represents a good faith estimate across all experience levels. Actual base salary will be determined by a variety of factors, including but not limited to, the scope of the role, relevant experience, job-related knowledge, education and training, key skills, and geographic market considerations. For roles available in multiple provinces, the range may vary to reflect differences in local labor markets. In addition to base salary, eligible positions may include other forms of compensation such as annual bonuses or long-term incentive opportunities.

To review all Serco benefits please visit: https://careers.serco-na.com/us/en/about-us.

Serco complies with all applicable federal, provincial, and local leave laws This is a Canada-based role and this position is for an existing vacancy, unless stated otherwise in the job description. Please submit applications exclusively through Serco’s external (or internal) career site. If an applicant has any concerns with job posting compliance, please send an email to: careers@serco-na.com.

Title: Business Analyst
Description:
Job Description:

Rakuten International is a division of Rakuten Group, Inc., a Japanese global technology leader in services that empower individuals, communities, businesses and society. Headquartered in San Mateo, California with more than 4,000 employees worldwide, the Rakuten International business portfolio includes market leaders in e-commerce, digital marketing, advertising, communications and entertainment. We create products and services that provide exceptional value by aligning members and the businesses that want to engage them in a shared community.

Rakuten is the most rewarding way to shop, giving millions of members Cash Back when they buy from their favorite brands. As a leading shopping platform, Rakuten partners with thousands of top brands across apparel, beauty and wellness, grocery, travel, on-demand services, subscriptions, and dining, helping members save on everyday purchases. Since 1999, Rakuten members have earned more than $4.6 billion in Cash Back, making it the largest Cash Back platform of its kind. Learn more at Rakuten.com.

Job Summary

Rakuten Rewards is seeking a data-driven Business Analyst to deliver actionable insights that enhance the experience for both consumers and merchant partners. In this high-impact role, you will partner closely with Product and Engineering teams to evaluate product performance, design experiments, and identify opportunities for optimization. You will leverage large-scale data to inform decision-making, prioritize initiatives, and drive measurable business impact. This role offers strong visibility and the opportunity to influence core product strategy through experimentation and analytics.

Key Responsibilities


Develop analytical frameworks to monitor product and campaign performance across consumer and merchant segments.
Design, execute, and analyze A/B tests and large-scale experiments to drive product improvements.
Partner with Product and Engineering to identify opportunities, estimate impact, and prioritize initiatives.
Build scalable reporting and dashboards using SQL and BI tools to deliver actionable insights to stakeholders.
Conduct deep-dive analyses to explain performance trends and recommend optimization strategies.
Serve as a subject matter expert on available data sets and tools to enhance product experience.
Monitor data quality and integrity within reporting systems, identifying and resolving discrepancies.
Translate complex data findings into clear, actionable business recommendations.
Present insights confidently to senior leadership and cross-functional stakeholders.
Ensure statistical rigor in test design, measurement, and performance analysis.
Maintain documentation of analytical methodologies and reporting standards.
Continuously identify opportunities to improve experimentation and analytics processes.


Qualifications:


Strong SQL skills with experience working in large-scale or Big Data environments.
Experience designing and analyzing A/B tests and experimentation frameworks.
Proficiency in statistical methods for hypothesis testing and performance measurement.
Experience with reporting and visualization tools (Tableau preferred).
Ability to translate analytical findings into clear, business-focused insights.
Strong written and verbal communication skills with confidence presenting to senior stakeholders.
Experience with experimentation platforms (e.g., Optimizely, Adobe Target, SiteSpect) preferred.


Minimum Requirements:


2+ years of experience as a Product or Business Analyst, preferably in retail or e-commerce.
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Mathematics, Economics, Statistics, Engineering, or a related quantitative field required.
Experience in digital product analytics and experimentation environments preferred.


Five Principles for Success

Our worldwide practices describe specific behaviors that make Rakuten unique and united across the world. We expect Rakuten employees to model these 5 Shugi Principles of Success.

Always improve, Always Advance - Only be satisfied with complete success - Kaizen

Passionately Professional - Take an uncompromising approach to your work and be determined to be the best

Hypothesize - Practice - Validate – Shikumika - Use the Rakuten Cycle to succeed in unknown territory

Maximize Customer Satisfaction - The greatest satisfaction for our teams is seeing their customers smile

Speed!! Speed!! Speed!! - Always be conscious of time - take charge, set clear goals, and engage your team

Rakuten is an equal opportunity employer. Accessibility accommodations for candidates with disabilities participating in the selection process are available on request. Any information received related to accommodation needs of applicants will be addressed confidentially.

Rakuten would like to thank all applicants for their interest in this role however only qualified candidates will be shortlisted.

Beware of fraudulent job offers claiming to be from Rakuten. Rakuten does not send unsolicited job offers or request money during the recruitment process. Learn more: https://rakutenemploymentalert.com/

At the time of posting, Rakuten expects the Compensation (base salary + discretionary bonus) for this role to be within the range shown below. Individual compensation will vary based on job-related factors, including the skills, qualifications, and experience of the successful candidate as well as business need and geographic location. The successful applicant for this role will be eligible for stock options, health, vision, dental insurance, RRSP matching, Personal Time Off (PTO), Volunteer Time Off (VTO), and other employee benefits as the company implements.

CAD $92,081.00 - 142,081.00 annually

Title: Analyst, Supply Chain
Description:
Find Your Limitless with Reliance

By joining Reliance as an Analyst, Supply Chain, you’ll become an important Team Member and will be provided with the tools you need to succeed. We have ambitious growth plans and are looking for Team Members who want to grow with us. At Reliance, we pride ourselves on the fact that we provide limitless opportunities for growth and we encourage it by rewarding our Team Members for their success in many ways. If you’re ready to find your limitless – join us!

What do we offer?


The tools you need to succeed – including the tools for the job as well as training and development programs
A successful and knowledgeable team to be part of and learn from
Competitive base pay plus the ability to earn more tied to your success – the only limit is your potential
A great benefits plan for you / your family
Career paths so you can advance in the company
Awarded Canada’s Most Admired Corporate Cultures with a proudly diverse and inclusive team


What will you do?


Demand forecasting: Build SKU/branch forecasts incorporating seasonality, weather, trend, and promo uplifts.
Supply planning: Generate time phased replenishment, transfers, and pre season build ahead plans; manage safety stock, reorder points, and MOQ thresholds.
S&OP cadence: Own analyst inputs into the monthly demand/supply review and consensus meeting.
Promo readiness: Pre promo supply check, mid promo burn rate tracking, post promo wind down to avoid residual stock.
Analytics & reporting: Maintain Power BI dashboards for forecast accuracy, fill rate, stock out risk, aged inventory, and weeks of supply.
Continuous improvement: Drive forecast accuracy, working capital reduction, and network optimization.
Calculate financial impact of vendor price changes, leverage consumption and historical factors to determine annual spend impact to be used in negotiations with suppliers
Generate and publish vendor scorecards monthly, measuring critical supply chain KPI’s
Reporting: Develop and publish various Supply Chain reports


What do you bring?


Bachelor's degree in Supply Chain, Operations, Business, Engineering, Economics, Statistics, or related field.
3–5+ years in demand planning, supply planning, S&OP, or inventory analytics — ideally in multi site distribution, field service, or HVAC.
Statistical demand forecasting with seasonality and promo overlays.
Strong inventory fundamentals: safety stock, reorder points, fill rate trade offs, MOQ economics.
Hands on with planning systems (e.g., Slimstock), ERP, and inventory platforms.
Advanced Excel and Power BI (DAX, dashboards).
Strong cross functional communication and influence.
ML based forecasting exposure.
IFS FSM / 360 Scheduler familiarity.
HVAC seasonality and commodity dynamics (copper, A2L refrigerant).
APICS CPIM/CSCP certification.


Why Reliance?

As a leader in home services and energy efficient solutions for over 60 years, Reliance Home Comfort® (Reliance) is committed to being the Destination of Choice by delivering exceptional experiences for customers seeking comfort and team members seeking a rewarding, inclusive workplace. With over 2 million customer relationships, Reliance is a trusted source and has become one of Canada’s largest providers of water heaters, furnaces, heat pumps, air conditioners, water purification, plumbing, protection plans, smart home, electrical, and EV chargers. Headquartered in Toronto, Reliance employs a workforce of over 2,700 Team Members across 28 branches in Canada and two locations in the United States. Reliance has been certified as a Great Place to Work in 2024 and has been recognized as one of Canada's Most Admired Corporate Cultures by Waterstone Human Capital, for eight consecutive years since 2016. Reliance remains focused on sustainability, workplace safety, diversity, inclusivity, and prudent governance.

This posting is for a new vacancy.

We use AI enabled tools to support parts of our selection process; however, every hiring decision is reviewed and finalized by our people.

Reliance Home Comfort is an equal opportunity employer. We welcome and encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds including minorities and those with disabilities. Accommodations are available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process.

For more information, please visit www.reliancehomecomfort.com.

Title: Data Governance Analyst
Description:
Accelerate your success at Colliers.

Why Colliers?

As one of Canada’s leading commercial real estate services firms, we are driven by our mission to accelerate success for our people, clients, and communities.

A career at Colliers gives you the opportunity to make an impact, advance your career, and collaborate with top-tier talent. Don’t just take our word for it – we’ve been recognized as a top employer on a number of prestigious lists, including Best Workplaces in Canada, Best Workplaces for Women, Best Workplaces with Most Trusted Executive Teams, among others.

Hybrid Role Toronto, ON

About The Role

As a Data Governance Analyst, you will play a key role in supporting our enterprise data governance journey as we build a global property and client intelligence program on the Google Cloud Platform (GCP). Reporting to the Data Governance Manager and collaborating with data engineers and architects, you will help bridge the gap between technical execution and business policy. This is an exciting opportunity to apply your technical skills and growing governance knowledge to help shape the integrity, security, and usability of our global data assets.


Manage the technical metadata repository and business glossaries to ensure our global data assets are easily discoverable.
Profile global datasets to identify anomalies, measure data quality, and determine root causes of issues, and support automated remediation workflows.
Assist in drafting, implementing, and enforcing data standards, naming conventions, and data classification policies across the organization.
Analyze and triage data-related incidents across multiple systems.
Collaborate with domain experts to define and document Critical Data Elements (CDEs) for core property, transaction, and client records.
Support the transition from regional, legacy database environments to our unified global platforms.
Identify opportunities to automate manual governance processes, streamlining operational workflows for engineering and business teams.
Compile and report on governance metrics and KPI dashboards to demonstrate program progress and data quality improvements to leadership. Engagement & Adoption
Support collaborative workshops with business lines and regional data leads to align on consistent global data definitions.
Help develop training materials and documentation to empower and upskill our network of global business data stewards.
Promote a data-driven culture and active stewardship by serving as a supportive liaison between business units and technical teams.
Possess strong SQL querying skills and hands-on experience executing data profiling within a cloud-based data warehouse.
Have a solid understanding of core data governance frameworks, metadata management, and data cataloguing concepts.
Skilled at translating complex technical constraints into clear, business-friendly explanations, and vice-versa.
Experience in configuring and managing Google Cloud Dataplex for automated policy enforcement and data profiling
Awareness of data privacy principles (e.g., PIPEDA, and GDPR)
Possess or working towards professional certifications in data governance (such as CDMP)
Have some experience working within an established or
growing data governance program.
Understanding of real estate industry terminology.
Experience with process documentation or workflow tools.
Have experience working closely with data engineering and architecture teams to embed governance into the data pipeline.


What You Bring


Bachelor’s degree (or equivalent) in a relevant field such as Information Systems, Business, Finance, or Economics, Data Analytics or Computer Science
A minimum of 2 years of experience in data governance, data quality management, or general data analyst roles is required.
Data or technology certifications, examples may include Foundational data analytics & querying
Have successfully collaborated within matrixed organizations to harmonize data across multiple regions and/or lines of business.
Relevant cloud or data governance certifications (e.g., CDMP)
Data governance frameworks and data privacy knowledge
Problem solving
Initiative and continuous learning
Collaboration
Relationship building
Ownership and accountability

Approximate Salary Range for Role $84,250 to $95,000 CAD.

Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. The range displayed on this job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hire salaries for the position across all Canada locations. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training.

This posting is for an existing vacancy within our organization.

Direct applicants only please, no agencies.

Innovative projects Work on cutting-edge initiatives that shape our communities, drive our growth, and make a tangible impact.

Title: Data Platform Operations Analyst
Description:
Requisition ID: 250035

Join a purpose driven winning team, committed to results, in an inclusive and high-performing culture.

The Data Platform Operations Analyst will support the capture of multiple stakeholder requirements to enable the implementation of data management principles along with modern Data Platform Management features. This role provides an excellent opportunity for individuals interested in leading governance of operations in the field of data platforms. This role requires working closely with multiple tenants’ team and assisting in various projects to support data-driven decision-making processes.

Is this role right for you? In this role, you will:


Identify trends, patterns, and outliers on the data platform along with providing improvisations to align platform with recommended solutions by collecting, cleaning and analyzing data from diverse sources to extract meaningful insights
Contribute to the development of data visualizations and presentations for internal stakeholders
Understand and collaborate on requirements from multiple key stakeholders/end users and effectively solve the requirements for Data Platform Management feature delivery
Address process automation and improvement objectives outlined by stakeholders, and collaborate with development team for design and solution feedback
Ensure Data Platform Management features are implemented correctly by working closely with development/testing teams
Work in an Agile mindset to accelerate delivery of data platform governance and management
Provide demonstrations of product features to stakeholder groups in sprint reviews / cadence(s)
Support end user onboarding, and inquiries on data platform management features
Establish and evolve end user documentation; collaborate with fellow team members to maintain documentation


Skills

Do you have the skills that will enable you to succeed in this role? We'd love to work with you if you have:


5+ years of industry experience along with a completed degree in Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or a related field
3+ years of experience on Data Platforms (Data Lake and Data Warehouse), with exposure to various analytics & data project life cycle (e.g. ETL).
3+ years of experience with platform monitoring through tools (Cloudera Manager, Grafana)
2+ years of experience on big data technologies (Cloudera/Hortonworks, Spark, Hive), along with hands-on experience of data manipulation and analysis using tools such as Python or SQL
Able to implement & enhance various dashboards through data visualization tool(s) such as Tableau or PowerBI
Strong communication skills (i.e.: spoken/written/presentation) to engage wide array of users to elicit requirements and feedback
Self-motivated to solve problems and able to work collaboratively with other team members.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Strong process documentation and optimization skills to articulate workflow requirements
Understanding of data management principles would be an asset
Knowledge of agile project methodology and artifacts, on software/application development lifecycle in an agile fashion would be an asset
Knowledge of relational databases (Oracle, SQL Server, MySQL, PostgreSQL) would be an asset
Productivity/Project collaboration tools experience: Confluence, Jira, Sharepoint
Databricks experience would an asset.
Fluency in Spanish Language to converse confidently for work related communication would be an asset


What's in it for you?


Diversity, Equity, Inclusion & Allyship - We strive to create an inclusive culture where every employee is empowered to reach their fullest potential, respected for who they are, and are embraced through bias-free practices and inclusive values across Scotiabank. We embrace diversity and provide opportunities for all employee to learn, grow & participate through our various Employee Resource Groups (ERGs) that span across diverse gender identities, ethnicity, race, age, ability & veterans.
Accessibility and Workplace Accommodations - We value the unique skills and experiences each individual brings to the Bank, and are committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive and accessible environment for everyone. Scotiabank continues to locate, remove and prevent barriers so that we can build a diverse and inclusive environment while meeting accessibility requirements.
Upskilling through online courses, cross-functional development opportunities, and tuition assistance.
Competitive Rewards program including bonus, flexible vacation, personal, sick days and benefits will start on day one.
Dynamic Ecosystem - Free tea & coffee, universal washrooms, and lots of space for team collaboration.
Community Engagement - No matter where you choose to work from; we offer opportunities for community engagement & belonging with our various programs.

Title: Logistics Analyst
Description:
Charger logistics Inc. is a world- class asset-based carrier with locations across North America. With over 20 years of experience providing the best logistics solutions, Charger logistics has transformed into a world-class transport provider and continue to grow.

We are seeking an individual with excellent communication and leadership skills to join the company. Charger logistics invests time and support into its employees to provide them with the room to learn and grow their expertise and work their way up. We are entrepreneurial-minded organization that welcomes and support individual idea and strategies.

We are looking for a data-driven Logistics Analyst to join our team. The ideal candidate will combine strong analytical skills with industry knowledge to drive continuous improvement in our damage mitigation programs.

Responsibilities{{{{:}}}}


Support Sales, Operations, and Customer Service teams with competitive pricing, in-depth performance analysis, and data-driven insights
Perform costing analysis and develop multi-scenario pricing options to support customer negotiations
Assist in building and evaluating pricing strategies; analyze volume, profit potential, and market trends
Monitor customer performance across volume, revenue, and margin; identify gaps and inconsistencies
Respond to pricing inquiries and resolve discrepancies in a timely manner
Write SQL queries to extract, transform, and analyze data from internal operational databases
Build custom reports and analytics dashboards using Tableau and other BI tools; present findings to leadership
Ensure accurate, clean reporting flows directly to leadership and upper management
Organize and maintain internal and external databases to support decision-making
Generate monthly and quarterly damage analysis reports and performance summaries for senior management
Conduct thorough investigations of equipment damage incidents — tractors, trailers, and cargo handling equipment
Analyze damage patterns and root causes; prepare detailed reports with photographic documentation and cost assessments
Review and process damage claims from initial report through full resolution
Coordinate with insurance carriers, repair vendors, and legal teams on complex claims
Negotiate settlements and manage vendor relationships for cost-effective repairs
Maintain accurate claims databases and tracking systems
Identify high-risk routes, facilities, and operational scenarios through trend analysis
Develop and maintain SOPs to reduce costs and improve operational efficiency
Create and implement driver training programs focused on damage prevention
Work with safety teams to establish best practices; conduct cost-benefit analyses for prevention initiatives
Present findings and recommendations to cross-functional teams


Requirements


Bachelor's degree in Business Analytics, Supply Chain, Statistics, Engineering, or related field
3+ years of experience in transportation, logistics, or insurance industries is an asset
Strong analytical skills is preferred
Experience with claims management systems and processes
Knowledge of commercial vehicle operations and equipment types
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Detail-oriented with strong problem-solving abilities.


Preferred Qualifications{{{{:}}}}


Experience in trucking, freight, or transportation industry
Professional certifications in risk management or claims analysis
Familiarity with DOT regulations and commercial vehicle safety standards
Background in fleet management or equipment maintenance


Benefits


Competitive Salary
Healthcare Benefit Package
Career Growth

Title: Insights Analyst
Description:
Reports to: Business Insights Manager

Department: Commercial Capabilities

Location: Etobicoke, On (Hybrid)







The pay range for the role is $62,600 to $104,300 plus bonus, comprehensive benefits, retirement savings plan, paid vacation, and other perks and opportunities.







Purpose:

We are charting a visionary future for Gay Lea Foods, aiming to achieve industry-leading results that will ensure the long-term prosperity of our esteemed Canadian dairy farmer-owned co-operative. As we embark on this growth journey, we are seeking an Insights Analyst who will play an integral part of the planning for the company’s growth.

In this role the incumbent contributes to the commercial capabilities team’s strategy to achieve growth that will strengthen the business, enable intelligent innovation, and deliver profits that sustain the Gay Lea Foods Co-operative model.

Reporting to the Business Insights Manager, the Insights Analyst will play a key support role in the development of tools, analytics, and actionable insights. With the use of Nielsen & other syndicated data the Insights Analyst will help develop and implement best-in-class category/industry recommendations. You will work closely and collaboratively with the entire commercial capabilities team to implement data-driven initiatives and support any ad-hoc reviews. This person will have the ability to analyze and interpret consumer, industry and sales data into actionable insights and recommendations. They will also support the development of automated reporting and query tools in an effort to increase insight the utilization of data and insights across the organization.







Key Responsibilities:

Provide regular and timely updates to the business regarding insights into shopper, category, channel and customer trends.
Support the business unit through detailed analysis with the data available across multiple retailers & industries to evaluate the performance of Gay Lea brands and competition.
Understand what elements of the marketing mix (pricing, promotion, assortment, shelving & competition) are driving results.
Develop and maintain standardized reporting utilizing advanced analytic platforms to enable the commercial team to create and track strategic objectives.
Develop automated data tools using Power BI and Excel.
Partner with the Insights Manager to Interpret complex consumer and sales data to develop clear, actionable recommendations.
Additional responsibilities as needed.







Qualifications:

Post Secondary Education.
3-5 years of experience working in a CPG Company or National Retailer.
Working knowledge of Nielsen tools/platforms and POS/AA systems (LDIA, IRI, Retail Link, etc.).
Experience in analyzing shopper and customer data is required.
Strong analytical background with advanced proficiency in Excel and PowerPoint.
Proficiency in Power BI, including DAX, Power Query, and data modeling.
Strong attention to detail and ability to execute with excellence.
Self-motivated with high energy, ability to multi-task and execute multiple projects on time.
Strong organization skills and proven ability to manage several projects at one time.
Strong presentation skills and the ability to inspire and influence others through story telling.







Gay Lea Foods is a Canadian, dairy farmer‑owned co‑operative with a clear purpose: to enrich life with dairy. Founded in 1958, the co‑operative produces high‑quality dairy products while creating lasting value for farming families, meaningful opportunities for employees, and positive impact in communities across Canada. As a co‑operative, Gay Lea Foods believes that when the business succeeds, its members, people, and communities should succeed along with it. To learn more, visit gaylea.com.

Gay Lea Foods is an equal-opportunity employer and values diversity in our workplace.

Reasonable accommodation is available upon request throughout our recruitment and selection process.

Gay Lea Foods may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support aspects of the recruitment process, including screening and assessment.

We are currently accepting applications for this open position.




Thank you for your interest in Gay Lea Foods.

Wishing you all the best with your career search!

Title: Data Scientist, Product Analytics
Description:
At Leap Tools, we are building the world's most advanced solutions for the interior décor industry. With customers in 80+ countries, our clientele includes Fortune 500 companies such as Home Depot, local retailers such as Alexanian's, and everything in between. We have been recognized as one of the fastest-growing tech companies by Deloitte for multiple years in a row, and we are looking for ambitious challenge-seekers to fuel our momentum and help us create an iconic global tech company.

About Our Product

Our technology lets you preview products in your own room before you buy them. Imagine you want to redesign your home and have been searching for new tiles for your kitchen, or a new rug for your living room. You definitely want to make sure it will look good in your space. We enable you to do that through our proprietary cutting-edge technology, presented in an extraordinarily simple and accessible way. Try our rug demo now! Simply upload a picture of your room using your mobile phone, and slide the rug under your coffee table: https://www.roomvo.com/rugdemo...

About You

You take pride in exceeding expectations. You love the satisfaction that comes from building and improving products using data and analytics. You uncover needs by listening closely and asking perceptive questions. You can translate these needs to analytical problems to address them. You pay close attention to details and it bugs you when others do not. You enjoy working with and manipulating data. You have a keen eye for how things should work and you use data to prove (or disprove) your assertions. You’re vigilant about keeping a high bar of quality but you’re not afraid to try something new. You are excited to take on new challenges and be part of something small that is growing into something big.

We hire humans, not job descriptions. You should apply even if this role and salary range don't align with your experience. We’re happy to create unique roles and compensation for the right talent.

About Our Tech Stack

Python (pandas, matplotlib, scikit, numpy, scipy), Superset, Redshift, PostgreSQL, Django, dbt

What You’ll Do


Use a variety of analytical and statistical approaches to solve key business problems, working with large and complex datasets
Collaborate with product managers and engineers to analyze user behavior, product usage, and feature adoption to improve the product and the user experience
Conduct and evaluate A/B tests and communicate results to drive product optimization and to empower customer-facing teams to drive adoption of new products / features
Work with product managers to identify key product metrics and build dashboards for monitoring performance
Proactively identify and interpret trends in the data to inform product development efforts, sales efforts and thought leadership in the home decor industry
Utilize statistical methods and machine learning techniques for a variety of use cases, such as predicting future trends and customer segmentation
Leverage our data to drive insights for individual customers, helping them understand user behavior and preferences for targeted marketing strategies


Requirements


Distinctive work experience in data preprocessing, data wrangling, feature engineering, data analysis and statistical modelling, delivering polished customer-ready insights
Exceptional attention to detail and focus on quality
Excellent communication to convey complex findings to non-technical stakeholders
Comfortable engaging with customers and senior executives
Technical background, demonstrated through a degree in statistics, mathematics, or other related technical field, or alternatively through relevant work experience
Proficiency in data querying languages (e.g., SQL) and at least one scripting language used for analysis (e.g., Python)


About Our Culture


We're a remote-first company that encourages our employees to work from where they're most productive.
We work in tight-knit teams to cultivate an ownership mentality.
We cherish curiosity and an obsession for details because we know these details are invaluable over the long run.
We're hyper-focused on our achievements and our ability to execute our promises. We act with urgency.
Work anywhere in the world for up to 3 months!
We value families, by offering a parental leave program
We offer a work-from-home stipend
Your birthday (and our company's birthday) is a day off!


About Our Hiring Process

Now: You upload your resume and complete a brief questionnaire.

Step 1: We arrange a video call with you to assess your abilities, and ask you to complete a take-home assessment.

Steps 2 and 3: You attend two video interviews soon after.

Step 4: You meet one of the founders.

Step 5: You receive an offer.

AI in Recruitment - At Leap Tools, we leverage AI technology to enhance our recruitment process. These tools assist with tasks such as resume screening, sourcing prospective candidates, and to support administrative tasks for enhanced operational efficiency. Founders and senior leadership are directly involved in our recruitment process, and AI is never used to make the final hiring decision. We are committed to the responsible use of AI in our hiring practices.

Expected compensation range, Ontario based: $80,000 - $110,000 + other benefits.

We value exceptional talent above all else. If your expectations or seniority sit outside the stated range, you should still apply. We can scale roles and compensation to match your unique situation.

This is an existing vacancy on the team.

Leap Tools is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering an inclusive, equitable, and accessible environment. Accommodations are available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the interview process. If you require any accommodation, please contact us at ta@leaptools.com.

Take the Leap. Apply now.

Our demo, in case you missed it: https://www.roomvo.com/rugdemo4r

You should apply to this job even if you don't fit this role perfectly because we can create a new role for you with corresponding compensation.
Show more

Title: Business Analyst
Description:
Job Description:

Rakuten International is a division of Rakuten Group, Inc., a Japanese global technology leader in services that empower individuals, communities, businesses and society. Headquartered in San Mateo, California with more than 4,000 employees worldwide, the Rakuten International business portfolio includes market leaders in e-commerce, digital marketing, advertising, communications and entertainment. We create products and services that provide exceptional value by aligning members and the businesses that want to engage them in a shared community.

Rakuten is the most rewarding way to shop, giving millions of members Cash Back when they buy from their favorite brands. As a leading shopping platform, Rakuten partners with thousands of top brands across apparel, beauty and wellness, grocery, travel, on-demand services, subscriptions, and dining, helping members save on everyday purchases. Since 1999, Rakuten members have earned more than $4.6 billion in Cash Back, making it the largest Cash Back platform of its kind. Learn more at Rakuten.com.

Job Summary

Rakuten Rewards is seeking a data-driven Business Analyst to deliver actionable insights that enhance the experience for both consumers and merchant partners. In this high-impact role, you will partner closely with Product and Engineering teams to evaluate product performance, design experiments, and identify opportunities for optimization. You will leverage large-scale data to inform decision-making, prioritize initiatives, and drive measurable business impact. This role offers strong visibility and the opportunity to influence core product strategy through experimentation and analytics.

Key Responsibilities


Develop analytical frameworks to monitor product and campaign performance across consumer and merchant segments.
Design, execute, and analyze A/B tests and large-scale experiments to drive product improvements.
Partner with Product and Engineering to identify opportunities, estimate impact, and prioritize initiatives.
Build scalable reporting and dashboards using SQL and BI tools to deliver actionable insights to stakeholders.
Conduct deep-dive analyses to explain performance trends and recommend optimization strategies.
Serve as a subject matter expert on available data sets and tools to enhance product experience.
Monitor data quality and integrity within reporting systems, identifying and resolving discrepancies.
Translate complex data findings into clear, actionable business recommendations.
Present insights confidently to senior leadership and cross-functional stakeholders.
Ensure statistical rigor in test design, measurement, and performance analysis.
Maintain documentation of analytical methodologies and reporting standards.
Continuously identify opportunities to improve experimentation and analytics processes.


Qualifications:


Strong SQL skills with experience working in large-scale or Big Data environments.
Experience designing and analyzing A/B tests and experimentation frameworks.
Proficiency in statistical methods for hypothesis testing and performance measurement.
Experience with reporting and visualization tools (Tableau preferred).
Ability to translate analytical findings into clear, business-focused insights.
Strong written and verbal communication skills with confidence presenting to senior stakeholders.
Experience with experimentation platforms (e.g., Optimizely, Adobe Target, SiteSpect) preferred.


Minimum Requirements:


2+ years of experience as a Product or Business Analyst, preferably in retail or e-commerce.
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Mathematics, Economics, Statistics, Engineering, or a related quantitative field required.
Experience in digital product analytics and experimentation environments preferred.


Five Principles for Success

Our worldwide practices describe specific behaviors that make Rakuten unique and united across the world. We expect Rakuten employees to model these 5 Shugi Principles of Success.

Always improve, Always Advance - Only be satisfied with complete success - Kaizen

Passionately Professional - Take an uncompromising approach to your work and be determined to be the best

Hypothesize - Practice - Validate – Shikumika - Use the Rakuten Cycle to succeed in unknown territory

Maximize Customer Satisfaction - The greatest satisfaction for our teams is seeing their customers smile

Speed!! Speed!! Speed!! - Always be conscious of time - take charge, set clear goals, and engage your team

Rakuten is an equal opportunity employer. Accessibility accommodations for candidates with disabilities participating in the selection process are available on request. Any information received related to accommodation needs of applicants will be addressed confidentially.

Rakuten would like to thank all applicants for their interest in this role however only qualified candidates will be shortlisted.

Beware of fraudulent job offers claiming to be from Rakuten. Rakuten does not send unsolicited job offers or request money during the recruitment process. Learn more: https://rakutenemploymentalert.com/

At the time of posting, Rakuten expects the Compensation (base salary + discretionary bonus) for this role to be within the range shown below. Individual compensation will vary based on job-related factors, including the skills, qualifications, and experience of the successful candidate as well as business need and geographic location. The successful applicant for this role will be eligible for stock options, health, vision, dental insurance, RRSP matching, Personal Time Off (PTO), Volunteer Time Off (VTO), and other employee benefits as the company implements.

CAD $92,081.00 - 142,081.00 annually

Title: Data Platform Operations Analyst
Description:
Requisition ID: 250035

Join a purpose driven winning team, committed to results, in an inclusive and high-performing culture.

The Data Platform Operations Analyst will support the capture of multiple stakeholder requirements to enable the implementation of data management principles along with modern Data Platform Management features. This role provides an excellent opportunity for individuals interested in leading governance of operations in the field of data platforms. This role requires working closely with multiple tenants’ team and assisting in various projects to support data-driven decision-making processes.

Is this role right for you? In this role, you will:


Identify trends, patterns, and outliers on the data platform along with providing improvisations to align platform with recommended solutions by collecting, cleaning and analyzing data from diverse sources to extract meaningful insights
Contribute to the development of data visualizations and presentations for internal stakeholders
Understand and collaborate on requirements from multiple key stakeholders/end users and effectively solve the requirements for Data Platform Management feature delivery
Address process automation and improvement objectives outlined by stakeholders, and collaborate with development team for design and solution feedback
Ensure Data Platform Management features are implemented correctly by working closely with development/testing teams
Work in an Agile mindset to accelerate delivery of data platform governance and management
Provide demonstrations of product features to stakeholder groups in sprint reviews / cadence(s)
Support end user onboarding, and inquiries on data platform management features
Establish and evolve end user documentation; collaborate with fellow team members to maintain documentation


Skills

Do you have the skills that will enable you to succeed in this role? We'd love to work with you if you have:


5+ years of industry experience along with a completed degree in Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or a related field
3+ years of experience on Data Platforms (Data Lake and Data Warehouse), with exposure to various analytics & data project life cycle (e.g. ETL).
3+ years of experience with platform monitoring through tools (Cloudera Manager, Grafana)
2+ years of experience on big data technologies (Cloudera/Hortonworks, Spark, Hive), along with hands-on experience of data manipulation and analysis using tools such as Python or SQL
Able to implement & enhance various dashboards through data visualization tool(s) such as Tableau or PowerBI
Strong communication skills (i.e.: spoken/written/presentation) to engage wide array of users to elicit requirements and feedback
Self-motivated to solve problems and able to work collaboratively with other team members.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Strong process documentation and optimization skills to articulate workflow requirements
Understanding of data management principles would be an asset
Knowledge of agile project methodology and artifacts, on software/application development lifecycle in an agile fashion would be an asset
Knowledge of relational databases (Oracle, SQL Server, MySQL, PostgreSQL) would be an asset
Productivity/Project collaboration tools experience: Confluence, Jira, Sharepoint
Databricks experience would an asset.
Fluency in Spanish Language to converse confidently for work related communication would be an asset


What's in it for you?


Diversity, Equity, Inclusion & Allyship - We strive to create an inclusive culture where every employee is empowered to reach their fullest potential, respected for who they are, and are embraced through bias-free practices and inclusive values across Scotiabank. We embrace diversity and provide opportunities for all employee to learn, grow & participate through our various Employee Resource Groups (ERGs) that span across diverse gender identities, ethnicity, race, age, ability & veterans.
Accessibility and Workplace Accommodations - We value the unique skills and experiences each individual brings to the Bank, and are committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive and accessible environment for everyone. Scotiabank continues to locate, remove and prevent barriers so that we can build a diverse and inclusive environment while meeting accessibility requirements.
Upskilling through online courses, cross-functional development opportunities, and tuition assistance.
Competitive Rewards program including bonus, flexible vacation, personal, sick days and benefits will start on day one.
Dynamic Ecosystem - Free tea & coffee, universal washrooms, and lots of space for team collaboration.
Community Engagement - No matter where you choose to work from; we offer opportunities for community engagement & belonging with our various programs.


#AIDataPlatforms

Location(s): Canada : Ontario : Toronto

Scotiabank is a leading bank in the Americas. Guided by our purpose: "for every future", we help our customers, their families and their communities achieve success through a broad range of advice, products and services, including personal and commercial banking, wealth management and private banking, corporate and investment banking, and capital markets.

At Scotiabank, we value the unique skills and experiences each individual brings to the Bank, and are committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive and accessible environment for everyone. If you require accommodation (including, but not limited to, an accessible interview site, alternate format documents, ASL Interpreter, or Assistive Technology) during the recruitment and selection process, please let our Recruitment team know. If you require technical assistance, please click here. Candidates must apply directly online to be considered for this role. We thank all applicants for their interest in a career at Scotiabank; however, only those candidates who are selected for an interview will be contacted.

Title: Industrial Engineering - Data analyst
Description:
Industrial Engineering – Data analyst

Reporting to the Team Lead (Industrial Engineering) – Industrial Engineering- Data Analyst, will support the Industrial Engineering team by collecting, analyzing, and interpreting production and operational data to drive continuous improvement initiatives. This role is responsible for developing data-driven insights, creating interactive dashboards, and managing job creation processes within MHICA systems and MES.

Shifts

Monday to Friday; 6 am – 2:30 pm

Compensation


Competitive compensation packages
Salary range: $75,000 - $85,000
Skills Progression Program to allow you to have clear, transparent, and an objective criterion that allows YOU to take charge of YOUR career knowing what is required to reach each level and do so at your own pace. We want to put your career progression in your control
Comprehensive Benefits (health care, dental care, life and disability coverage) & RRSP group contribution programs with RRSP increasing based on seniority
All-encompassing Employee Assistance Program (EAP) with 24/7 confidential support
Corporate Partnerships (health and wellness, recreation and leisure, etc.)
Company bonus incentive plan – which increases based on years of service
Retirement bonus based on age and years of service
Company events and celebrations throughout the year
Long service vacation cash out at year end
Recognition program
Reimbursement Programs including



Uniform – production



Mr. Safety Shoe – work boots allowance



Prescription safety glasses



Childcare subsidy

What To Expect From Us


Industry Information
Tier 1 Aero Structures Facility
Training & Development



Extensive training programs



Employee referral program



Professional development programs



Employee engagement programs all year round including



Year-round events



MHICA Wings Peer to Peer Recognition Program



Leadership programs

Responsibilities


Extract, organize, and analyze production, performance, and labor data from various sources (MES, MHICA I-Tag system, ERP, etc.).
Create, manage, and update production jobs within MES and MHICA I-Tag systems.
Develop and maintain Power BI dashboards and automated reports to support Industrial Engineering and Operations.
Write and optimize SQL queries to extract and transform data for analysis and reporting.
Monitor and validate data integrity across multiple systems and ensure consistency in job creation and tracking.
Support time studies, process efficiency analysis, and productivity tracking.
Collaborate with Industrial Engineers to identify trends, bottlenecks, and improvement opportunities in production workflows.
Assist in developing key performance indicators (KPIs) and reporting frameworks for departmental and site-level performance.
Partner with IT and Operations teams to enhance data accessibility and reliability.
Document procedures, create user guides, and train team members on dashboards, data tools, and systems use.


Qualifications

Skills & Qualifications


Proficiency in Power BI for data visualization and dashboard development.
Strong command of SQL for querying, data extraction, and reporting.
Familiarity with job creation and maintenance within MES and MHICA systems.
Advanced Excel skills (pivot tables, macros, data modeling).
Understanding of data structures, ETL processes, and industrial data flow.
Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities.
Excellent attention to detail and data accuracy.
Effective communication and collaboration skills.
Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.
Current work authorization for Canada is required for all openings.
Please note: A criminal background check is required for candidates aged 18 and over. Instructions and next steps will be provided as part of the offer process.


Experience


Minimum 2–4 years of experience in data analytics, preferably within a manufacturing or industrial environment.
Hands-on experience with MES systems, or similar job tracking and production management systems.


Education


Bachelor’s degree in industrial engineering, Data Analytics, Computer Science, or a related field.


We value the individual behind every application. Each resume is reviewed by our recruitment team - we do not use automated AI screening to assess resumes.

About Us

MHI Canada Aerospace, Inc. (MHICA), a group company of Mitsubishi Heavy Industries, is a Tier 1 manufacturer of major aircraft structures and assemblies, based in Mississauga, Ontario.

Over the past decade, MHICA has built more than aircraft components, it has built a recognized worldwide reputation for capacity, precision, on-time delivery and excellence. MHICA has two state-of-the-art facilities combined to 476,000 sq. ft. This comprises of Manufacturing & Assembly, Engineering, Quality and Supply Chain, where more than 800 highly-skilled employees are working on Bombardier's sector-leading Global 5000/6000 and Challenger 350 business aircraft. MHICA's technicians build and join wing assemblies and fuselage sections, as well as perform systems and flight control assembly installations and testing.

MHI Canada Aerospace is an equal opportunity employer and encourages women, aboriginal people, persons with disabilities and members of visible minorities to apply. MHICA is committed to providing employment accommodation in accordance with the Ontario Human Rights Code and the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act, 2005 (AODA). MHICA will provide accommodations to job applicants with disabilities throughout the recruitment process. If you require an accommodation, please notify us and we will work with you to meet your needs.

We thank all applicants in advance for their interest; however, only those candidates selected for an interview will be contacted.

Title: Fraud Analyst
Description:
About This Opportunity

At Meridian our aspiration is to integrate our purpose into everything we do for people, the planet, and communities. We believe that our greatest opportunity is to create opportunity and meet people where they are.

Reporting to the Manager, Fraud Analytics, this role is responsible to provide analysis, insights, and intelligence to aid in the delivery of a proactive fraud prevention and detection strategy, in line with policy, risk appetite, and appropriate standards. The analyst will support the development and implementation of reports, controls, policies, procedures, scorecards, and dashboards, and provide monitoring and guidance for the Meridian Credit Union, and Meridian OneCap (collectively, “Meridian”) Fraud Management & Corporate Security programs.

Is this role right for you? In this role, you will:


Conduct control reporting on existing detection strategies and countermeasures, and proactively drive incremental improvements.
Assist Fraud Management & Corporate Security leadership to develop and implement a quality control program. Escalate insights to Fraud Management & Corporate Security leadership to drive program change.
Conduct periodic quality control analysis to ensure policies, procedures, and internal frameworks are adhered to, and maintain the Fraud Management & Corporate Security program in an always-audit-ready state.
Identify enhancements to detection systems and approaches, coordinate implementation with business and technology partners, and perform post-implementation monitoring.
Conduct root cause analysis driving large individual losses or emerging fraud trends, and develop strategies and controls to mitigate their impact.
Aggregate and update existing periodic reporting, and work with Manager to enhance, automate, and streamline dashboarding and reporting for Fraud Management and Corporate Security.
Maintain and create documentation of risk-management frameworks, controls, systems, models, and tools employed by the program as required.
Provide suggestions on rules, models, and strategies for Meridian’s fraud detection systems and tools based on analysis and insights gained as part of quality control functions.
Assist fraud investigators, analysts, Manager, Fraud Analytics and Senior Manager, Fraud Management in the analysis of applications and complex cases as required to detect fraud patterns, develop review frameworks for other internal partners, and develop strategies to counter emerging threats.
Identify, cleanse, map, aggregate, and document new data sources and work with other analysts and peers to answer business questions and develop ways to connect and use the new data sources with existing databases/data warehouse as it applies to reporting and the quality control function.
Support the Fraud Management & Corporate Security team leadership on:
the development of intelligence and communication on fraud trends to prevent fraud losses;
the assessment of industry/third party tools, from credit bureaus, fintechs, or other vendors, which can advance the Meridian Fraud Management program;
conducting research into industry best practices, trends, and capabilities;
the identification of system enhancements to improve the Fraud Management & Corporate Security programs.
Foster and maintain productive relationships with internal and external stakeholders, partners, and industry peers.
Assist the Manager in operationalizing analysis and insights by working with third-party vendors and internal partners to communicate requirements and risks.
Communicate project status, developments, and issues with the Manager and other Fraud leadership proactively.
Create or support the creation of monthly, quarterly, and ad-hoc reports covering fraud metrics, fraud strategy performance, and trends analysis.
Assist the Manager to research, scope, create, and present business cases required to acquire new tools and resources to augment the Meridian Fraud Management Program.
Support the Fraud Management & Corporate Security team in the execution of short-term and multi-year plans to achieve program and Meridian goals.


We’d love to work with you if you have the following skills and abilities:


Advanced knowledge of Microsoft Power BI. You know how to capture and work with data, and transform, analyze, and visualize the results.
Proficient at using Microsoft SQL Server to extract and isolate data. Working knowledge of Python or other tools (e.g. SAS, R) to complete data aggregation, cleansing, analysis, and automation.
Hands-on experience with low-code or no-code automation tools such as Power Automate, Power Apps, or comparable platforms to increase efficiency in reporting and workflow processes.
Familiarity with cloud analytics solutions such as Microsoft Fabric, Databricks, or Snowflake is considered an asset.
An understanding of the financial services industry, gained through a combination of experience, self-directed research, and learning is a nice to have.

Highly developed analytical and problem-solving skills. Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

Title: Business Analyst
Description:
Overview

Adastra is looking for a Business Analyst who turns complex enterprise data into clear, actionable solutions. You’ll map data flows, define requirements, and collaborate with business and technical teams to make sure reporting and analytics truly deliver value. If you enjoy solving complex problems with data and shaping how teams make decisions, this role puts you at the center of it all.

Primary Location: Toronto, ON

Work Model: Hybrid 3-Days Onsite

Employment Type: Permanent Full-Time

Vacancy Status: New

Compensation Range: $53,000 - $63,000 CAD

Responsibilities


Elicit, gather, and document business and technical requirements for data-driven projects
Produce functional and non-functional business requirements, solution specifications, and workflow/process documentation
Analyze enterprise data and identify sources to support reporting, analytics, and data warehouse initiatives
Create and maintain Source-to-Target Mapping (STM) documents for data warehouse / data mart projects
Document the flow and transformation of data from source systems to Data Warehouse/Data Marts and from Data Warehouse/Data Marts to downstream applications
Collaborate with business sponsors, technology teams, and cross-functional stakeholders to ensure delivered solutions meet business intent and provide value
Perform data profiling and analysis using SQL to validate data, derive complex business logic, and support reporting/analytics needs
Assist with user acceptance testing (UAT), data validation, and other quality assurance activities
Coordinate with multiple teams, manage priorities, and handle ambiguous requirements effectively



Qualifications, Skills & Experience


Bachelor’s degree in Business, Information Systems, Computer Science, or related field
3+ years of experience as a Business Analyst, preferably in Data Warehouse / Data Mart environments
Hands-on experience eliciting business requirements and producing functional/non-functional specifications
Strong SQL skills, with the ability to write complex queries for data extraction and analysis
Experience creating and maintaining Source-to-Target Mapping (STM) documents
Familiarity with enterprise applications and data management processes
Strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and ability to translate ambiguous requirements into actionable outcomes
Excellent communication, stakeholder engagement, and documentation skills
Ability to work effectively in cross-functional teams with both business and technical groups
Strong organization, coordination, and time management skills



NICE TO HAVE


Experience with call center or customer data environments
Familiarity with data governance, compliance, or security standards in analytics projects
Experience with BI tools beyond Power BI, such as Tableau or Looker
Exposure to Agile project delivery and tools like Jira or Confluence
Understanding of business system architecture and enterprise application integrations
Ability to perform data modeling or advanced analytics to support reporting and decision-making



About Adastra

Adastra is a global leader in AI and data-driven transformation, helping organizations lead with artificial intelligence—responsibly, strategically, and at scale. With over 25 years of experience, Adastra empowers enterprise clients to unlock business value through data innovation, operational excellence, and smart customer engagement.

Trusted by some of the world’s most prominent brands, Adastra delivers end-to-end solutions grounded in thoughtful strategy, robust governance, and deep technical expertise. From defining vision to ensuring execution, Adastra guides organizations through every stage of their AI, data and cloud journey—building future-ready capabilities and delivering measurable, lasting impact.

Adastra serves clients across key industries including financial services, automotive, manufacturing, technology, media and telecom (TMT), healthcare, retail, and professional services. The company employs more than 2,000 professionals across North America, Europe, and Asia.

What We Offer


Opportunity for advancement and career progression
Competitive compensation package
Comprehensive benefits plan
Successful referral program
The opportunity to work with one of Canada’s 50 Best Managed Companies
Satisfaction of working for a reputable company
A flexible, dynamic, and diverse workplace



EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER

In our commitment to promote fair and equitable treatment of all employees and applicants, Adastra Corporation provides equal employment opportunities for all individuals regardless of age, sex, disability, race, ethnic origin, citizenship, creed, sexual orientation, marital status, or any other ground as described in the Ontario Human Rights Code. In addition, accommodation will be provided during the hiring process. Adastra Corporation’s implementation and support of employment initiatives, encourage diversified labour force participation and equal access to opportunities based on merit and performance.

AI Usage - Our hiring process includes the use of AI-enabled tools to screen applications (e.g., keyword matching, qualification ranking). A human recruiter reviews all AI-generated shortlists to make informed hiring decisions.

There has been an increased instance of fraudulent job offers coming from people posing as Adastra HR employees. Please note that Adastra will never request fees as part of our recruiting process and any emails sent to you that are not from ‘@adastragrp.com’ or ‘@talent.icims.com’ are fraudulent. All employment offers are sent via DocuSign and if you receive an employment offer from a suspicious email and not via DocuSign, it is fraudulent.

Contact careers@adastragrp.com to inquire about jobs at Adastra, to report a suspicious request for money or personal information from external websites or suspicious employment offers.

Title: Data Architect & Business Intelligence
Description:
When applicable, Bombardier promotes flexible and hybrid work policies.

Why join us?

At Bombardier, we design, build and maintain the world’s peak-performing aircraft for the world’s most discerning people and businesses, governments and militaries. We have been successful in setting the highest standards by putting our people at the heart of it all, and defining excellence, together. 

Working at Bombardier means operating at the highest level. Every day, you are part of a team that delivers superior experiences and products, pushing the boundaries of what's possible in our industry and beyond. By prioritizing employee growth and development, we empower everyone to reach their full potential on their own terms, because the best work happens when you are free to be yourself and share your unique expertise.

Benefits

Our Benefits

With our employees’ well-being top of mind, we offer a comprehensive and competitive Benefits Program, which includes the following:


Insurance plans (Medical, life insurance, and more)
Employee Assistance Program
Competitive base salary



What are your contributions to the team?


Participate in our data transformation with the design, development, and implementation of business intelligence solutions across materials management focused on driving actions in support of our core business objectives.
Develop SQL scripts to create reports from our data warehouse (SQL server) or Microsoft Azure Data Lake.
Support and develop Python scripts for data transformation (ETL) to reproduce existing analysis tool or to build new ones in collaboration with other business analysts.
Develop, improve and maintain our Data Governance tools in Access & Power BI.
Facilitate collaborative sessions with cross functional business stakeholders to drive discovery, data analysis, review to gain a more in-depth understanding of problem statements, define business requirements and document the project scope.
Lead and/or participate in continuous improvement initiatives to augment the materials management team’s operational processes efficiencies.  
Analyze, develop problem statements, and utilize data to drive solutions and insight into our inventory control practices and supply chain processes within a worldwide parts network to improve parts availability and / or inventory turns.
Validate all development work for quality and ensure requirements are met with the relevant stakeholders.
Conduct walk-through and sign off sessions. 
Build and maintain a repository of reusable documentation to support future initiatives and training.
Provide ad-hoc analysis requirements as needed



How to thrive in this role?


You hold a Bachelor degree in Computer or Software Engineering, or other related fields combined with 2 to 5 years of experience in developing business intelligence solutions in a supply chain environment.  
Masters or other relevant certification, after-market supply chain management and aerospace industry context knowledge are considered as strong assets.
You exhibit a continuous improvement mindset with relevant experience in improving business processes. 
You have a passion and strong capacity for big data analytics using relevant technologies to automate, perform data transformation, build business intelligence, and improve analytical insights. 
You hold knowledge of core supply chain concepts, processes, and systems. Able to understand how different departments and processes intersect to impact supply chain processes. 
You have appropriate interpersonal skills and communication methods to effectively develop presentations, present business cases and communicate to all levels of the organization. 
You are comfortable working with big data, cloud-based computing, interacting with IT solutions and technical personnel.  
You are proficient with MS Suite (Required), Advance excel (Required), Power BI (Required), Azure Synapse (Required), Azure Databricks (Required), Python (Required),  SQL (Required), Access (Preferred) with the ability and excitement to learn new tools. 
Occasional travel may be required in this role.  
Bilingualism in French and English is mandatory for Montreal, and an asset for Toronto.



Now that you can see yourself in this role, apply and join the Bombardier Team!

Please note: You don’t need all the skills, knowledge, and experience listed to apply for this position! We’re not looking for the perfect candidate, we’re looking for great talent and passionate individuals.

Bombardier is an equal opportunity employer and encourages persons of any race, religion, ethnicity, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, immigration status, disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics to apply.

  • You have appropriate interpersonal skills and communication methods to effectively develop presentations, present business cases and communicate to all levels of the organization. 
  • You are comfortable working with big data, cloud-based computing, interacting with IT solutions and technical personnel.  
  • You are proficient with MS Suite (Required), Advance excel (Required), Power BI (Required), Azure Synapse (Required), Azure Databricks (Required), Python (Required),  SQL (Required), Access (Preferred) with the ability and excitement to learn new tools. 
  • Occasional travel may be required in this role.  
  • Bilingualism in French and English is mandatory for Montreal, and an asset for Toronto.


Now that you can see yourself in this role, apply and join the Bombardier Team!

Please note: You don’t need all the skills, knowledge, and experience listed to apply for this position! We’re not looking for the perfect candidate, we’re looking for great talent and passionate individuals.

Bombardier is an equal opportunity employer and encourages persons of any race, religion, ethnicity, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, immigration status, disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics to apply.

Title: Forecasting Analyst – Amazon.ca Sales Team
Description:
CREATIVITY IS OUR SUPERPOWER. It’s our heritage and it’s also our future. Because we don’t just make toys. We create innovative products and experiences that inspire fans, entertain audiences and develop children through play. Mattel is at its best when every member of our team feels respected, included, and heard—when everyone can show up as themselves and do their best work every day. We value and share an infinite range of ideas and voices that evolve and broaden our perspectives with a reach that extends into all our brands, partners, and suppliers.

The Team:
Join the Canada Sales organization on the Amazon Sales Team—where data meets growth. This team drives topline performance through sharp forecasting, strong in-stock execution, and disciplined inventory management. You’ll partner across Sales, Supply Chain, and Amazon to turn demand signals into results.

What Your Impact Will Be


Own item-level forecasts and monthly shipment plans aligned to consumer demand and business constraints
Build and refine forecasting models using POS trends, seasonality, promotions, and product history
Drive forecast accuracy measure performance, identify root causes, and implement improvements
Lead forecasting discussions in Amazon Joint Business Planning meetings; present insights and action plans
Partner with Sales and Amazon teams on in-season execution, promotions, and order decisions (chase/cancel)
Manage replenishment and PO flow; recommend domestic and import orders
Monitor and improve key metrics (in-stock, weeks of cover, fill rate)
Act as liaison across customer service, supply planning, and allocation teams
Deliver clear, actionable analytics to support business decisions


What We’re Looking For


3–5 years of experience in forecasting, demand planning, or analytics
Strong analytical skills with experience managing large SKU portfolios (500–1000+) and translating data into actionable insights
Experience with statistical forecasting and retail/customer replenishment processes
Familiarity with Amazon Vendor Central, Cognos, or similar tools (preferred)
Excellent communication, organization, and cross-functional collaboration skills in fast-paced environments
The pay range is indicative of projected hiring range, however base pay will be determined based on a candidate’s work location, skills and experience. Mattel offers competitive total pay programs, comprehensive benefits, and resources to help empower a culture where every employee can reach their full potential.


Don’t meet every single requirement? At Mattel, we are dedicated to an inclusive workplace and a culture of belonging. If you’re excited about this role but your past experience doesn’t align perfectly with every qualification in the job description, we still encourage you to apply. You may be just the right candidate for this or other roles. Join Mattel's Talent Community and start receiving exciting updates about our company, our employees, and our culture. Also, interested in signing up for job alerts? Do so here!

How We Work

We are a purpose driven company aiming to empower generations to explore the wonder of childhood and reach their full potential. We live up to our purpose employing the following behaviors


We collaborate Being a part of Mattel means being part of one team with shared values and common goals. Every person counts and working closely together always brings better results. Partnership is our process and our collective capabilities is our superpower.
We innovate At Mattel we always aim to find new and better ways to create innovative products and experiences. No matter where you work in the organization, you can always make a difference and have real impact. We welcome new ideas and value new initiatives that challenge conventional thinking.
We execute We are a performance-driven company. We strive for excellence and are focused on pursuing best-in-class outcomes. We believe in accountability and ownership and know that our people are at their best when they are empowered to create and deliver results.


Who We Are

Mattel is a leading global toy and family entertainment company and owner of one of the most iconic brand portfolios in the world. We engage consumers and fans through our franchise brands, including Barbie, Hot Wheels, Fisher-Price, American Girl, Thomas & Friends, UNO, Masters of the Universe, Matchbox, Monster High, MEGA and Polly Pocket, as well as other popular properties that we own or license in partnership with global entertainment companies. Our offerings include toys, content, consumer products, digital and live experiences. Our products are sold in collaboration with the world’s leading retail and ecommerce companies. Since its founding in 1945, Mattel is proud to be a trusted partner in empowering generations to explore the wonder of childhood and reach their full potential.

Mattel’s award-winning workplace culture has been recognized by Forbes, Fast Company, Newsweek, Great Place to Work, TIME, and more.

Visit us at https //jobs.mattel.com/ and www.instagram.com/MattelCareers .

Mattel is an Equal Opportunity Employer where we want you to bring your authentic self to work every day. We welcome all job seekers, and all applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, ethnicity, color, national origin, religion, sex, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, veteran and protected veteran status, disability status, and or any other basis protected by applicable federal, state or local law.

Pursuant to the Los Angeles Fair Chance Ordinance and the California Fair Chance Act, qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment.
