Title: Senior Operations Data Analyst (Hybrid)
Description:
Your opportunity

By joining the Kardium team, you can help make a difference in the lives of millions around the world. We have developed a ground-breaking medical device for the diagnosis and treatment of the most common heart rhythm disorder, atrial fibrillation – which can cause stroke, heart failure, and other heart-related complications.

Kardium’s mission is to deliver the best treatment for atrial fibrillation. To achieve this, we have developed The Globe® Pulsed Field System – we have a fantastic technical team that has developed a product with strong, unique, and customer-valued differentiators versus other solutions on the market. We have built connections with key opinion leaders who are already using the Globe System and are helping to tell our story.

We have obtained FDA approval and officially begun the commercial launch of the Globe® Pulsed Field System. This is a pivotal and exciting time in our journey, as we bring this life-changing technology to patients!

We are seeking an Operational Analyst who you will be responsible for delivering high-impact dashboards, metrics and reporting across various operational teams. You will play a critical role in enabling real-time operational visibility and data-informed decision-making, supporting both daily execution and strategic planning initiatives. The analyst will work cross-functionally with operations, purchasing, and quality teams, with a strong focus on data quality and continuity during our transition to Microsoft Dynamics 365 ERP system.

Please note, this opportunity is a hybrid work arrangement, located in Burnaby, BC.

Meaningful work you will be a part of

Your Responsibilities Will Include


Develop and maintain critical operational metrics and key process indicators (KPIs) to support manufacturing, planning, and quality functions
Create and manage visual dashboards to provide real time insight into operational performance using tools such as Power BI and Excel
Provide analytical support for continuous improvement initiatives and projects to identify and prioritize optimization opportunities for cost reduction and performance improvements.
Query and extract relevant data from ERP and other data sources using SQL or other relevant tools.
Partner with cross-functional teams to identify data requirements and alignment with operational goals
Collaborate with stakeholders to develop scalable reporting frameworks and templates to support future growth.
Advise on data architecture and structure as systems evolve and new systems introduced, ensuring the right data is collected and accessible.
Lead initiatives to improve data quality and governance, identifying gaps and implementing data validation processes.
Train and support end-users in interpreting dashboard data and utilizing insights for decision-making.
Ensure data security and compliance with relevant regulations and company policies.


What You Bring To The Team

Our Kardium team is smart, creative, and passionate about developing cutting-edge medical devices to help improve people’s lives. We work in a collaborative environment based on trust and respect. We understand that only by working together can we solve what was thought unsolvable.

You will be successful in this role because you possess these attributes:


Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Business Analytics, Information Systems, or a related field.
Minimum 5 years experience in data analytics or reporting in a manufacturing or supply chain environment.
Advanced skills in Power BI, SQL and Excel.
Highly analytic and data driven with the ability to organize data and derive conclusions
Demonstrated experience building and maintaining dashboards and reporting systems used by cross-functional teams.
Strong understanding of operational process (manufacturing, supply chain) as well as key operations metrics (e.g., OEE, throughput, inventory turns, supplier performance).
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.
Experience working with ERP systems (Microsoft D365 or equivalent is a strong asset).
Knowledge and experience working in the regulated manufacturing industry is an asset.


Compensation

Kardium has listed the total cash compensation range (base salary + 5% RRSP contribution) that we expect to pay applicants for this role, as of the time of this posting. Pay offered will be determined based on numerous relevant business and candidate factors including, for example, education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, and business or organizational needs. For candidates with more or less experience than listed above, the pay range will be adjusted.

As a permanent employee, you will also participate in Kardium’s stock option plan.


$90,000 – 125,000 (CAD Annually)


Your Benefits & Well-being

The total cash we’ve listed for this position includes a base salary, plus an annual contribution to a Retirement Savings Plan account to help support your financial goals.

Comprehensive medical & dental coverage for all permanent employees – effective as of Day 1, with no waiting period.

Work-day flexibility – we offer a flexible work environment for daily working hours. In addition, we provide 3 personal days per year.

Support for you (and your dependents) overall well-being.

Family building – we provide top up for maternity leave, adoptive leave, and parental leave for non-birthing parents. Employees can also enroll in benefit coverage for fertility drug treatment.

Career progression and learning support.

Professional membership support.

Life at Kardium

What makes us a great place to work?


Our Purpose
Our People
Our Culture


At Kardium, we embrace diversity of background, experience, and perspective and we’re committed to inclusion and equity at every level. We encourage applications from all qualified candidates who represent the full diversity of all communities.

We collaborate with Employee Resource Groups (ERGs), as volunteer-led groups who share a common dimension of diversity and come together to provide support, education, and other opportunities across Kardium. HTML:
Your opportunity

By joining the Kardium team, you can help make a difference in the lives of millions around the world. We have developed a ground-breaking medical device for the diagnosis and treatment of the most common heart rhythm disorder, atrial fibrillation – which can cause stroke, heart failure, and other heart-related complications.

Kardium’s mission is to deliver the best treatment for atrial fibrillation. To achieve this, we have developed The Globe® Pulsed Field System – we have a fantastic technical team that has developed a product with strong, unique, and customer-valued differentiators versus other solutions on the market. We have built connections with key opinion leaders who are already using the Globe System and are helping to tell our story.

We have obtained FDA approval and officially begun the commercial launch of the Globe® Pulsed Field System. This is a pivotal and exciting time in our journey, as we bring this life-changing technology to patients!

We are seeking an Operational Analyst who you will be responsible for delivering high-impact dashboards, metrics and reporting across various operational teams. You will play a critical role in enabling real-time operational visibility and data-informed decision-making, supporting both daily execution and strategic planning initiatives. The analyst will work cross-functionally with operations, purchasing, and quality teams, with a strong focus on data quality and continuity during our transition to Microsoft Dynamics 365 ERP system.

Please note, this opportunity is a hybrid work arrangement, located in Burnaby, BC.

Meaningful work you will be a part of

Your Responsibilities Will Include

  • Develop and maintain critical operational metrics and key process indicators (KPIs) to support manufacturing, planning, and quality functions
  • Create and manage visual dashboards to provide real time insight into operational performance using tools such as Power BI and Excel
  • Provide analytical support for continuous improvement initiatives and projects to identify and prioritize optimization opportunities for cost reduction and performance improvements.
  • Query and extract relevant data from ERP and other data sources using SQL or other relevant tools.
  • Partner with cross-functional teams to identify data requirements and alignment with operational goals
  • Collaborate with stakeholders to develop scalable reporting frameworks and templates to support future growth.
  • Advise on data architecture and structure as systems evolve and new systems introduced, ensuring the right data is collected and accessible.
  • Lead initiatives to improve data quality and governance, identifying gaps and implementing data validation processes.
  • Train and support end-users in interpreting dashboard data and utilizing insights for decision-making.
  • Ensure data security and compliance with relevant regulations and company policies.

What You Bring To The Team

Our Kardium team is smart, creative, and passionate about developing cutting-edge medical devices to help improve people’s lives. We work in a collaborative environment based on trust and respect. We understand that only by working together can we solve what was thought unsolvable.

You will be successful in this role because you possess these attributes:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Business Analytics, Information Systems, or a related field.
  • Minimum 5 years experience in data analytics or reporting in a manufacturing or supply chain environment.
  • Advanced skills in Power BI, SQL and Excel.
  • Highly analytic and data driven with the ability to organize data and derive conclusions
  • Demonstrated experience building and maintaining dashboards and reporting systems used by cross-functional teams.
  • Strong understanding of operational process (manufacturing, supply chain) as well as key operations metrics (e.g., OEE, throughput, inventory turns, supplier performance).
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.
  • Experience working with ERP systems (Microsoft D365 or equivalent is a strong asset).
  • Knowledge and experience working in the regulated manufacturing industry is an asset.

Compensation

Kardium has listed the total cash compensation range (base salary + 5% RRSP contribution) that we expect to pay applicants for this role, as of the time of this posting. Pay offered will be determined based on numerous relevant business and candidate factors including, for example, education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, and business or organizational needs. For candidates with more or less experience than listed above, the pay range will be adjusted.

As a permanent employee, you will also participate in Kardium’s stock option plan.

  • $90,000 – 125,000 (CAD Annually)

Your Benefits & Well-being

The total cash we’ve listed for this position includes a base salary, plus an annual contribution to a Retirement Savings Plan account to help support your financial goals.

Comprehensive medical & dental coverage for all permanent employees – effective as of Day 1, with no waiting period.

Work-day flexibility – we offer a flexible work environment for daily working hours. In addition, we provide 3 personal days per year.

Support for you (and your dependents) overall well-being.

Family building – we provide top up for maternity leave, adoptive leave, and parental leave for non-birthing parents. Employees can also enroll in benefit coverage for fertility drug treatment.

Career progression and learning support.

Professional membership support.

Life at Kardium

What makes us a great place to work?

  • Our Purpose
  • Our People
  • Our Culture

At Kardium, we embrace diversity of background, experience, and perspective and we’re committed to inclusion and equity at every level. We encourage applications from all qualified candidates who represent the full diversity of all communities.

We collaborate with Employee Resource Groups (ERGs), as volunteer-led groups who share a common dimension of diversity and come together to provide support, education, and other opportunities across Kardium. Examples of our ERGs include Women ERG, Pride ERG, Race Ethnicity & Cultural Heritage (REACH) ERG, Truth & Reconciliation ERG, Persian ERG, Chinese ERG, Barangay Kardium (Filipino) ERG, Irish ERG, Brazilian ERG, and Early Career at Kardium ERG.

Title: Senior Business Analytics & Insights
Description:
Marketing Statement

As the largest operating company in the integrated TransLink enterprise, Coast Mountain Bus Company (CMBC) operates a fleet of clean-fuel conventional and community shuttle buses, zero-emission trolley buses, and passenger ferry SeaBuses in Metro Vancouver, the largest single transit service area in Canada.

At CMBC, one of BC’s Top Employers, we're committed to providing an innovative, healthy, and engaging workforce. This is reflected in our workforce of over 5,500 employees performing over 400 unique jobs, who are committed and empowered to deliver service that attracts nearly 1.1 million passengers daily and connects people, businesses, and communities in the Metro Vancouver region.

We have the important job of helping our passengers get to work, appointments, visiting with family and friends, and back again every day. Metro Vancouver relies on us, and we take that as a point of pride.

Responsibilities

PRIMARY PURPOSE

Provides technical expertise to the development of a data governance framework for CMBC, including leading stakeholder engagement, developing, and monitoring progress of long-term roadmap, establishing requisite business processes, and ensuring the Data Warehouse, Business Intelligence and Analytic (DW/BI&A) solutions align with the established framework. Provides technical leadership and supervision on the design, development, and quality assurance of monthly dashboard reports to the Executive Management Team (EMT), to ensure timely and accurate delivery. Supports the Manager, Business Analytics & Insights with leading the annual Corporate Business Planning and Reporting process through stakeholder engagement with leaders from across the organization, ensuring alignment to Corporate and Enterprise-level strategic plans.

Key Accountabilities

Provides technical leadership on planning and implementation of DW/BI&A solutions. Designs, implements, and maintains customized data warehouse, business intelligence and analytics (DW/BI&A) technical solutions. Ensures DW/BI&A solutions function effectively and align with standards, policies and supports ongoing Data Governance Program within CMBC. Ensures solutions adhere to best practices and Enterprise standards.

Designs, implements, and maintains the Corporate Business Plan BI reporting solution, data collection process and KPI integration to ensure the alignment of planned annual works to strategic long-range workplans, Enterprise goals, as well as ties together corporate-level KPIs to the annual initiatives to ensure alignment to planned works and performance measures assessed.

Provides technical expertise in the development and sustainment of a data governance framework for CMBC, including standardized documentation. Leads stakeholder engagement and ensures the DW/BI&A solutions developed align with the governance framework established. Leads discussions with those identified as part of the governance framework within the organization and looks for alignment opportunities from across the Enterprise.

Stays abreast of new techniques, technologies, and industry trends, and participates in relevant industry events related to Data Governance. Leads the team towards modern practices and techniques; establishes and participates in design reviews, regular or ad-hoc data quality reviews ensuring data quality throughout the data pipeline.

Manages and develops projects of moderate to high impact, risk, and complexity. Designs and develops project scope, schedules, and work plans; defining and prioritizing deliverables; ensuring compliance with Enterprise standards, policies and procedures. Alerts management to critical issues and risks. Identifies and resolves project issues, including scheduling overruns, conflicting agendas, and technical issues.

Provides technical support to the maintenance of custom Extract Transform Load (ETL) solution, as well as continual assessment of metadata reporting to ensure data integrity and quality assurance.

Consults and collaborates with business and technology stakeholders on data management and data architecture strategies, governance, practices, and policies.

Supports the Executive Management Team by providing KPIs to report on monthly performance; leads requirements gathering, target setting, report design and delivery and documentation. Carries out analysis related to variances in corporate performance measures, recommending best practices to the Senior Management team for problem areas.

Prepares quarterly reports for the CMBC Board of Directors and TransLink Statutory Reporting requirements.

Provides support to the Manager in the development and maintenance of the Team’s Strategic Plan Roadmap, planning and decisions making. Provides input on the dependency and sequencing of projects as well as highlights any potential project or compliance risks to ensure project priorities align to annual objectives.

Participates in the development and implementation of a continuous improvement strategy by offering Business Process advisory services to other departments. Supports the delivery of better value and greater efficiency through the identification and elimination of unnecessary complexity within business processes and identification of better ways of working, applying Lean methodology.

Acts as a key liaison with International Benchmarking Groups to conduct detailed surveys and analysis. Determines international best practices related to specific Key Performance Indicators and completes analysis of moderate to high impact, recommending business changes with financial/budgetary impacts. Completes follow-up analysis to confirm impacts and ROI. Presents findings to Executive Team, and at conferences as requested by Executive leadership.

Develops business cases to support KPIs with strategic objectives and leverages available benchmarking resources for best practices; provides comparable performance measures from other transit systems and business enterprises to stakeholders towards informed decision making. Forecasts the implications of various alternatives to maximize the effectiveness of KPIs for organizational success.

Represents the Business Analytics & Insights Team on inter-departmental committees and as a technical input for larger scale capital projects as requested.

Provides technical support and guidance to other Analysts and supports Managers within the organization by connecting them to relevant data sources for their reporting needs, including developing custom SQL views of required data.

Qualifications

EDUCATION & EXPERIENCE:

The requirements for this job are acquired through completion of a university degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Data Analytics, or other relevant business-related fields plus seven (7) years of progressive experience in performance reporting functions in a multifaceted organization.

Other Requirements

Advanced knowledge of concepts, principles, practices and techniques pertaining to all aspects of performance measures, benchmarking, balanced scorecards and dashboards

Excellent knowledge of Microsoft Data Warehouse and BI technologies, both Cloud and on-prem (SQL Server, SSIS, SSAS, SSRS, Power BI Desktop, PowerBI Report Builder, PowerBI Service, Tableau etc.)

Strong knowledge of Microsoft PowerPlatform solutions including PowerApps, PowerAutomate and Power Platform Admin Centre.

Strong knowledge and understanding of the relevant concepts of data mining and data warehousing

Demonstrated ability to communicate the complexities of technical programs (e.g. Kimball methodology, modern data warehousing).

Advanced experience of data modeling and business process design software, such as Visio.

Advanced knowledge of coaching and mentoring teams on Data Warehouse & Business Intelligence Architect concepts and methodology.

Advanced analytical and problem-solving skills to handle conflict resolution, facilitating discussion and alternatives of different approaches.

Solid leadership skills with ability to manage and influence direct or indirect reporting staff, and to provide specialized guidance and expertise to all levels of the organization.

Excellent planning, organization, and time management skills with strong ability to organize competing priorities.

Experience maintaining ETL process using software such as SSIS

Prior experience in design, deployment, or sustainment of Data Governance Program

Advanced knowledge of concepts, principles, practices, and techniques pertaining to all aspects of performance measures, benchmarking, balanced scorecards, and dashboards

Advanced interpersonal and communication skills to provide specialized guidance and expertise to managers, staff and customers; and to develop and manage critical relationships at all levels in the enterprise.

Experience in leading and facilitating Lean/process improvements event is an asset.

Other Information

Recruitment Process: An applicant will be required to demonstrate their suitability for this position by meeting the minimum level of qualifications and experience in order to be invited into the selection process. A standard interview format will be used including general, scenario and behavioural descriptive interview questions.

Work Schedule

37.5 hours per week.

Work Designation

Hybrid

This position offers the flexibility of working both on-site and remotely within B.C.

Rate of Pay

The salary for this position ranges from $84,480 - $126,720 per annum. Actual salary offered will be based on education, experience, skills, and qualifications as they relate to the role.

We aim to provide competitive pay that reflects your professional background and expertise. We want to ensure our offer will align with your strengths considering experience, skills, and internal equity to offer a fair and equitable salary. The Total Compensation Package, which includes Extended Health, Dental, Transit Pass and enrollment in the Public Service Pension Plan as well as other employment offerings will be discussed in detail as your application progresses.

How To Apply

Please click the 'Apply' button at the top right corner or go to https://www.translink.ca/About-Us/Careers.aspx to apply for this position and view instructions on the process.

INSTRUCTIONS: Please save your (1) cover letter, and your (2) resume as one pdf document prior to uploading your application on-line.

Posting Date: May 27, 2026

Closing Date: Open until filled

Please note that only those short listed will be contacted.

Having trouble applying? Please view the System Requirements & FAQ's by going to http://www.translink.ca/careers.

We welcome and encourage Indigenous applicants, people of colour, all genders, 2SLGBTQ+ and persons with disabilities to apply. Learn more about TransLink's commitment to equity, diversity and inclusion.

Accommodations are available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process. For a confidential inquiry, simply email us at jobs@translink.ca

Title: Data Quality Assurance Analyst - FTT
Description:
Marketing Statement

A career at TransLink and our family of companies means working with people with a wide range of skills and perspectives, all teaming up towards a common goal: preserving and enhancing the region's world-envied quality of life. Together, we connect the region and enhance its livability by providing a sustainable transit and transportation network, embraced by our communities and people.

At TransLink we are dedicated to building a workforce that reflects the diversity of the communities in which we live. We’re committed to fostering an inclusive, equitable and accessible workplace, recognizing the unique value and skills every individual brings.

Looking for a great place to work where your contributions are valued and you can make a difference in a vibrant city? At TransLink, one of BC’s Top Employers, you'll help make Metro Vancouver a better place to live, built on transportation excellence. Put your future in motion!

Summary

Responsibilities

The Data Quality Assurance (QA) Analyst is responsible for ensuring the accuracy, consistency, and reliability of TransLink’s enterprise data assets and analytical systems. This role focuses heavily on data validation, ETL testing, and database quality assurance, supporting the delivery of trusted, high-quality data for business intelligence, analytics, and operational decision-making.

The Analyst creates and executes data-centric testing strategies, develops automated data validation frameworks, and partners closely with Data Architects, Solution Architects, Data Analysts, Data Warehouse Analysts, Functional Analysts, Business Intelligence Analysts, and other roles to ensure that data pipelines, integrations, and applications perform accurately and consistently across systems.

Education

Qualifications

Diploma in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Computer Systems Engineering, or Mathematics from a recognized post-secondary institution, including recent coursework in database management, data warehousing, cloud computing, and software testing is preferred.

Experience

Minimum three (3) years of experience in data testing, data validation, or ETL/warehouse QA within large-scale or cloud data environments including proven experience in testing data pipelines, ETL processes, and cloud-based data integrations, advanced proficiency in SQL and Microsoft SQL Server.

Experience validating Azure Data Factory or Azure Synapse Analytics workflows, background in test automation and scripting using Python or equivalent languages and familiarity with data observability, data profiling, and data governance frameworks preferred.

Up to one (1) year of on-the-job experience may be required to gain familiarity with TransLink’s specific systems and practices.

Other Information

Recruitment Process: An applicant will be required to demonstrate their suitability for this position by meeting the minimum level of qualifications and experience in order to be invited into the selection process. A standard interview format will be used including general, scenario and behavioural descriptive interview questions.

Work Schedule

37.5 hours per week.

This is a full-time temporary position for 12 months.

Work Designation

Hybrid

This position offers the flexibility of working both on-site and remotely within B.C.

Rate of Pay

Salary $8,236 - $9,896 per month (Actual salary offered will be commensurate with education, experience and internal parity).

The Total Compensation Package includes Extended Health, Transit Pass and enrollment in the Public Service Pension Plan upon eligibility. Focus on your development through training, and mentorship programs. Enjoy a variety of health and wellness programs, including access to gym facilities. Speak to us to know more about what we offer.

How To Apply

Please click the 'Apply' button at the top right corner of the page or go to http://www.translink.ca/careers to apply for this position and view instructions on the process.

Description:
ob Location: Burnaby, British Columbia, Canada V3N 4X8

Employment type: Permanent

Hours of work: Full-time (37.5 hrs/wk)

Annual Salary: $ $ 70,800.00 - 76,300.00

No. of positions: 1

Closing date: June 24, 2026




About the job:




What you’ll do




• Performs the duties of the Asset Information System Analyst 1 job : (a) Prepares training materials and delivers training courses to end-users; (b) Delivers training and mentors team members; (c) Analyzes business requirements, prepares reports, analyzes the results, and makes recommendations for improvements; (d) Deals directly with first and third tier service providers such as, Fujitsu to ensure end-user problems have been resolved in a satisfactory and timely manner; (e) Prepares and delivers end-user communication as required by business owners and technical owners; (f) Directs and performs complex testing for application fault analysis, analyzes the results, and makes recommendations for changes; (g) Implements complex system administration activities under the general supervision of the System Administrator using a high security clearance; (h) Manages departmental records and documentation; (i) Manages complex business reporting requirements using advanced reporting applications; (j) Co-ordinates the publishing of information to the intranet / SharePoint/databases; and (k) Ensures applications, data and processes meet compliance requirements (i.e. NERC and CIP, PCBs, etc.).

• Technical owner for Stations/Lines work management data (project costs, estimate costs, hours, etc.) and asset management data (equipment records, equipment failure records, etc.). Researches sources of data, analyzes the plausibility and quality of the data and where there are errors or anomalies, investigates root cause of problem, change sources and advises IT of correct data field to be used. Ensures reports are extracting right data and report information is correct and valid. Develops work processes and business rules to enable service providers and internal users to correctly apply asset management support systems. Prepares and maintains various training materials and helpdesk manuals. 3. Coordinates maintenance policies and standards within the asset information system by: (a) Implementing and revising maintenance programs/instructions within the system for all Stations/Lines equipment; and (b) Coordinating policy and standard structure.

• Ensures applications adhere to business process requirements, meets compliance, functional and technical requirements by: a) Pro-actively identifying and resolving business issues and, where required, providing recommendations for resolution to management; b) Dealing directly with various business offices and staff to gather and review recommendations and implement appropriate solutions to ensure the success of business systems.

• Is considered a Subject Matter Expert (SME) when working with a team to develop and implement new or revised business information system applications and as such: (a) May act as a "task manager" or participate on a project team to coordinate and facilitate changes and enhancements to existing or new applications; (b) Ensures second tier application support personnel deliver solutions that meet agreed-to business requirements; and (c) Represents the interests of Stations/Lines to ensure business needs are met.

• Assists in development and implementation of pilot and new computer systems for a variety of Asset Management Services applications by liaising with other departments and outside consultants, controlling project scheduling, analyzing and controlling project costs, resolving conflicts and approving work estimates within assigned limits. Tasks include researching and documenting user requirements; devising alternate solutions and recommend best approach to address issue; providing and/or collecting estimate for proposed solutions; provide assistance in business case preparation; coordinating with system developers and users; testing new system before actual implementation; assisting in system roll- out.

• Provides technical advice and guidance to incumbents in the Asset Information System Analyst 1 job, other specialists temporarily assigned to projects, and to lower-level support staff.

• Performs duties of a minor nature related to the above duties that do not affect the rating of the job.




What you bring




• Diploma of Technology from an Institute of Technology in Computer Systems and: either four (4) years of experience as an Asset Information System Analyst 1 or four (4) years of experience analyzing large and complex datasets. OR

• Six (6) years of equivalent work experience includes experience working with asset information and work management systems, budgeting, processes and reporting systems including providing front line support, user training and participation in system development projects. Of the six (6) years of experience, three (3) years of experience must include working with work management systems.




What we offer




• A comprehensive benefits package - A minimum of 15 paid vacation days - A lifetime pension - Flexible work model, depending on your role type - Training and development courses For more information on the benefits we offer, visit bchydro.com/benefits.




For more information on the benefits we offer, visit bchydro.com/benefits.




What else you should know




• This position is affiliated with the Movement of United Professionals union (MoveUP/COPE). http://moveuptogether.ca

• In-office presence required a minimum of 2 days per week

• This role is based out of our Edmonds location; however, other locations may be considered, subject to seating availability

• Experience with SAP FIORI and SAP GUI is preferred

• Experience with Asset Hierarchy and Master Data maintenance in SAP EAM is an asset

• Experience with Jira Service Management is an asset

• Experience with document management is an asset

• Strong attention to detail is required

• Must be able to navigate unfamiliar systems and be comfortable with evolving processes

• The Asset Information Support team is a fast-paced, high-volume environment requiring strong organizational, project management, and communication skills to effectively manage workload and changing priorities

• A test will be administered as part of the interview and assessment process. Burnaby, British Columbia, Canada V3N 4X8 Before you apply, please confirm you meet BC Hydro’s time in role requirement. M&P employees must meet the time in role requirements specified in the updated M&P TIR policy. For MoveUP and IBEW employees, the current time in role as outlined in the Collective Agreements will apply. Don't forget to update your Candidate Profile with your current resume and copies of your certifications. If applicable, include your Trades Qualification. This will ensure we have all the necessary information to assess your application without any delays. Date Posted: 2026-06-10 Closing Date: 2026-06-24 52444010 For internal use




How to apply




Interested candidates should submit their applications online at https://app.bchydro.com/careers/current_opp.html or click 'Apply'




You must use a supported browser, such as Firefox, Internet Explorer, Google Chrome or Safari. Your pop up blocker will also need to be disabled for the BC Hydro Careers site.




On the BC Hydro Careers site, click on the Apply button in order to complete the steps to apply for this job. Please be sure to update your Candidate Profile with your current resume and include copies of your certifications, if applicable.

Title: Business Planning Analyst
Description:
We’re looking for an experienced Business Planning Analyst for our Prevention Divisional Strategy and Operations department.

In this role, you’ll provide support for a variety of duties related to strategic and operational activities, planning, analysis, performance, reporting, regulation implementation, and quality management. These duties will be provided to Prevention Services through an assignment of work, primarily to support strategic and operational projects and programs.

How you’ll make a difference: You’ll help ensure B.C. workers go home safely at the end of their workday.

Where you’ll work

At WorkSafeBC, we offer a hybrid work model that combines working remotely, and in our offices based on the operational needs of the position.


Provide support and assistance in developing the departmental plan, divisional roadmap and contributing to enterprise business plan
Help to define measures and prepare and maintain divisional and departmental reports
Apply business planning principles and practices, including result-based management, to track, monitor and report on measures
Support management through project management, facilitation of meetings and applying business improvement change management principles to business planning and projects
Gather and analyze information and data to understand problems and/or opportunities, and develop creative and pragmatic solutions for various levels of management including senior leadership
Write concise, clear, and grammatically correct business documents to inform or persuade a variety of audiences
Consistently models the appropriate level of organizational behaviours expected of all WorkSafeBC employees: responsive, respectful, fair, collaborative, accountable, and forward thinking.


We’re Looking For People Who Can


Independently plan, prioritize, and coordinate a diverse set of activities to consistently meet targets in a changing environment
Lead and monitor multiple projects and project teams to completion
Prepare, deliver, and defend presentations to a variety of clients
Write concise business documents to inform or persuade a variety of audiences
Facilitate meetings with clients and co-workers


Your Background And Experience


A master’s degree in a business-related discipline, such as business administration, accounting, economics, policy, law or finance and a minimum of four years of related work experience in business and/or financial analysis
Or, a bachelor’s degree in a business-related discipline, such as business administration, accounting, economics, policy, law or finance, and a minimum of four years of related work experience in business or strategic planning, business process improvement, project management and gap analysis and/or data analysis reporting
Project management and a business process analysis background is strongly preferred
Experience in public sector is an asset


We’ll consider equivalent combinations of education and experience.

Important to know

Before we can finalize any offer of employment, you must:


Confirm you’re legally entitled to work in Canada


WorkSafeBC’s COVID-19 Employee Mandatory Vaccine Policy (the “Policy”) is suspended effective January 9, 2023, however we reserve the right to re-implement it in response to changes in the public health landscape, including public health orders. We are committed to the protection, health, and safety of our employees and our Communicable Disease Prevention Program and related protocols remain in effect.

Who We Are

At WorkSafeBC, we promote safe and healthy workplaces across British Columbia. We partner with workers and employers to save lives and prevent injury, disease, and disability. When work-related injuries or diseases occur, we provide compensation and support injured workers in their recovery, rehabilitation, and safe return to work. We’re honoured to serve the 2.49 million workers and 263,000 registered employers in our province.

What’s it like to work at WorkSafeBC?

It’s challenging, stimulating, and rewarding. Our positions offer diversity and opportunities for professional growth. Every day, the work we do impacts people and changes lives. What we do is important, and so are the people we do it for.

Our ability to make a difference relies on building a team with a rich variety of skills, knowledge, backgrounds, abilities, and experiences that reflects the diversity of the people we serve. We are committed to fostering a welcoming, inclusive, and supportive work culture where everyone can contribute as their best, authentic self.

Learn more: Discover who we are.

Our Benefits

As a member of our team, you’ll have access to services and benefits that help you get the most out of work — and life. Along with a competitive salary, your total compensation package includes:


Defined benefit pension plan that provides you with a lifetime monthly pension when you retire
3 weeks of vacation in your first year, with regular increases based on years of service
Extensive health care and dental benefits
Optional leave and earned-time-off arrangements
Development opportunities (tuition reimbursement, leadership development, and more)


Salary: $47.61 – $60.00/ hourly or $89,747.67 - $113,100.75/ yearly

Learn more: Find out what we offer.

Want to apply?


Applications are welcomed immediately, however must be received no later than 4:30 p.m. PST on the closing date.
Please note that we will be starting assessments prior to the closing date.


We encourage all qualified applicants to apply. If you require an accommodation in the assessment process, please email Recruitment Testing Accommodation (SM) when you submit your application.

Title: Business Analyst - Regional Home & Community Care/Primary Health Care/Regional Care Integration
Description:
Salary

The salary range for this position is CAD $38.88 - $55.89 / hour

Job Summary

Join Fraser Health - Transform Home & Community Care

Fraser Health is building the next generation of Home & Community Care integrated, data-driven, and digitally enabled. We are seeking a Business Analyst - Regional Home & Community Care/Primary Health Care/Regional Care Integration to play a pivotal role in advancing clinical systems, workflow optimization, and automation across the region.

Why This Role Matters

You Will Help


Reduce administrative burden for clinical teams
Enable automation and intelligent workflows (RPA, AI, digital tools)
Improve access, flow, and patient outcomes in community care
Standardize processes across a complex, multi-program environment


What You’ll Do


Act as the bridge between clinical operations, data and technology, ensuring systems align with clinical and business needs
Lead process mapping, requirements gathering, and workflow redesign to identify improvement and automation opportunities
Support and scale automation initiatives (RPA)
Collaborate with clinical, operational, data and IT partners
Drive continuous improvement by standardizing processes and workflows
Lead development testing (UAT), implementation, and adoption of new system and automation solutions


Who You Are

You’re a strategic thinker with strong analytical skills, a passion for healthcare improvement, and the ability to communicate complex insights clearly. You enjoy working collaboratively and are driven to make a difference through data.

Join us and help turn insight int

Detailed Overview

Supporting the Vision, Values, Purpose and Commitments of Fraser Health including service delivery that is centered around patients/clients/residents and families:

Provides leadership and support by applying expert knowledge in health economics and skills in data extraction, data manipulation and analysis to advise on evidence-based clinical practice and operational decision making; plans and develops reporting requirements by reviewing clinical, financial, human resource and operational data to develop strategic business cases; prepares supporting documents, briefing papers, strategic and tactical reports for an assigned area and/or portfolio for submission to funding organizations, Fraser Health Executive and other stakeholders; works collaboratively with clinical teams and other internal stakeholders in the support of the mission, philosophies and goals of Fraser Health.

Responsibilities


Coordinates work activities related to data collection and the production of key performance metrics reports and evaluation measures related to the assigned program/initiatives and other assigned projects.
Verifies the accuracy, consistency and integrity of data and information by consulting with project and work team leads to confirm and validate the data and contents of reports and documents.
Provides detailed multi-source data analysis, interpretation and advice by compiling and reviewing financial, human resource, clinical and operational data.
Documents reports on data quality and provides recommendations to resolve data quality and collection issues; provides technical advice to staff regarding data, statistical analysis and related data support to improve and maintain consistent data quality and collection.
Collaborates with staff and internal contacts to identify reporting needs, analyses current processes and identifies opportunities to reduce redundancies and ensure accurate and consistent reporting processes to support key business objectives and project initiatives.
Provides work direction and expert advice on methods to address business needs through the improved use of automated tools; facilitates discussion on future requirements in consultation with users and prepares business cases to document and assist in refining existing work processes.
Prepares strategic documents for program service planning and delivery including briefing papers, confidential business cases and operational/clinical reviews for area/department leadership.
Provides expertise during different phases of information system implementation to ensure reporting requirements meet established guidelines and standards; responds to data requests as required.
Develops annual performance plans including goals and objectives for assigned area of responsibility in collaboration with the Director/Manager or appropriate designate.
Participates on internal/external committee meetings as requested.


Qualifications

Education and Experience

Bachelor’s degree in Health Economics, Statistics, Computer Science or related discipline, supplemented with five (5) to seven (7) years’ recent related experience in decision support, data analysis and/or health care management, or an equivalent combination of education, training and experience.

Competencies

Demonstrates the leadership practices of the Fraser Health Leadership Framework of Clear, Caring and Courageous and creates the conditions for people to succeed.

Professional/Technical Capabilities


Demonstrated knowledge and understanding of the health care system.
Demonstrated ability to analyze clinical, administrative and financial data.
Demonstrated understanding of relational and multi-dimensional database concepts.
Knowledge of other health care disciplines and their role within healthcare.
Working knowledge of health care economics and statistics.
Ability to operate related equipment including applicable software applications.
Physical ability to perform the duties of the position.


About Fraser Health

Fraser Health is the heart of health care for over two million people in Metro Vancouver and the Fraser Valley in British Columbia, Canada, on the traditional, ancestral and unceded lands of the Coast Salish and Nlaka’pamux Nations and is home to 32 First Nations within the Fraser Salish region.

People - those we care for and those who care for them - are at the heart of everything we do. Our hospital and community-based services are delivered by a team of 50,000+ staff, medical staff and volunteers.

We are committed to planetary health and value diversity in the work force. We strive to maintain an environment of respect, caring and trust. Fraser Health’s hiring practices aspire to ensure all individuals are treated in an inclusive, equitable and culturally safe manner.

Together, we are the heart of health care.

Instagram | Facebook | LinkedIn | X | Indeed | Glassdoor

IMPORTANT: Recruitment scam warning

Please be on alert for recruitment scams. We are aware of several scams targeting individuals by falsely claiming to represent Fraser Health. Always take precautions when sharing personal details with unverified sources. Please note that we will never ask for money or payment, nor ask you to download or install an app at any point during our recruitment process.

We are committed to planetary health and value diversity in the work force. We strive to maintain an environment of respect, caring and trust. Fraser Health’s hiring practices aspire to ensure all individuals are treated in an inclusive, equitable and culturally safe manner.

Together, we are the heart of health care.

IMPORTANT: Recruitment scam warning

Please be on alert for recruitment scams. We are aware of several scams targeting individuals by falsely claiming to represent Fraser Health. Always take precautions when sharing personal details with unverified sources. Please note that we will never ask for money or payment, nor ask you to download or install an app at any point during our recruitment process.

Title: Analyst, Employee Benefits
Description:
Who We Are

We are BC’s largest Insurance Broker and looking for individuals to grow with us! A career at HUB means your career opportunities are endless. From auto, personal or commercial lines, claims, employee benefits, administration, to becoming a full-time producer or account manager, we have a role for you. Our culture is renowned within the industry, and we value our people. Helping our clients protect what matters most is crucial to who we are, and we are on the lookout for those who share the same passion.

What We’re Looking For

The Analyst works closely with the team to provide financial analysis of carrier and client statements, assist in the day-to-day data administration as well as supporting and providing high-level analysis and detailed commentary over their work.

Our Burnaby Still Creek head office location is centrally located in the Willingdon Business Park near The Amazing Brentwood. This location is easily accessible by the Gilmore sky train station with many shops, restaurants and other amenities nearby.

Responsibilities


Provide accurate and timely data for client renewal reports obtained from internal and external sources
Conduct renewal analysis, negotiate fees and rates with carriers – in alignment with department strategy
As required, review client claim history data from providers – working alongside the team to ensure all required reports are collected
Review and verify financial statements for accuracy, as well as perform quote analyses on behalf of the team
Actively manage all assigned accounts, and represent HUB and our values in all company and/or carrier events and presentations.
Handle confidential information and data – adhering to HUB’s privacy and confidentiality agreement
Develop innovative methods to improve processes via automation and technology platforms
Create user friendly templates and utilize available software to simplify processes for the team


Skills, Characteristics & Qualifications


Intermediate proficiency in Microsoft Word, PowerPoint, and Outlook, with advanced expertise in Microsoft Excel, including complex spreadsheets, data tables, and formula development
Proven ability to collaborate effectively in cross-functional team environments
Strong analytical and problem-solving capabilities, with demonstrated experience in diagnosing issues and developing innovative solutions
Proven ability to interpret and analyze numerical datasets to identify trends and resolve discrepancies
Excellent organizational skills with the capacity to manage competing priorities and deliver results within time-sensitive deadlines


Asset Experience To Have


Bachelor’s degree or equivalent in Business Administration or Computer Science
Experience working with group benefits or insurance
Completion of the Life License Qualification Program (LLQP).


Where This Can Take You

We invest in our employees! We offer hands-on coaching and mentorship, alongside extensive training to ensure you succeed, today and tomorrow. Your journey with HUB can open doors to a diverse, rewarding career within the insurance industry and beyond!

What We Offer


Competitive compensation
Company matching RRSP contributions
Tuition financing and career-related training and development
Customizable flexible benefits options for you and your family
Mental and physical wellness initiatives
A positive, collaborative and team-oriented environment
Ongoing personal and career development


If this sounds like a place you want to be or want to talk to us about the role and opportunities, contact us/apply now.

The expected salary range for this position is $55,000 to $70,000 and will be impacted by factors such as the successful candidate’s skills, experience and working location, as well as the specific position’s business line, scope and level. HUB International is proud to offer comprehensive benefit and total compensation packages which could include extended health benefits, disability insurance, RRSP matching, paid-time-off benefits, and eligible bonuses, and commissions for some positions. We invest in our employees! Your journey with HUB can open doors to a diverse, rewarding career within the insurance industry and beyond!

Our Culture Starts with You


We are looking for SELF-STARTERS with an ENTREPRENEURIAL mindset who will take OWNERSHIP of their business
We want you to be INNOVATIVE and open to sharing your ideas
You work with URGENCY while providing a high level of PROFESSIONAL SERVICE towards our customers, communities, & colleagues.
You are a TEAM PLAYER who positively impact those around them; We MOTIVATE each other to GROW TOGETHER
You work with a high degree of INTEGRITY and ACCOUNTABILITY


If this sounds like a place you want to be or want to talk to us about the role and opportunities, contact us or apply now. To find associated positions at our HUB simply search myHUBbc as a keyword in the search bar on our careers page!

Department Account Management & Service

Required Experience: 1-2 years of relevant experience

Required Travel: No Travel Required

We endeavor to make this website accessible to any and all users. If you would like to contact us regarding the accessibility of our website or need assistance completing the application process, please contact the recruiting team HUBRecruiting@hubinternational.com. This contact information is for accommodation requests only; do not use this contact information to inquire about the status of applications.

Title: Client Reporting Analyst
Description:
Are you interested in joining one of Canada’s top performing asset managers?  Connor, Clark & Lunn Financial Group (“CC&LFG”) is looking for a Client Reporting Analyst for an existing vacancy in our Institutional Client Reporting team. You will join our dedicated Client Reporting team to provide reporting operations expertise to CC&LFG’s institutional investment affiliates and interact with all levels of the organization, including daily contact with our affiliates’ Client Relationship Managers. If you have a focus for producing high quality work, are an expert multi-tasker, have a knack for using new technology and work well under pressure, this role could be a great fit for you. The Client Reporting Team places a high value on our team spirit and creating a culture where each of us has an opportunity to succeed. We are a solution-oriented and collaborative team, seeking like-minded individuals who thrive in a challenging and dynamic environment. Our offices currently operate on a hybrid model with a combination of in-office and remote working days throughout the week.

What You Will Do


Setup and maintenance of client accounts and client data
Ensure that a multitude of reports are accurately set up, maintained, produced and distributed
Communicate with various Client Relationship Managers and other key personnel across CC&L to support and trouble shoot the entire reporting production process; liaise with the internal development team and Information Systems support team to provide fixes and solutions
Building and maintaining report layouts using a mix of in-house tools and leading third party technology to ensure highly effective reporting operations
Work closely with the team’s dedicated development team members on using a third party IDE to enhance and develop report layouts and assign logical rules to support the automated report production as well as, translating business logic to source code written in a proprietary language
Analyze, maintain and improve the reporting process to maximize efficiency and minimize risk
Investigate and advise on best reporting practice


What You Bring


You have an interest in learning new technologies; developing and implementing new business processes. \
You have a positive attitude and collaborative spirit. Current and former colleagues would say that you’re an easy person to work with a one-team mindset
You are able to manage competing tasks and can successfully navigate working with multiple lines of business to implement a large number of reports.
Demonstrated ability to evaluate business problems and effectively apply solutions with subject matter expertise.
You have a proven ability to communicate well with internal stakeholders. You are clear on what you need and you clarify what others need; you manage expectations and are professional and positive all at the same time.
Advanced working knowledge of Microsoft Excel, Word and PowerPoint (e.g. ability to use and understand formulas, charting tools, styles etc.). Knowledge of Simcorp’s Coric Client Communication solution is an asset.
You have an undergraduate degree coupled with 2-3 years in a related role or similar transferrable experience.


The salary range for this position is $60,000 - $80,000.  The salary range provided reflects the base salary range for this position as required by legislation.  In addition, there is an annual performance bonus which contributes to the total compensation of this position. Further questions may be directed to the HR team during the interview process.

For a closer look at how you can build your career with us, we invite you to explore cclgroup.com. 

CC&L Financial Group is committed to creating a diverse and inclusive environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to gender, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation or expression, disability, or age. 

Your application will be reviewed by a member of the hiring team - AI is not used in the screening, assessment or selection of applications at this time.

Title: Manager, Data & Analytics Business Partner
Description:
We’re searching for a Manager, Data & Analytics Business Partner. Are you searching for new possibilities?

Westland Insurance Group Ltd is growing and has an exciting opportunity for a Manager, Data & Analytics Business Partner to join our growing team. This hybrid role is based out of our Head Office in Surrey, BC.

Expected Compensation: The expected hiring range for this role is $106,000- $138,000 with a 35-hour work week. Your rate of pay will be determined based on your individual qualifications and experience, while considering internal equity. 

Westland also offers a competitive annual short-term incentive program that enables employees to share in the rewards of the business and emphasizes the measures that are key to our success.

Westland is committed to continuous learning where employees can grow their potential and write the stories of their careers. Financial assistance is available for approved work-related training/development courses leading to certification, and programs that offer professional development, including seminars and conferences.

Discover What’s Possible, With Westland.

The Westland story is all about growth, and that means plenty of possibility for everyone on our team. Every day, Westland proves that building a great business means taking care of clients, communities, and each other with equal commitment. As we continue to open new doors, we're inviting amazing people like you to join us.

As a Manager, Data & Analytics Business Partner, you’ll act as a strategic analytics leader partnering with senior business leaders to deliver insights that shape business decisions and drive measurable impact for Westland

Interested in learning more?

Why You'll Love Westland


An open, flexible and welcoming workplace
Plenty of opportunities to grow and learn
Autonomy to drive your own success
Be part of our inclusive culture, alongside an extraordinarily talented community of people with a wide variety of backgrounds
A total rewards program that takes care of your financial, physical, and mental health
Flexible paid Values Days to celebrate days important to you
Paid Volunteer Days for employees as part of Amplifying Communities, a program to support causes that matter most to our employees and clients


Why We’ll Love You


You are a strong analytics leader who thrives on partnering with business leaders to drive decision-making
You bring a consultative mindset and enjoy translating business questions into actionable data insights
You have a strong foundation in data analytics, business intelligence, or a related field, supported by a relevant degree (e.g., Statistics, Computer Science, Data Science, Economics, Mathematics)
You have at least 7 years of experience in data analytics or a related discipline, with demonstrated business impact
You are experienced in working directly with senior stakeholders and building trusted partnerships
You are comfortable working with tools and programming languages such as SQL, Python, or R
You have experience with modern data platforms such as Databricks, Snowflake, or Microsoft Fabric
You are a strong communicator who can translate complex analytics into meaningful insights for non-technical audiences


Once Here, You'll


Act as the primary data and analytics partner for a business unit, building strong relationships with leadership and embedding data into decision-making
Design and deliver high-impact analytics and insights that drive measurable business outcomes such as growth, retention, and efficiency
Lead strategic data initiatives and develop prototypes that demonstrate business value and can scale into enterprise solutions
Improve data literacy and enable self-service analytics by promoting trusted data, tools, and best practices
Collaborate with Data Office teams to align analytics solutions with enterprise data platforms and capabilities
Identify opportunities to leverage data, analytics, and AI/ML to solve business challenges
Contribute to building and scaling the Data & Analytics Business Partner function, with the opportunity to grow and lead a team of analysts over time


Want to get to know each other better? Send your resume our way.

You belong here

We strive to be much more than a great place to work. Here, you join a collective of incredibly vibrant, purpose driven, and open-minded people. Every day, Westland proves that building a great business means taking care of communities, clients, and each other with equal commitment. You will be supported and respected for who you are and for the voice you add to the conversation.

At Westland, you will feel the power of community. Westland Insurance Ltd. is an inclusive organization that values diversity in its workforce. We listen, champion equity and diversity, and create safe welcoming spaces where everyone has a voice and is free to be themselves. Westland Insurance Ltd. encourages applications from all qualified individuals and will accommodate applicants' disability-related needs, up to the point of undue hardship, throughout all stages of the recruitment and selection process.

If you require a disability-related accommodation in order to participate in the recruitment process, please contact the recruitment team by email at careers@westlandinsurance.ca

Title: Actuarial Analyst II
Description:
Why BCAA?

Feel proud of what you do and help shape the future of BC at a BC Top Employer. We’re driven by purpose – delivering peace of mind to one in three BC households with insurance, roadside assistance, auto repair, Evo Car Share and Evolve E-Bike Share. With a long history of keeping roads safe and giving back in ways that build stronger communities, we offer a wide variety of meaningful, rewarding careers that move BC forward.

We are currently hiring an Actuarial Analyst (Level I OR II) based in our Head Office in Burnaby.

Please note that this is a hybrid role based out of our Burnaby, BC office. Applicants who are not located within commuting distance of our Head Office or are not looking to relocate to the Greater Vancouver/Lower Mainland/Fraser Valley region will not be considered.

As an Actuarial Analyst (Level I OR II), you’ll play a key role in supporting pricing sophistication, underwriting capability, and product development for BCAA’s Property & Casualty insurance products. This role blends actuarial analysis with hands-on problem solving to deliver insights that enhance rating methodologies, support P&C portfolio performance, and strengthen decision-making across our insurance business.

This is an excellent opportunity for someone progressing through CAS actuarial exams who wants to grow in a collaborative, corporate environment. You’ll work closely with senior actuarial team members, contribute to research and predictive modeling projects, and gain valuable exposure to property and casualty insurance analysis, product design, and risk assessment.

Who you are:


You are analytical and detail-oriented, with a strong foundation in actuarial methods and insurance concepts.
You thrive on problem-solving and enjoy using data to uncover insights and develop practical solutions.
You are highly organized, capable of managing competing priorities with accuracy and focus.
You work well independently but also collaborate effectively in a team environment.
You’re motivated to continue writing CAS actuarial exams and building your actuarial career.
You communicate technical findings clearly, tailoring your message to both technical and non-technical audiences.



What you’ll do:


Contribute to the development of rating models and actuarial analyses to support product pricing and underwriting strategies.
Assist in preparing and reviewing actuarial studies, including loss ratio analysis, trend analysis, reinsurance governance, etc.
Work with senior team members to scope projects and establish appropriate actuarial or statistical approaches.
Extract, clean, and analyze internal and external data sources to build accurate actuarial datasets.
Document methodologies and summarize results in a clear, concise manner for senior actuaries and leadership.
Contribute to research projects to deepen understanding of insurance portfolio performance and emerging risks.
Continuously build actuarial knowledge while proactively sharing insights with team members.



What you bring:


Bachelor’s degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Actuarial Science, or a related quantitative field.
Minimum 2 years of actuarial or analytical experience, ideally within insurance. Consideration will also be given to candidates with relevant project or internship experience.
Completion of at least 2 CAS actuarial exams, with continued progress towards an Associate designation.
Knowledge of actuarial concepts including pricing, reserving, and risk analysis.
Strong skills in statistical tools/languages such as R or Python.
Experience working with large datasets and databases, and the ability to query, merge, and prepare data this data.
Solid written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to explain technical topics to non-technical audiences.
A proactive approach to problem solving, with strong organizational skills and attention to detail.
Experience with dashboarding/visualization tools such as PowerBI or Tableau.
Exposure to machine learning algorithms, clustering, or advanced predictive modeling.
Familiarity with insurance product development or regulatory filings.
Understanding of GIS or spatial analytics tools.
Bonus Skills & Experience:
Experience with predictive modeling or machine learning techniques applied to P&C insurance.
Familiarity with regulatory requirements impacting property and casualty insurance.
Knowledge of claims analytics, loss development, and reserving methodologies.
Exposure to reinsurance structures and their impact on portfolio management.



The salary range for this position is $75,363.00 to $103,624.44 and is determined via job-related knowledge, skills, experience, certifications, market demand and internal equity. In addition to base salary and BCAA benefits, this position is eligible for a performance bonus target of 9%, with the opportunity to earn up to 15%.

What we offer:


The chance to make a difference every day in the lives of British Columbians, BCAA Members and our communities.
Career and personal development to help you grow and reach your goals.
Incentives that recognize team and individual sales and performance.
Flexible benefits to support your health and lifestyle, and wellness dollars* to keep your body, mind and soul thriving.
A competitive rewards package including salary performance bonus programs, pension plan*, and more to help build your personal wealth.
Paid time off including vacation, and days for wellness, volunteering, and cultural, spiritual or religious connection.
BCAA membership, with valuable perks and Member savings on every day and special spending, from gas and coffee to clothes, sports and event tickets, and hotels.
Meaningful, rewarding work for a BC Top Employer known for putting people first and constantly building a better workplace for all our team members.
for permanent employees



We’re always looking for amazing people like you. Apply today!

BCAA is an Equal Opportunity Employer, committed to providing accessible, inclusive employment. We have a long-standing partnership with the Presidents Group which strives to increase employment opportunities for people living with disabilities, and champion accessible employment in BC. If you need personalized support through application and recruitment, please contact HRAccommodations@bcaa.com.

  • You thrive on problem-solving and enjoy using data to uncover insights and develop practical solutions.
  • You are highly organized, capable of managing competing priorities with accuracy and focus.
  • You work well independently but also collaborate effectively in a team environment.
  • You’re motivated to continue writing CAS actuarial exams and building your actuarial career.
  • You communicate technical findings clearly, tailoring your message to both technical and non-technical audiences.


What you’ll do:

  • Contribute to the development of rating models and actuarial analyses to support product pricing and underwriting strategies.
  • Assist in preparing and reviewing actuarial studies, including loss ratio analysis, trend analysis, reinsurance governance, etc.
  • Work with senior team members to scope projects and establish appropriate actuarial or statistical approaches.
  • Extract, clean, and analyze internal and external data sources to build accurate actuarial datasets.
  • Document methodologies and summarize results in a clear, concise manner for senior actuaries and leadership.
  • Contribute to research projects to deepen understanding of insurance portfolio performance and emerging risks.
  • Continuously build actuarial knowledge while proactively sharing insights with team members.


What you bring:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Actuarial Science, or a related quantitative field.
  • Minimum 2 years of actuarial or analytical experience, ideally within insurance. Consideration will also be given to candidates with relevant project or internship experience.
  • Completion of at least 2 CAS actuarial exams, with continued progress towards an Associate designation.
  • Knowledge of actuarial concepts including pricing, reserving, and risk analysis.
  • Strong skills in statistical tools/languages such as R or Python.
  • Experience working with large datasets and databases, and the ability to query, merge, and prepare data this data.
  • Solid written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to explain technical topics to non-technical audiences.
  • A proactive approach to problem solving, with strong organizational skills and attention to detail.
  • Experience with dashboarding/visualization tools such as PowerBI or Tableau.
  • Exposure to machine learning algorithms, clustering, or advanced predictive modeling.
  • Familiarity with insurance product development or regulatory filings.
  • Understanding of GIS or spatial analytics tools.
  • Bonus Skills & Experience:
  • Experience with predictive modeling or machine learning techniques applied to P&C insurance.
  • Familiarity with regulatory requirements impacting property and casualty insurance.
  • Knowledge of claims analytics, loss development, and reserving methodologies.
  • Exposure to reinsurance structures and their impact on portfolio management.


The salary range for this position is $75,363.00 to $103,624.44 and is determined via job-related knowledge, skills, experience, certifications, market demand and internal equity. In addition to base salary and BCAA benefits, this position is eligible for a performance bonus target of 9%, with the opportunity to earn up to 15%.

What we offer:

  • The chance to make a difference every day in the lives of British Columbians, BCAA Members and our communities.
  • Career and personal development to help you grow and reach your goals.
  • Incentives that recognize team and individual sales and performance.
  • Flexible benefits to support your health and lifestyle, and wellness dollars* to keep your body, mind and soul thriving.
  • A competitive rewards package including salary performance bonus programs, pension plan*, and more to help build your personal wealth.
  • Paid time off including vacation, and days for wellness, volunteering, and cultural, spiritual or religious connection.
  • BCAA membership, with valuable perks and Member savings on every day and special spending, from gas and coffee to clothes, sports and event tickets, and hotels.
  • Meaningful, rewarding work for a BC Top Employer known for putting people first and constantly building a better workplace for all our team members.
  • for permanent employees


We’re always looking for amazing people like you. Apply today!

BCAA is an Equal Opportunity Employer, committed to providing accessible, inclusive employment. We have a long-standing partnership with the Presidents Group which strives to increase employment opportunities for people living with disabilities, and champion accessible employment in BC. If you need personalized support through application and recruitment, please contact HRAccommodations@bcaa.com.
